userImpact: Users can now generate and manage synthetic Expel tabular test datasets locally or in S3 for testing, dashboarding, and validation purposes.
author: alex380125

title: chore: make cpu and memory configurable as parameters
summary: Made CPU and memory allocations configurable as CloudFormation parameters across ECS task definitions, enabling operators to optimize resource consumption without modifying stack templates. Reduced frontend CPU and memory reservations after discovering low utilization, improving cost efficiency while maintaining performance.
userImpact: Operators can now customize CPU and memory allocations per deployment, and frontend resource consumption has been optimized based on actual usage patterns.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Feat redis infra
summary: Introduced Redis Stack infrastructure to support caching in the application plane, including serverless cache support with usage limits and subnet group configuration. Redis credentials are securely managed through AWS Secrets Manager, with proper username validation and ECPU balance management.
userImpact: Users can now leverage Redis caching infrastructure for improved application performance with configurable cache usage limits and automatic failover capabilities through the Redis Stack.
author: jdb-arthur

title: NO-REF: Change multiprocessing method from fork to spawn
summary: Changed the multiprocessing start method from `fork` to `spawn` to improve CI test stability and eliminate deprecation warnings that occur when forking multithreaded processes like FastAPI/uvicorn. While `spawn` introduces a ~2 minute performance regression in the full test suite, it prevents potential deadlocks and resource-sharing issues that cause test flakiness, yielding a net stability gain.
userImpact: CI tests are now more stable and no longer emit deprecation warnings when using multiprocessing with multithreaded FastAPI/uvicorn servers.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Whitespace changes and python minor version CI change
summary: Updated CI base image to a higher Python minor version to resolve a bug in the current version. Whitespace changes made to trigger CI builds.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update dependency markupsafe to v3
summary: Updated MarkupSafe dependency from v2.1.5 to v3.0.0, a major version bump that adds Python 3.13 support (including experimental free-threaded build) and drops support for Python 3.7 and 3.8. The upgrade includes performance improvements (40% faster escaping for plain strings) and API refinements to Markup method signatures.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a dependency update that improves performance and modernizes the underlying library.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated Faker from ~=30.1.0 to ~=30.3.0 and Black from ~=24.8.0 to ~=24.10.0 to pull in the latest non-major dependency versions. These updates include performance improvements, bug fixes, and compatibility enhancements in both tools.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Development dependencies updated to latest compatible versions.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1578
summary: Fixed CI pipeline behavior so the `Update-Frontend-Version` step only runs on the main branch, preventing unintended MR merges when new FE images are detected on feature branches. This ensures the pipeline can update the FE image independently without automatically merging unrelated MRs.
userImpact: CI pipelines on feature branches will no longer trigger automatic MR merges when frontend versions are updated.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1578 fix auto merging mrs
summary: Fixed an issue preventing automatic merging of merge requests. A configuration change was made to the GitLab CI pipeline to resolve the auto-merge functionality.
userImpact: Merge requests configured for automatic merging now complete successfully without manual intervention.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1379 - Add exclude query param to get_jobs endpoint, remove unused job type, lower retry count for scheduled jobs
summary: Added `exclude_kinds` query parameter to the `get_jobs` endpoint, allowing users to filter out jobs of specific types. The parameter takes precedence over `kinds`, so excluding a job type will remove it from results even if it's also in the kinds filter. Also fixed a PostgreSQL compatibility issue where the retry count for scheduled jobs exceeded the 32-bit integer limit, and removed an unused duplicate job type for data retrieval.
userImpact: Users can now exclude specific job types from query results using the `exclude_kinds` parameter, and scheduled jobs will no longer cause 500 errors in PostgreSQL due to integer overflow.
author: peter496

title: UP-1410: log chart bugfix
summary: Fixed a log chart configuration issue in the CloudFormation monitoring stack for ECS. This corrects a problem introduced in a previous merge that was unintentionally applied.
userImpact: Log chart monitoring on ECS now displays correctly without the erroneous configuration.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1410: add upsolve monitoring support
summary: Added Upsolve service monitoring support to CloudFormation templates for the monitoring dashboard and alarms. Updated CloudWatch charts and rate calculations to include Upsolve metrics alongside other services, leveraging dynamic dashboard capabilities and AWS best practices for ECS Container Insights.
userImpact: Users can now monitor Upsolve service metrics including task counts and performance rates in the centralized monitoring dashboard alongside other services.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1573 | Allow for empty webhook header dict update
summary: Previously, webhook header updates with an empty dictionary were being omitted during the update process. This fix ensures that webhooks can now be properly updated with an empty headers dict, allowing users to clear webhook headers when needed.
userImpact: Users can now successfully clear webhook headers by updating a webhook with an empty headers dictionary.
author: ooizig

title: chore(deps): update dependency redis to v5.1.1
summary: Updates redis-py dependency from v5.1.0 to v5.1.1. This patch release includes bug fixes for Redis Set command return types, Hiredis availability handling, and async pipeline cluster command support.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a dependency maintenance update that improves underlying library stability.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1491 metrics and query docs
summary: Added comprehensive external-facing product documentation for Arthur's metrics system and query language. The documentation includes detailed guidance on writing queries for dashboards and alert rules, with practical examples and screenshots showing the alert rule creation workflow. This provides users with self-service resources to understand metrics and construct custom queries.
userImpact: Users can now reference official documentation to learn how to write and execute queries for dashboards and create alert rules, with clear examples and visual guidance throughout the process.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1570 fix toxicity distribution dashboard
summary: The toxicity distribution dashboard was incorrectly calculating distributions from sketch medians rather than merged sketches, resulting in vastly undercounted values (500 vs. 43.5k inferences). Fixed by updating the query to use `kll_float_sketch_merge()` and `kll_float_sketch_get_pmf()` to properly aggregate all values across the time interval.
userImpact: Users will now see accurate toxicity distribution counts on the dashboard that correctly reflect the total number of inferences in the selected interval.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1490 webhooks product guide
summary: Created a comprehensive product guide for Arthur Webhooks with usage examples, screenshots, and integration tutorials for Slack and Jira. The guide demonstrates how to set up and configure webhooks with step-by-step visual instructions.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1231: point app plane to on-prem upsolve
summary: Configured the app plane to point to on-premises Upsolve instead of a cloud-hosted instance. This includes updates to bootstrap configuration, settings, and CloudFormation templates to properly establish connectivity between the app plane and on-prem Upsolve infrastructure.
userImpact: Deployments can now successfully communicate with and bootstrap the on-premises Upsolve service through the app plane.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update dependency authzed to v1
summary: Updated the authzed dependency from v0.19.0 to v1.0.0. According to the upstream release, this major version bump contains no breaking changes and is safe to adopt.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest authzed library features and improvements while maintaining compatibility with existing code.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update dependency cfn-lint to ~=1.16.0
summary: Updated cfn-lint dependency from ~=1.15.0 to ~=1.16.0. This is a routine dependency update to incorporate upstream improvements and bug fixes from the CloudFormation linting tool.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-846 - Change schedule jobs job to retry (nearly) infinite times
summary: Increased the maximum retry attempts for schedule jobs to nearly infinite (sys.maxsize) instead of a finite limit. This prevents schedule jobs from being exhausted due to transient service issues, ensuring scheduled tasks continue to run reliably even after extended outages.
userImpact: Schedule jobs are now resilient to transient failures and will no longer be permanently exhausted by temporary service disruptions.
author: peter496

title: UP-846 - Fix import, reduce test parallelism to reduce flakiness
summary: Fixed an incorrect import statement in the aggregation matcher module. Additionally reduced test parallelism in the CI configuration to improve test reliability and reduce flakiness.
userImpact: Tests now run with reduced parallelism, improving reliability and reducing intermittent test failures.
author: peter496

title: chore: enable upsolve ecs healthcheck & docker-compose fix
summary: Added curl to the upsolve Docker image to re-enable ECS healthcheck functionality that was previously disabled. The image now includes proper cache cleanup during installation to minimize image size, and docker-compose configuration has been updated to reflect the healthcheck re-enablement.
userImpact: The upsolve container now properly reports health status to ECS, ensuring deployment reliability and visibility into container state.
author: alex380125

title: chore: upsolve exec and disable ecs healthchecks
summary: Disabled ECS health checks that were causing service instability, while keeping ALB health checks intact. Enabled ECS Exec to allow direct container access for debugging and troubleshooting.
userImpact: The upsolve service will remain stable and operational, and operators can now connect directly to the container via ECS Exec for debugging purposes.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1468 | Add default order by id to queries
summary: This MR adds a secondary sort by ID to alert queries to ensure consistent and deterministic pagination when primary sort fields are non-unique. Without a secondary sort, result ordering becomes ambiguous for rows with identical primary sort values, leading to inconsistent pagination behavior. The change adds a default order_by id clause to the alerts repository query logic.
userImpact: Users will now receive consistent, predictable pagination results when querying alerts with non-unique sort fields, eliminating ordering ambiguity.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1528 add default limit and page size for raw data retrieval job
summary: Raw data retrieval jobs previously fetched all available data for the specified time range without limits, which could result in hundreds or thousands of inferences being loaded unnecessarily. This change adds a default limit parameter to restrict data retrieval to a reasonable subset unless explicitly overridden by the user, improving performance and reducing unnecessary data loading.
userImpact: Raw data retrieval jobs now default to fetching a limited subset of data instead of loading all available inferences, reducing memory usage and improving performance while allowing users to request more data when needed.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency faker to ~=30.1.0
summary: Updated the Faker dependency from ~=30.0.0 to ~=30.1.0. This minor version update includes improvements to the en_IN address provider with PIN code range and union territories support.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1521 fix data retrieval job status on failure
summary: Fixed exception handling in the raw data retrieval job so it correctly marks the job as failed when data upload to the platform fails. Previously, upload failures were not properly caught, leaving the job in an ambiguous state despite unsuccessful completion.
userImpact: Users will now see accurate job status when data retrieval uploads fail, enabling them to identify and address upload errors instead of mistaking failed jobs for successful ones.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated redis-py to 5.1.0, which introduces client-side caching support for improved performance, and uvicorn to 0.31.0 for the latest enhancements. This MR applies routine non-major dependency updates to keep the system current with upstream improvements and bug fixes.
userImpact: Users can now leverage client-side caching in redis-py 5.1.0 for faster cache access, and benefit from the latest uvicorn performance and stability improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1519 reuse redis client across threads
summary: The Redis client is now reused across threads instead of creating separate instances for each thread. This reduces resource overhead and improves performance when multiple threads access Redis.
userImpact: Applications using Redis now have more efficient memory usage and better performance in multi-threaded environments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1519 fix redis connection 2
summary: Fixed Redis connection configuration issue that was causing ConnectionError when connecting to the Redis cache endpoint. Updated settings and dependency configuration to properly resolve the serverless cache connection parameters.
userImpact: Users can now successfully connect to the Redis cache without encountering "No such file or directory" errors during cache operations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1519 fix redis connections
summary: Fixed SSL Redis connections by updating configuration and dependency initialization. This addresses an issue where secure Redis connections were not being properly established in the app plane.
userImpact: Applications can now establish secure SSL connections to Redis without connection failures.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1516 fix shield connector
summary: Fixed a pagination bug in the shield connector that prevented it from correctly iterating over all available inferences when the single `page` filter was not set. The connector now properly pages through all results regardless of filter configuration, with comprehensive test coverage added to prevent regression.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve all inferences from the shield connector without needing to manually set a `page` filter, ensuring complete data retrieval.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated authzed from 0.18.2 to 0.19.0 and authzed/spicedb from v1.36.2 to v1.37.0 to keep dependencies current with minor version releases. This routine dependency maintenance ensures compatibility with the latest non-breaking updates from upstream projects.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Roll back user creation on failure
summary: Fixed a critical bug where user creation would succeed but leave the system in a malformed state if the Keycloak user type update failed. The user would be created in the database but lack the required role binding, causing bootstrap to falsely report success on retry. Now user creation is rolled back if the Keycloak update fails, ensuring consistency.
userImpact: Users will no longer be left in a malformed state with missing role bindings when Keycloak user type updates fail during initial setup.
author: peter496

title: chore: remove old stacks from a bad rebase or something
summary: Removed 8 CloudFormation stack files that were inadvertently committed during a rebase. These files were not meant to be in the repository and have been cleaned up.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: grafana target group
summary: Fixed an incorrect target group configuration in the Grafana ECS CloudFormation stack. This ensures Grafana traffic is properly routed to the correct target group.
userImpact: Grafana dashboard access is now properly routed through the correct target group in the AWS load balancer configuration.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: new_stacks
summary: Introduces a new baseline set of CloudFormation stacks for Arthur Scope infrastructure deployment. The stacks cover core infrastructure (VPC, IAM, ECS, load balancers), database components (RDS, TimescaleDB), and scope application services (auth, SpiceDB, app plane, data plane, frontend, Grafana), organized into modular stack directories for flexible deployment.
userImpact: Users can now deploy Arthur Scope infrastructure using a comprehensive set of pre-defined CloudFormation stacks that handle networking, security, database, and application components.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Update dependency numpy to v2
summary: Updated numpy dependency from v1 to v2 across all Dockerfiles and requirements files. This major version upgrade brings improved performance, updated APIs, and access to the latest NumPy features and bug fixes through v2.1.1.
userImpact: Users benefit from NumPy 2.x improvements including enhanced performance and bug fixes, though projects may need to review code for compatibility with NumPy 2.x API changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency Faker to v30
summary: Faker dependency updated from v29.0.0 to v30.0.0. This major release includes improvements to slug generation, ensuring slugs are always generated with ASCII characters for better consistency and compatibility.
userImpact: Users will benefit from more reliable slug generation with guaranteed ASCII character output when using Faker's slug generation methods.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency datasketches to ~=5.1.0
summary: Updated the datasketches dependency from ~=5.0.2 to ~=5.1.0 across app_plane and data_plane requirements. This minor version update brings the latest improvements and bug fixes from the Apache DataSketches library.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest DataSketches features and fixes available in version 5.1.0.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1495 add better URL parsing to API client
summary: The API client now properly handles URLs configured with trailing slashes by extracting only the scheme and host components. Previously, such configurations would cause failures, limiting the flexibility of client setup.
userImpact: Users can now configure the API client with URLs that include trailing slashes without encountering errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-846 - Refactor how dataset schemas are defined, support column aliasing
summary: Redesigned dataset schema model to support column aliasing and resolve join conflicts. Previously schemas were serialized data without type safety; now they use a rigid `DatasetSchema` structure with globally unique column IDs, source names, and an alias mask that maps columns to friendly names. This enables conflict detection across joined tables, better tracking without relying on string paths, and provides a proper data model for UI schema editing.
userImpact: Users can now set up column aliases to resolve naming conflicts when joining datasets, and the system will detect conflicts upfront rather than producing ambiguous results.
author: peter496

title: UP-394 proof read API docs
summary: Fixed typos and corrected HTTP response codes across all API documentation endpoints. This comprehensive proofread improves accuracy and clarity of the API reference for developers integrating with the platform.
userImpact: Developers now have accurate API documentation with correct response codes and fixed typos across all endpoints.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-394 fix API docs SSO
summary: Fixed API docs Authorize button redirect URL to match the `/api` load balancer prefix, preventing requests from incorrectly forwarding to the UI and causing 404 errors. Updated Keycloak OAuth2 client configuration to allow the corrected redirect URL for API documentation authentication.
userImpact: Users can now successfully authenticate via the Authorize button in API docs without encountering redirect errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Add rate/count of failures for toxicity
summary: Added rate and count metrics for toxicity failures to the MVP Shield dashboard. This provides better visibility into failure patterns and trends for the toxicity service, with consistent data point intervals across related bar charts.
userImpact: Users can now monitor the rate and count of toxicity failures on the dashboard with improved visualization consistency.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-509: enable container insights
summary: Enabled Container Insights for the ECS cluster to provide detailed monitoring metrics and performance insights. This enables the foundation for building out CloudWatch dashboards with container-level visibility and diagnostics.
userImpact: Users can now monitor ECS container performance metrics and logs through CloudWatch Container Insights, enabling better visibility into application health and resource utilization.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1473 database bootstrap commands
summary: Adds automated database bootstrap commands that create the `upsolve` and `spicedb` databases on startup, with idempotent logic to safely skip creation if databases already exist. Also upgrades upsolve to 0.0.7 for x86 architecture support and removes the hard-coded Grafana container name from the docker-compose stack to prevent naming conflicts.
userImpact: Database initialization is now automated as part of the bootstrap process, eliminating the need for manual database creation steps and supporting both ARM and x86 architectures.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1230-bugfix: Gate upsolve bootstrap step behind flag
summary: The upsolve bootstrap step in the app plane is now gated behind a feature flag (ARTHUR_UPSOLVE_BOOTSTRAP_ENABLED), allowing operators to control whether the database patching step runs during initialization. This prevents unintended bootstrap behavior when the flag is disabled.
userImpact: Operators can now enable or disable the upsolve database bootstrap step via an environment variable, giving them control over when database patches are applied during app initialization.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies to their latest minor versions: authzed/spicedb (v1.35.3 → v1.36.2) and cfn-lint (~=1.14.0 → ~=1.15.0). These updates bring in bug fixes and incremental improvements from upstream projects.
userImpact: Users benefit from upstream bug fixes and improvements in SpiceDB and CloudFormation linting without breaking changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Draft: UP-1231: CFT for upsolve
summary: CloudFormation templates (CFT) have been added to enable deployment of the Upsolve service on ECS within the Arthur Scope infrastructure. This includes new CloudFormation templates for Upsolve ECS deployment, load balancer configuration, security groups, and secrets management, along with build and deployment scripts to containerize and push the Upsolve image to ECR.
userImpact: Operators can now deploy Upsolve as an ECS service using CloudFormation templates, enabling consistent infrastructure-as-code deployment across environments.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] fix app start up
summary: App was failing to start due to an incorrect import path in permissions_router.py that referenced a non-existent 'scope' module. The import statement has been corrected to resolve the ModuleNotFoundError and allow the application to initialize successfully.
userImpact: The application now starts up without ModuleNotFoundError failures.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1471: Fix timestamp typo
summary: Fixed a typo in the timestamp field within the API data retrieval model. The typo was corrected across the model definition, router, and corresponding unit tests to ensure consistency.
userImpact: API responses for data retrieval now use the correct timestamp field name.
author: peter496

title: UP-1471 - Include status and nulled body on GET retrieved data if not loaded.
summary: GET data retrieval endpoints now include a `status` field and return a nulled body when data has not yet been loaded. This simplifies state management by making the response model clearly indicate whether data is available or still processing, removing the need for separate logic to interpret the response state.
userImpact: Users can now reliably check the `status` field when polling data retrieval endpoints to determine if data is ready, with the body explicitly null when data is not yet available or has expired.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Add flags to prevent recursive API models from getting split into input / output
summary: Added flags to the server configuration to prevent FastAPI from automatically splitting recursive API models into separate Input/Output variants. This addresses a complex OpenAPI documentation generation issue where certain model configurations (nullability, defaults, response_model usage) triggered unwanted model duplication. The workaround flags allow teams to control this behavior without waiting for upstream FastAPI resolution.
userImpact: Users can now prevent API models from being unnecessarily split into Input/Output variants by configuring the new server flags, simplifying OpenAPI schema generation for recursive models.
author: peter496

title: UP-897 | Add permissions check endpoint
summary: This MR introduces a new permissions check endpoint that allows clients to verify whether specific permissions are granted for a given resource. The endpoint supports bulk permission checking in a single call to SpiceDB, improving efficiency over N individual round trips. An upper limit was added to prevent excessively large permission check requests.
userImpact: Users can now verify multiple permissions for a resource in a single API call via the new /api/v1/permissions/check endpoint.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1314 | Add project level jobs filtering based on model_id
summary: Added project-level job filtering based on the model_id field, enabling developers to filter jobs that have a model_id defined. The implementation refactors job spec handling to dynamically extract job spec subclasses and filter based on the presence of the model_id field, eliminating the need to manually register new model-specific job types.
userImpact: Users can now filter jobs at the project level by model_id, allowing more granular control over job visibility and organization within projects.
author: ooizig

title: Update dependency anyio to ~=4.6.0
summary: Updated anyio dependency from ~=4.5.0 to ~=4.6.0. This minor release fixes several asyncio issues including 100% CPU usage during task group exit, cancel scope propagation bugs, and inconsistent task uncancellation behavior.
userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes in asyncio task group handling and cancel scope behavior, resolving CPU usage spikes and cancellation propagation inconsistencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1212 UP-1213 | FetchData job execution
summary: Implements DataFetch job execution capabilities (UP-1212, UP-1213), enabling users to submit and monitor data retrieval operations as background jobs. The implementation includes job specification models, execution operators, API endpoints, database migrations, and authorization controls via SpiceDB schema updates to ensure data retrievals are scoped to individual models.
userImpact: Users can now submit data fetch operations as jobs and track their execution status and results through the API.
author: ooizig

title: [no-ref] add call flow diagram for raw data retrieval job
summary: Added a call flow diagram to the Raw Data Retrieval technical documentation to explain how the raw data retrieval job works. This improves developer understanding of the internal data retrieval flow.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 fix CFT secret IAM access
summary: ECS tasks were unable to retrieve secrets from AWS Secrets Manager due to missing IAM permissions on the task execution role. Added the necessary secretsmanager:GetSecretValue permission to the task execution role policy in the CloudFormation template.
userImpact: Tasks can now successfully pull secrets from AWS Secrets Manager without encountering AccessDeniedException errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 move root user credentials to a secret
summary: Fixed failed deployments caused by invalid Systems Manager parameter references for root user credentials. Root user credentials are now properly stored in AWS Secrets Manager with correct ARN references in ECS task definitions and configuration.
userImpact: Deployments no longer fail with ClientException errors when provisioning ECS resources with root user credentials.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 add configurations for missing bootstrap params
summary: The ECS bootstrap task was missing environment variables for the data plane client secret, and the CloudFormation stack lacked parameters for root user bootstrapping. This change adds the missing configurations to both the app-plane and data-plane ECS templates, along with corresponding parameter definitions in the dev parameters and root scope files.
userImpact: Bootstrap processes for ECS tasks and root user initialization now have all required configuration parameters, enabling proper setup and functionality.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 fix ECS entrypoint for latest bootstrap command
summary: Updated the ECS entrypoint command to use the latest bootstrap script. The ECS start command was still referencing an old bootstrap script, which has now been corrected to ensure proper initialization.
userImpact: ECS deployments now use the correct bootstrap command and initialize properly on startup.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency Faker to v29
summary: Updated Faker dependency from v28.4.1 to v29.0.0. This release includes a fix for pydecimal distribution when called with a range across 0.
userImpact: Faker library now correctly handles pydecimal generation across the zero value, fixing previously incorrect distribution behavior.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency anyio to ~=4.5.0
summary: Updated anyio dependency from ~=4.4.0 to ~=4.5.0. This minor version upgrade includes performance improvements for Lock and Semaphore, support for new Python 3.13 Path methods, enhanced process spawning with additional keyword arguments, and several bug fixes including subprocess handling and socket stream behavior.
userImpact: Users benefit from up to 50% performance improvements in anyio's locking primitives, better Python 3.13 compatibility, and fixes for subprocess and socket stream edge cases.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1230: docker-compose set up for upsolve
summary: Added docker-compose configuration and bootstrap scripts to enable local development of the upsolve service. This includes database initialization scripts, upsolve-specific bootstrap logic, and necessary environment configuration for the upsolve container to integrate with the app-plane and existing services.
userImpact: Developers can now run upsolve locally using docker-compose alongside the rest of the development environment without manual setup.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1346 - Correctly update latest_run_start update
summary: Fixed a bug where the latest_run_start timestamp was not being correctly updated when a job transitioned to the running state. The field is an implementation detail excluded from API models, so it was previously missing from test coverage.
userImpact: Job run timestamps are now accurately tracked and updated when jobs start running.
author: peter496

title: UP-1467 - Dashboard updates
summary: Enhanced the Shield dashboard with a new inference count over time visualization and improved sensitive data rule charts with more descriptive titles and corrected axis labels. These updates provide clearer insights into system performance and data governance metrics.
userImpact: Users can now view inference counts over time and see clearer, better-labeled charts for sensitive data rule monitoring on the dashboard.
author: peter496

title: feat: add configurable interval to policy alert rules
summary: Adds a configurable `interval` field to policy alert rules, allowing users to customize the check frequency instead of using a hardcoded default. The interval is stored in the database with columns for `interval_unit` and `interval_count`, and materialization/bulk-update operations now respect the configured value. The change is backward compatible with a default of 1 day when not specified.
userImpact: Users can now specify custom check intervals when creating or updating policy alert rules, giving them greater control over alert rule execution frequency.
author: videetparekh1995

title: add engine audit logs to sts dev deployment
summary: Enables audit logging for the genai-engine in the STS Arthur dev deployment environment. This change activates audit log collection for the development environment to test and validate audit logging functionality before broader rollout.
userImpact: Development team can now test and validate engine audit logging functionality in the STS Arthur dev deployment.
author: talerez

title: chore: adjust aurora cpu alarm configuration
summary: Adjusted Aurora RDS CPU alarm configuration to support customizable datapoints threshold and established new default values. This provides greater flexibility in alarm behavior for CPU monitoring.
userImpact: Users can now configure the number of datapoints required to trigger Aurora CPU alarms, with improved default settings for more predictable alarm behavior.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Add --frozen flag on uv sync calls to install exactly what's locked in the lockfile
summary: The `uv sync` command is now invoked with the `--frozen` flag across Docker and CI/CD configurations. This ensures dependencies are installed exactly as specified in the lockfile, preventing unexpected version changes during builds.
userImpact: Builds and deployments now use locked dependency versions consistently, reducing the risk of environment drift and ensuring reproducible installations.
author: nori2

title: AuthZ explainer doc
summary: Added comprehensive authorization overview documentation to explain AuthZ concepts and workflows. This documentation serves as a reference guide for understanding the system's authorization mechanisms and implementation details.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: zach.fry

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates via Renovate for non-major versions, including minor and patch updates to authlib, fastapi, pydantic, boto3, faker, arthur-common, and spicedb. This MR applies routine maintenance updates to keep dependencies current and address any bug fixes or improvements in minor releases.
userImpact: No direct user impact; routine dependency maintenance to incorporate upstream bug fixes and improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: feat: add init container to set volume permissions
summary: Added an init container to set proper permissions on the audit log volume and file at container startup. This ensures the audit logging volume is accessible and writable before the main application container starts, preventing permission-related failures.
userImpact: Audit logging now functions reliably without permission-related errors when the application starts.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: update upsolve dashboard patch with new dashboards
summary: Updated the Upsolve dashboard patch from version 2026041416260 to 2026041615281, introducing new dashboards to the Upsolve system. This patch update provides users with enhanced dashboard functionality and improved visibility into their Upsolve data.
userImpact: Users now have access to new dashboards in the Upsolve system with the latest dashboard patch applied.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: mypy from 1.20.0 to 1.20.1 and pydantic_core from 2.45.0 to 2.46.0. These are routine patch and minor version updates that include bug fixes and improvements to the mypy type checker.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: feat: parameterize hardcoded ports in docker-compose services
summary: Parameterized hardcoded port numbers and bootstrap credentials in docker-compose services using environment variable substitution. Services now accept KEYCLOAK_PORT, VITE_PORT, GENAI_ENGINE_PORT, GENAI_UI_PORT, and credential variables with sensible defaults, enabling compatibility with arthur-stack's multi-stack port offset system while maintaining backward compatibility.
userImpact: Users can now run multiple docker-compose stacks on the same host by setting port offset environment variables (e.g., KEYCLOAK_PORT=8087), and existing deployments without env vars continue to work with default port assignments.
author: videetparekh1995

title: feat: add filters to list policies endpoint (UP-4244)
summary: Added three new query parameter filters to the list policies endpoint: `owner_group_id` (equality filter), `compliance_status`, and `model_id` (both joined through policy_model_assignments). These filters enable users to narrow down policy lists by owner group, compliance status, and associated model, while the existing case-insensitive name search remains functional.
userImpact: Users can now filter policies by owner group ID, compliance status, and model ID when listing policies, enabling faster navigation and management of large policy sets.
author: videetparekh1995

title: [no-ref] add policy resource to upsolve token API
summary: Added "policy" as a supported resource type for the Upsolve token API. The token now returns all model IDs a user can access that have the policy assigned, enabling support for the policy overview dashboard.
userImpact: Users can now request Upsolve tokens with policy resource access, allowing the policy overview dashboard to function with proper permission scoping.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] export policy dashboard
summary: Exported the policy dashboard from the Upsolve scope and made the filter controllable. This change relocates the dashboard configuration as part of ongoing refactoring of the policy management system.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: add audit log forwarding to cloudwatch
summary: Adds audit log forwarding to CloudWatch with configurable environment variables. The Dockerfile now creates the audit logs directory with proper ownership before switching to the arthur user, resolving permission issues that previously prevented audit log writes.
userImpact: Audit logs are now automatically forwarded to CloudWatch for centralized monitoring and retention, with configurable logging parameters to control behavior based on deployment needs.
author: talerez

title: revert adding audit logging to aws
summary: This change reverts the recent addition of audit log forwarding to AWS infrastructure. The revert removes audit logging configuration from the ECS application plane and monitoring CloudFormation stacks, undoing changes that were introduced in a prior commit.
userImpact: AWS audit logging forwarding is no longer active, restoring the previous logging behavior.

summary: Disabled audit logs in the Arthur Scope application plane CloudFormation stack configuration. This is an infrastructure configuration change with no direct user-facing impact.

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Routine dependency updates for non-major versions including boto3 (1.42.87 → 1.42.88) and arthur-common (2.4.54 → 2.4.55). These are automated patch and minor version bumps to keep dependencies current with bug fixes and minor API enhancements from upstream packages.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Update upsolve db patch file
summary: Updated the upsolve database patch file to upsolve-patch-2026041319244 and bumped the upsolve base image from 0.4.24 to 0.4.26. This ensures the container is using the latest compatible patch and base image versions.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updates all non-major dependencies across the project, including boto3 (patch), OpenTelemetry packages (minor versions), pytest, type stubs, and TimescaleDB. These updates bring in the latest bug fixes, minor improvements, and type-checking enhancements while maintaining backward compatibility.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved AWS SDK functionality, enhanced observability capabilities from OpenTelemetry, better testing tools, and updated type information.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: add a new log group for audit logs in cloudwatch
summary: Adds a new CloudWatch log group in the scope deployment to support the new audit log system. The log group is configured with a Retain deletion policy to preserve audit logs for 365 days after stack teardown, ensuring audit trail continuity across infrastructure changes.
userImpact: Audit logs are now captured and retained in CloudWatch for compliance and operational visibility, persisting beyond stack lifecycle changes.
author: talerez

title: feat: split compliance rule ID into policy rule ID and materialized rule ID
summary: ComplianceRuleSummary now distinguishes between the policy rule definition ID (via the `id` field) and the materialized alert rule ID (via the new `materialized_rule_id` field). This change clarifies the two levels of rule identity: policy definitions and their concrete materializations on the alert rules model, resolved through a join on DBComplianceRuleHistory without requiring a schema migration.
userImpact: Users can now separately access the policy rule definition ID and the materialized alert rule ID through ComplianceRuleSummary, enabling more precise identification and fetching of rules via the alert rules API.
author: videetparekh1995

title: feat: policy compliance history endpoint
summary: Added a new `GET /v1/policies/{policy_id}/compliance_history` endpoint that returns the complete rule compliance evaluation history with infinite pagination. The implementation includes a reusable `list_compliance_history` repository method supporting list-based filters, a new `POLICY_LIST_COMPLIANCE_HISTORY` permission in SpiceDB, and a `created_at` field in `ComplianceRow` for visibility into historical evaluation timestamps.
userImpact: Users can now query the full compliance history for any policy, with support for filtering by policy, assignment, model, and rule IDs to track how compliance evaluations have evolved over time.
author: videetparekh1995

title: [no-ref] fix datasketches compilation on 2.26.1
summary: Fixed datasketches compilation issues on Timescale 2.26.1 by updating the build configuration in the metrics database Dockerfile. This resolves compilation failures when building the extension against the newer Timescale version.
userImpact: Users can now successfully build and deploy the metrics database extension on Timescale 2.26.1 without compilation errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.42.83→1.42.84), faker (40.12.0→40.13.0), and uvicorn (0.43.0→0.44.0). These are routine dependency updates managed by Renovate to keep the project current with upstream patches and minor releases.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency updates are internal maintenance to ensure security and compatibility.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore: use custom phili var
summary: Updated the GitLab CI configuration to use a custom phili variable in the inquirer-sts-platform deployment environment. This is a configuration change to standardize variable usage in the CI/CD pipeline.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: add workspace compliance overview endpoint (UP-4155)
summary: Adds `GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/compliance` endpoint providing a flat compliance overview table with per-rule, per-model status across all workspace policies. Introduces a normalized `compliance_rule_statuses` table backed by Alembic migration and backfill logic, with dual-write synchronization in `set_compliance_status` to keep both the JSON column and normalized table in sync. Endpoint supports comprehensive filtering (status, policy, model, rule_type, rule_id, frequency, text search), sorting, pagination, and summary rollup counts.
userImpact: Users can now query a unified compliance overview for their entire workspace with rich filtering and sorting capabilities, replacing the need for manual JSON inspection of compliance status details.
author: videetparekh1995

title: create audit logging middleware
summary: Adds audit logging middleware that captures HTTP request and response metadata including the request path, authenticated user, path parameters (resource IDs), and response IDs. The middleware is configurable via environment variables to enable organizations to control audit logging behavior and integrate with external logging systems like CloudWatch.
userImpact: Users can now track API access patterns and audit user actions by enabling audit logging, which captures detailed metadata about each API call including who accessed which resources and what responses were returned.
author: talerez

title: Add package management info
summary: Added documentation on package management using uv to CLAUDE.md. This provides users and developers with guidance on managing project dependencies and environment setup with the uv package manager.
userImpact: Users can now reference documentation on how to use uv for package management in the project.
author: nori2

title: Enforce 3-day package minimum for uv dependency resolution
summary: Enforces a 3-day minimum age threshold for packages resolved during uv dependency resolution. This ensures that newly published packages with potential stability issues are not inadvertently used, improving build reliability and reducing the risk of introducing unstable dependencies.
userImpact: Dependency resolution now automatically excludes packages published within the last 3 days, reducing the risk of using unstable or untested packages in builds.
author: nori2

title: create audit logging tdd
summary: Created the technical design documentation for audit logging, defining the audit log event structure and schema. This document outlines how audit events will be captured, including resource identification from request payloads and path parameters, user tracking, and versioned event objects to establish an API contract for downstream consumers.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Add configurable port and CORS support for multi-stack development
summary: Added support for configurable application port and CORS origins via environment variables (`SCOPE_APP_PORT` and `CORS_EXTRA_ORIGINS`), enabling multiple development stacks to run simultaneously without port collisions. The docker-compose.yaml has been updated to parameterize host-mapped ports for the application, database services, and related infrastructure.
userImpact: Developers can now run multiple instances of the application stack in parallel by setting `SCOPE_APP_PORT` and extending allowed CORS origins with `CORS_EXTRA_ORIGINS`, eliminating port conflict issues during multi-stack development.

title: UP-4128 add project_id to policy assignments response
summary: The policy assignments API response now includes the project_id field. This change adds the missing project identifier to policy assignment responses, enabling clients to correlate policy assignments with their associated projects without additional lookups.

title: Fix alert_interval migration ORM breakage
summary: The `alert_interval` migration was importing `DBAlertRule` from the ORM and querying/updating rows during migration execution. As new columns were added to the model (e.g., `policy_alert_rule_id`), SQLAlchemy generated SELECTs referencing non-existent columns in the migration context, breaking fresh database bootstraps. Replaced all ORM queries with raw SQL UPDATE statements that are decoupled from the evolving model.
userImpact: Fresh database bootstraps now complete successfully without UndefinedColumn errors during the alert_interval migration.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Bump upsolve to 0.4.24
summary: Bumps the upsolve container image from 0.4.23 to 0.4.24 and updates the associated database patch file. This update includes a new patch version to ensure compatibility with the upgraded upsolve service.
userImpact: Users will receive the latest upsolve service improvements and bug fixes included in version 0.4.24.

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updates non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.42.74 → 1.42.78), numpy (2.4.3 → 2.4.4), and types-requests (2.32.4.20260324 → 2.33.0.20260327). These are routine patch and minor version updates managed through Renovate.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency maintenance update to keep packages current with latest non-breaking versions.

title: Add scheduled compliance job bootstrapping
summary: Introduces scheduled compliance job bootstrapping that automatically creates a per-model compliance schedule chain when a policy assignment is created, using deterministic UUID5-based schedule IDs to prevent duplication. The system now registers the SCHEDULE_COMPLIANCE_JOBS job kind with infinite retries, 45MB limit, and MEDIUM priority, and routes it through the existing job spec extractor infrastructure.
userImpact: Users can now have compliance checks automatically scheduled at the model level when policy assignments are created, with built-in deduplication ensuring only one schedule chain runs per model.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Clean up the data_plane code that's been migrated to Github
summary: Removed the data_plane code that has been migrated to a separate Github repository. This cleans up the codebase by eliminating duplicate code that is now maintained elsewhere, reducing maintenance burden and avoiding divergence between repositories.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal code organization improvement to eliminate duplicate maintenance of migrated data_plane functionality.
author: nori2

title: UP-4128 return entire compliance job instead of just ID
summary: The policy_assignment resource now returns the complete compliance job object instead of just the job ID. This includes detailed information about the job state, spec, timing, and trigger context, providing richer visibility into policy compliance execution.
userImpact: Users can now access full compliance job details directly from policy assignment responses, eliminating the need for separate job lookup calls.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Submit compliance jobs when policy assignments are created
summary: Automatically trigger compliance check jobs when policy assignments are created, and when alert or attestation rules are created, updated, or deleted. Jobs are submitted immediately with a compliance_job_id returned in the response. Compliance logic has been moved from routers into operators to ensure jobs are triggered consistently regardless of call site, and the check_policy_compliance method now accepts a raise_on_empty parameter so rule operations on policies with no assignments are safe no-ops.
userImpact: Users no longer need to manually trigger compliance checks after creating policy assignments or modifying policy rules; checks are now automatically submitted and tracked via returned compliance_job_id.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4154 Fire webhook on compliance status transition to NON_COMPLIANT
summary: Fire a policy's notification webhook when a compliance status transitions to NON_COMPLIANT after the enforcement delay expires. The system now tracks when violations are notified via `last_violation_notified_at` on policy assignments, cleared on return to COMPLIANT status, and exposes this field in API models for visibility into notification history.
userImpact: Users can now receive timely webhook notifications when policy assignments transition to non-compliant status, with audit trail visibility through the exposed `last_violation_notified_at` field.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 compliance check endpoints
summary: Adds three new compliance check endpoints for policies, models, and policy assignments that trigger compliance jobs and return the list of jobs created. Each endpoint sets a compliance_job_id on the relevant assignment resource, which is cleared when compliance status is subsequently set via the set_compliance_status endpoint.
userImpact: Users can now trigger compliance checks via POST endpoints for policies, models, and policy assignments, receiving a list of queued jobs and tracking job progress via the compliance_job_id field.
author: ianmcgraw

userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Policy assignment endpoints with materialization and authz
summary: Implements full policy assignment, attestation, and compliance status API endpoints with comprehensive authorization, input validation, and cascade deletion. Restructures the monolithic PoliciesOperator/Repository into per-resource operator/repository pairs (PolicyAssignments, Attestations) with cross-resource logic handled at the operator level, ensuring clean separation of concerns and testability.
userImpact: Users can now create, list, and delete policy assignments across multiple models with full SpiceDB authorization checks; create and view attestation records per assignment with support for latest/valid filters; and set/view compliance status with detailed alert/attestation rule results, all with proper cascade deletion on unassignment.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add NEEDS_ATTENTION compliance status for enforcement delay period
summary: Introduces `NEEDS_ATTENTION` as a new compliance status for policy assignments with rule violations still within the enforcement delay grace period. This intermediate status provides model owners time to remediate without triggering webhooks or tags, and automatically transitions to `NON_COMPLIANT` once the delay expires. The compliance evaluation logic and metrics have been updated to reflect this new status in the enforcement workflow.
userImpact: Model owners can now see policies that need attention before enforcement begins, allowing them to remediate violations within the grace period without triggering compliance incidents or automation.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 api tweaks for job
summary: Added multiple API filtering capabilities to support the compliance check job: model list policy assignments now support assignment_id filtering, alert rules API supports assignment_id filtering and returns policy_assignment_id and policy_alert_rule_id fields, attestations API adds latest=true and valid=true filters. Additionally, PATCH and DELETE operations are now blocked for policy-managed alert rules to prevent accidental modification.
userImpact: Users can now filter policy assignments, alert rules, and attestations by assignment ID and attestation validity status, and policy-managed alert rules are protected from direct modification through the API.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-4128 add compliance check job API spec
summary: Added API specification and database support for a new compliance check job type. The changes include schema updates, API models, and repository logic to enable compliance job creation and management through the jobs API.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage compliance check jobs through the API using the new job type specification.
author: ianmcgraw

title: uv for dependency management
summary: Replaced requirements.txt and poetry.lock with uv as the unified dependency manager across app-plane and arthur-client. This change modernizes the development workflow with improved dependency pinning capabilities via uv.lock files and simplifies the CI/CD pipeline by using uv sync instead of multiple package management tools.
userImpact: Users and developers can now use uv for consistent, reproducible dependency management with pinned versions across all development environments and CI pipelines.
author: nori2

title: Implement policy alert and attestation rule CRUD with authz and propagation
summary: Implements full CRUD operations for policy alert rules and attestation rules with SpiceDB authorization enforcement. Alert rules include static SQL validation and live propagation to materialized alert rule rows across all assigned models. Attestation rules support creation, listing, retrieval, updates to metadata, and deletion with cascade safety checks preventing removal if it's the last rule on an applied policy.
userImpact: Users can now create, read, update, and delete policy alert and attestation rules through dedicated REST endpoints, with automatic validation and permission enforcement ensuring data consistency across all policy assignments.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Policy CRUD (Create/Read/Update/Delete) with Inline Rules
summary: Implemented complete Policy CRUD operations with inline rules management. Added `PoliciesRepository` with atomic policy + rule creation, eager-loaded retrieval, paginated list with filtering/sorting, and cascade delete; `PoliciesOperator` for transactional wrapping; and 5 wired router endpoints with SpiceDB permission checks. Includes database translators for API ↔ DB model conversion and comprehensive endpoint lifecycle tests covering all CRUD operations, validation, filtering, and error cases.
userImpact: Users can now create, read, update, and delete policies with inline alert and attestation rules through fully authorized REST endpoints, with permission enforcement via SpiceDB.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add an alert rule validation endpoint without modelId requirement
summary: Added a new alert rule validation endpoint that validates alert rule definitions without requiring a modelId parameter. This enables users to validate alert rule syntax and structure independently of a specific model, while an accompanying endpoint configuration change whitelists the endpoint from authorization checks to allow static validation without full permission scoping.
userImpact: Users can now validate alert rule configurations standalone using a new endpoint that doesn't require model context, streamlining validation workflows for alert rule setup.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Add policy ORM models, database relationships, and migration
summary: Added five new database tables to support policy-based governance: `policies`, `policy_alert_rules`, `policy_attestation_rules`, `policy_model_assignments`, and `attestation_records`. Extended the `alert_rules` table with nullable foreign keys to link materialized alert rules back to their policy source, and added `POLICY_PARENT` and `MODEL_PARENT` to the `AuthZRelationship` enum to support dual-parent SpiceDB relationships on policy assignments. All changes include an Alembic migration with named constraints for rollback support.
userImpact: Organizations can now create organization-scoped policies with alert rule templates and attestation rules, assign policies to models to track compliance status, and record attestation actions with immutable audit trails.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Use oidc
summary: Replaced access key-based authentication with OpenID Connect (OIDC) across all deployment environments and platforms. This migration improves security by eliminating the need to manage long-lived access keys and includes account verification steps to ensure proper authorization. The change consolidates authentication logic and removes deprecated axios-based STS authentication.
userImpact: Deployments now use OIDC authentication instead of access keys, improving security and reducing credential management overhead while maintaining the same deployment workflow.
author: jdb-arthur

title: [no-ref] set renovate minimumReleaseAge 3 days
summary: Updated Renovate configuration to enforce a 3-day minimumReleaseAge delay before automatically upgrading packages. This provides a grace period for package maintainers to identify and revoke malicious or vulnerable versions before they are adopted.
userImpact: Dependencies will be upgraded with a 3-day delay from their release date, reducing the risk of immediately adopting compromised packages.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Atomic policy creation with inline rules and field renames
summary: Policies can now be created atomically with inline alert and attestation rules in a single POST request; at least one rule must be provided at creation time. The response has been simplified to return only PolicySummary (id, name, description). Field names have been standardized: grace_period_days → enforcement_delay_days, grace_period_ends_at → enforcement_starts_at, and interval_days → validity_period_days.
userImpact: Users can now create policies with rules in one atomic operation, eliminating the previous two-step process of creating a policy then adding rules separately.
author: videetparekh1995

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

These are routine dependency updates managed by Renovate to keep libraries current with bug fixes and minor improvements.

author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Redistribute policy permissions across existing roles
summary: Redistributed policy permissions across roles to enforce proper isolation: removed the premature Policy Manager role, consolidated model-level policy operations (`assign`, `remove`, `attest`, `check_compliance`) into Project Admin, and eliminated dead SpiceDB code. Added `is_assigned_in` reverse relation enabling model owners to read policies assigned to their models via a cross-model-isolated permission path. Introduced `NEEDS_ATTENTION` compliance status for grace period violations and restricted Project Reader to read-only access without compliance checking capabilities.
userImpact: Users with Project Admin role can now manage policy assignments and attestations on models in their project, while model owners can view policies assigned to their models without org-level policy read permissions—maintaining proper model isolation and following the design principle that org-level roles grant no model-level access.
author: videetparekh1995

summary: Upgraded upsolve to fix an issue where migrations were not running on fresh or existing installations. title: Bug fix: PUT Agents un-sets model id and unregisters registered agents

userImpact: Agents will no longer lose their model IDs or registration status when updated via the PUT endpoint.

userImpact: Existing deployments can now successfully upgrade without database migration failures.

summary: Split the Helm chart for control plane into separate stateful and stateless service charts. Stateful services (PostgreSQL, SpiceDB, TimescaleDB) are now managed in a dedicated `stateful_services` Helm chart, while stateless services remain in the `control_plane` chart. This improves deployment flexibility and allows independent scaling and management of stateful vs stateless components.
userImpact: Deployments must now use two Helm charts—one for stateless services and one for stateful services—providing better separation of concerns and independent deployment control.

title: UP-3081: Implement CSP
summary: Content Security Policy (CSP) infrastructure was partially implemented on the backend, including CSP reporting endpoint and configuration. After review discussion, the team determined that CSP headers must be served by the frontend container rather than the backend API, as CSP is only enforced by browsers on HTML entry points.
userImpact: No direct user impact; CSP header enforcement moved to frontend infrastructure for proper browser-level protection.

title: UP-3128: Deploy GenAI Engine from trigger
summary: Added capability to deploy the Arthur GenAI Engine to the dev environment via a POST trigger with a trigger token and deployment variables. The implementation includes GitLab CI/CD pipeline configuration, deployment documentation, and a trigger script to initiate deployments remotely.
userImpact: Users can now trigger GenAI Engine deployments to the dev environment by sending a POST request with the appropriate token and variables, enabling automated and on-demand deployment workflows.

title: Deploy metrics db ha

This includes new CloudFormation templates for HA cluster infrastructure, deployment scripts for AWS environments, updated Dockerfiles with unified build processes, and comprehensive operations documentation for managing the HA setup.
userImpact: Users can now deploy metrics database in high-availability mode with automatic failover and leader election via Patroni, ensuring improved reliability and uptime for metrics data.

userImpact: Import sorting now works with Python 3.14 and reports absolute paths in skipped file messages, with improved handling of hanging indents in complex import statements.

title: Increasing the Aurora DB ACU to 8-32 ACU

userImpact: Production database can now scale to handle increased workloads with a minimum of 8 ACUs and maximum of 32 ACUs.

title: UP-815 fix role binding permission check
summary: Fixed a permission check bug that prevented IDP-controlled users from assigning role bindings. The role binding operator now uses SpiceDB and token context to verify group membership instead of the application database, which no longer tracks IDP-managed group memberships.
userImpact: Users in IDP-managed groups can now successfully assign role bindings to other groups without encountering permission errors.

title: [no-ref] fix keycloak local dev https required error
summary: Fixed a local development issue where Keycloak was incorrectly requiring HTTPS due to docker port forwarding requests no longer originating from recognized private IP addresses. The fix configures Keycloak's SSL mode to "none" for the local arthur realm, allowing developers to authenticate over HTTP on localhost.
userImpact: Local developers can now successfully bootstrap and authenticate with Keycloak in local development environments using the latest Docker Desktop without encountering HTTPS requirement errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

These tests ensure that nested segmentation columns do not produce errors and support the functionality added in related engine and common library changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: UP-2845: Custom aggregation product docs
summary: Added comprehensive product documentation for custom aggregation metrics, including an overview guide with step-by-step examples, screenshots of metric configurations, and guidance on aggregation arguments. Documentation includes links to related query overview guides and covers inference count and category count metric patterns with visual walkthroughs of the UI.
userImpact: Users can now access detailed documentation on how to create and configure custom aggregation metrics with visual examples and best practices.

summary: Separated configuration values for frontend and authentication hosts in Helm templates. This allows independent configuration of the frontend and Keycloak authentication service endpoints, improving deployment flexibility.
userImpact: Users can now configure frontend and authentication hosts independently in Helm values instead of using a single shared host value.

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: Timescale db ha
summary: Added TimescaleDB base image with Patroni for high-availability database replication management. Includes new Dockerfile, helper scripts for EC2 instance management, and updated compose configuration to support multi-node Patroni cluster setup.
userImpact: Users can now deploy and manage a highly available TimescaleDB cluster using Patroni for automated replication and failover across multiple nodes.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-3074: Introduce search functionality list aggs
summary: This change introduces search functionality for list aggregations across the application. Updates to the base repository add filtering capabilities, while the custom aggregations repository extends support for searching list-based aggregations. A new router endpoint was added to expose this functionality through the workspaces API.
userImpact: Users can now search and filter custom aggregations in list format through the workspaces API.
author: alex380125

title: UP-3070: Custom aggregations test tech design
summary: Technical design document created to establish decisions for testing custom aggregations in Arthur AI. The design addresses three key architectural questions: where to store test custom aggregation specs, where to store the resulting test metrics, and whether tests should run over datasets or models. The document was reviewed with stakeholder input to finalize implementation approach.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: Ssl bypass client
summary: Updated ArthurOIDCMetadata to accept a verify_ssl parameter for secure requests. This allows clients to control SSL verification behavior during authentication discovery and device authorization flows, enabling flexibility in different deployment environments.
userImpact: Users can now configure SSL verification when establishing OIDC connections, improving support for environments with custom certificates or specific security requirements.
author: ibrahim109

title: UP-2842: Add support for custom metrics in aggregation matcher
summary: The aggregation matcher now supports custom metrics alongside default metrics, enabling users to create and match aggregations based on custom-defined metrics in their workspace. This required refactoring the aggregation loading and matching logic to handle custom metrics with their versioned configurations and argument specifications.
userImpact: Users can now define and use custom metrics in aggregation matchers, allowing greater flexibility in how aggregations are configured and applied across datasets and models.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: Deploy arthur engine
summary: This merge request enables deployment of the Arthur Engine to the AWS dev environment with dynamic version management. The implementation includes deployment scripts, GitLab CI configuration, and infrastructure setup to automatically detect and deploy the appropriate Arthur Engine version to the dev platform.
userImpact: Users can now deploy and run the Arthur Engine in the AWS dev environment with automatic version detection and deployment orchestration.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Avoid cert copy that's not working in JPMC
summary: Fixed certificate copy operation that was failing in JPMC environments by removing the non-working cert copy step from the Docker entrypoint and Dockerfile. This resolves deployment issues specific to JPMC deployments where the previous cert copy logic was causing failures.
userImpact: JPMC deployments now complete successfully without certificate copy errors.
author: nori2

title: Postgres HA with streaming replication and auto-failover with Patroni and etcd
summary: Added a RHEL-compatible Docker Compose setup for deploying a 3-node PostgreSQL cluster with 1 primary and 2 replicas using Patroni and etcd for high availability. The configuration includes streaming replication and automatic primary failover capabilities, enabling production-ready PostgreSQL HA deployments.
userImpact: Users can now quickly stand up and test a highly available PostgreSQL cluster with automatic failover and data replication using a pre-configured Docker Compose environment.
author: nori2

title: add validations and unit tests for custom aggregations
summary: Implemented comprehensive validation for custom aggregations by consolidating all model-level validations into a single BaseCreateCustomAggregationSpecSchema validation function. This function performs all validation checks and returns a complete list of passes and failures, enabling users to see all issues at once rather than one error at a time. Added extensive unit tests covering nested columns, aliases, and SQL validation scenarios.
userImpact: Users now receive comprehensive validation feedback with all issues identified in a single API call when creating custom aggregations, improving the configuration experience in the UI.
author: talerez

title: Ssl bypass client
summary: Added verify_ssl parameter to OAuth session configuration and DeviceAuthorizer to allow clients to bypass SSL verification when needed. This enables flexibility in environments with non-standard certificate setups or development scenarios where SSL verification may need to be disabled.
userImpact: Users can now disable SSL verification for OAuth authentication by setting the verify_ssl parameter in their client configuration.
author: ibrahim109

title: Refactor SSL context configuration in JWKClient to ensure verify_mode is set...
summary: Fixed SSL context configuration in JWKClient to properly set verify_mode when SSL verification is disabled. This ensures the SSL verification setting is correctly applied when the client initializes its connection context.
userImpact: SSL verification settings in JWKClient are now correctly enforced when disabled, preventing potential certificate validation issues.
author: ibrahim109

title: Implement SSL context configuration for JWKClient based on Keycloak SSL...
summary: Refactored SSL context handling in JWKClient to support configurable SSL verification, including the ability to create unverified SSL contexts for non-verified connections. This improves compatibility with Keycloak SSL configurations and provides more flexible SSL management for JWT validation endpoints.
userImpact: Users can now configure SSL context behavior for JWKClient connections, enabling support for self-signed certificates and custom SSL configurations when connecting to Keycloak or other JWT providers.
author: ibrahim109

title: Implement SSL context configuration for JWKClient based on Keycloak SSL...
summary: Enhanced JWKClient with SSL context configuration support for improved Keycloak integration. The implementation now respects SSL verification environment variables, allowing operators to control certificate validation behavior based on their deployment requirements.
userImpact: Users can now configure SSL certificate verification for Keycloak JWK endpoints via environment variables, enabling flexible deployment in environments with self-signed or internal certificates.
author: ibrahim109

title: UP-2994: Support for nested metric arguments
summary: Added support for nested metric arguments, enabling more complex metric configurations. This enhancement allows users to pass nested data structures as metric arguments, expanding the flexibility of metric definitions and improving the expressiveness of metric queries.
userImpact: Users can now define and use nested metric arguments in their metric configurations, enabling more sophisticated and complex metric queries.
author: alex380125

title: Retrieve Keycloak SSL verification setting from environment variable
summary: The KeycloakClient now retrieves its SSL verification setting from an environment variable instead of using a hardcoded value. This allows operators to control SSL verification behavior at deployment time without code changes, improving flexibility for different deployment environments.
userImpact: Users can now configure Keycloak SSL verification behavior via environment variable, enabling secure deployments across different environments with varying certificate requirements.
author: ibrahim109

title: fix: remove entrypoint override
summary: Removed an entrypoint override for ml-engine in the docker-compose configuration. This resolves an issue where the manual override was causing incorrect behavior or preventing the service from running with its default entrypoint.

title: Eliminate the use of cp command. It's not working in the JPMC environment and we can't reproduce it.
summary: Replaced the use of the cp command in the spicedb Dockerfile and docker-entrypoint.sh scripts with an alternative approach. The cp command was not functioning in the JPMC environment, causing deployment issues that could not be reproduced in standard environments.
userImpact: SpiceDB deployments in the JPMC environment now work correctly without cp command failures.

title: fix: remove profile from aws cli commands
summary: Removed the profile parameter from AWS CLI commands in the deployment script. This fixes issues where profile configuration was interfering with AWS CLI operations during stack deployment.
userImpact: AWS stack deployments now execute without profile-related configuration conflicts.
