title: UP-2172: Create sign up endpoint
summary: A new sign-up endpoint has been created to enable user registration and organization creation via an email-based invitation workflow. Users can now accept invitations, authenticate via Keycloak, and be automatically provisioned with an organization and org member role. The implementation includes backend registration router, updated authentication clients, and comprehensive unit test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now sign up for accounts via email invitation and be automatically assigned to an organization with the org member role upon accepting the invitation.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2210 upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgraded upsolve backend dependency to version 0.2.31. The export functionality remains unchanged, and this update brings the latest upstream improvements and bug fixes from the upsolve project.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest upsolve backend improvements and stability enhancements without any breaking changes to the export functionality.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2209 make numeric sketch metrics use continuous tag
summary: Numeric sketch metrics now respect a continuous tag to distinguish between truly continuous numeric columns and categorical columns represented as integers. Previously, all INT and FLOAT columns received sketch metrics, causing PSI drift detection to fail on low-cardinality categorical integer columns that couldn't form 10 distinct buckets. This change ensures categorical integer columns receive categorical count metrics instead.
userImpact: Users can now tag numeric columns as categorical or continuous to receive appropriate metrics—sketch aggregation for continuous values and categorical count for categorical values, fixing data drift calculations on low-cardinality integer columns.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2073 tech design for shield state sync
summary: Technical design documentation for Shield state synchronization with the control plane, covering implementation options discussed at the Mar. 2025 offsite. The document outlines API contracts, task state management, and integration patterns for syncing Shield tasks and rules with the Arthur control plane.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] increase auth replicas to two
summary: Increased authentication service replicas from one to two across all ECS environments to prevent zero-replica alerts. This ensures service availability during maintenance windows by guaranteeing at least one replica remains healthy when another is being updated.
userImpact: Users will experience improved reliability and uptime of authentication services, with no service interruptions during infrastructure maintenance.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2167, UP-2169: add config for enabling emails and forgot password flow
summary: Added configuration options to enable email sending and the forgot password flow in Keycloak. Administrators can now configure SMTP settings (host, port, username, password) with support for both StartTLS and SSL, and enable/disable the forgot password feature via configuration parameters. Changes include new config entries, CloudFormation parameters to accept credentials, and bootstrap logic to apply these settings to the Keycloak realm.
userImpact: Administrators can now configure email capabilities in Keycloak, enabling users to receive password reset emails and use the forgot password flow in SaaS deployments.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1698 add axios ground truth dataset
summary: Added scripts for creating the Axios ground truth dataset, including utilities for fetching inference schema and managing ground-truth onboarding. These scripts support internal dataset preparation workflows for the Axios customer.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Onprem functionality with OpenShift
summary: Added Helm charts to deploy Scope on-premise environments running on OpenShift. Includes control plane and data plane charts with templates for all core components (App Plane, frontend, Keycloak, Redis, SpiceDB, Upsolve) and configuration examples for secrets management.
userImpact: Users can now deploy and manage Scope on on-premise OpenShift clusters using Helm, enabling self-hosted deployments with standardized Kubernetes manifests.
author: kacperek0

title: Patch token cost calculation for nulls
summary: Fixed token cost calculation to handle null values from legacy Shield instances that didn't send token data. The aggregation query now uses COALESCE to return 0 for entries without token values, making the system resilient to null token counts that could cause API validation errors downstream.
userImpact: Token cost aggregations no longer fail when processing legacy data with null token counts, ensuring accurate cost calculations across all Shield instances.

userImpact: Users can now slice and filter metrics by dataset_name and dataset_id, enabling accurate metric tracking and custom dashboards when multiple datasets feed into the same monitoring system.

This change adds a check to skip tag creation if a success tag from AWS dev deployment already exists on the commit.
userImpact: Release tags are now created only for commits with new changes, preventing unnecessary duplicate releases and improving the reliability of the release workflow.

userImpact: Users will no longer receive duplicate alert notifications for the same data point across different metric calculation runs, reducing alert fatigue and improving the clarity of alert history.

userImpact: The timescale service image will now be correctly versioned and tagged in the container registry when built.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update Readme to include changes to /etc/hosts
summary: Updated README with instructions for modifying /etc/hosts on Mac systems to enable communication between Arthur Scope and Arthur Auth. This configuration change was necessary to resolve connectivity issues in local development environments.
userImpact: Mac users can now follow updated setup instructions to configure their /etc/hosts file and properly enable communication between Arthur Scope and Arthur Auth.
author: videetparekh1995

title: chore: scale down old dev to 0
summary: This change scales down all old development ECS services to 0 replicas, effectively decommissioning the legacy dev environment. The update modifies CloudFormation templates across multiple services (app-plane, auth, data-plane, frontend, grafana, spicedb, and upsolve) to remove them from active use.
userImpact: No direct user impact—internal infrastructure change to retire legacy development services.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update dependency faker to v34
summary: Updated the Faker dependency from v33.3.1 to v34.0.2, a major version bump that includes fixes for date_time provider calculations when end_datetime is set in the past, date parsing for "today", and improvements to relative date_time_between calculations. This is a routine dependency update applied to test requirements.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is a dependency update for test infrastructure.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project including boto3, cfn-lint, FastAPI, Pydantic, and TimescaleDB. Changes include patch updates to Python packages and a minor version update to TimescaleDB (2.17.1 to 2.18.0), with a fix applied to timescale setup for compatibility with version 2.18.0.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates maintain compatibility while bringing in minor fixes and improvements to AWS SDK, CloudFormation linting, API framework, and database functionality.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2015 fix migration on dev
summary: Fixed a database state leak in the available dataset spice migration that was polluting the permissions bootstrap function via a shared db_manager instance. The fix clears the db_manager transaction after migration, gives each bootstrap section its own isolated db_manager instance, and adds a missing commit() call to persist permission changes.
userImpact: Permissions bootstrap during development migrations now correctly applies without state corruption from prior migrations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Pin Metrics DB to 2.17.1 to prevent clang-19 error
summary: Pinned the TimescaleDB Docker image to version 2.17.1 to resolve a build-time clang-19 compilation error. This prevents the "No such file or directory" error that was occurring during the metrics database container build.
userImpact: Metrics database builds now complete successfully without clang-19 errors.
author: videetparekh1995

title: chore: allow slower startup time
summary: Updated CloudFormation timeout configurations in the ECS app plane and load balancer templates to accommodate longer startup times. This resolves startup failures that occurred when the application exceeded the previous timeout threshold during initialization.
userImpact: Applications no longer fail to start up if initialization takes longer than the previous timeout threshold.
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: composite alarm definition
summary: Fixed syntax errors in composite alarm CloudFormation definition that were preventing proper deployment. The correction ensures alarm configuration is correctly interpreted and applied by CloudFormation.
userImpact: Composite alarms now deploy correctly with valid CloudFormation syntax.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates multiple non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.36.2 → 1.36.3), cachetools (5.5.0 → 5.5.1), google-cloud-bigquery (3.28.0 → 3.29.0), and pytest_httpserver (1.1.0 → 1.1.1). The updates include bug fixes, documentation improvements, and expanded Python 3.13 support.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1976: data drift chart implementation
summary: Implemented data drift visualization charts that calculate Population Stability Index (PSI) for both categorical and numerical features. The implementation includes SQL queries for reference and test set comparison across configurable date ranges, with visual charts showing distribution shifts over time for regression and binary classification models.
userImpact: Users can now visualize data drift trends through interactive PSI charts that detect and display distribution changes in model features over time.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1993: fix avail datasets pagination
summary: Fixed a pagination bug in the available datasets query where duplicate results from an outer join on `DBDatasetLocatorField` were causing incorrect pagination counts and result distribution. The pagination calculation now accurately reflects the actual number of unique available datasets rather than the inflated count from duplicates.
userImpact: Available datasets now display correct pagination counts and all results fit on the appropriate pages, eliminating the false duplicate entries that previously spread results across multiple pages.
author: alex380125

title: chore: conditionally destroy grafana stack
summary: The Grafana stack can now be conditionally destroyed as part of a phased removal effort. This change allows the ECS service to be deleted, with the target group removal deferred to a subsequent release, reducing deployment risk.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1994 add aggregation problem type tags
summary: Added missing `model_problem_type` tags to aggregation functions in confusion matrix, inference count by class, and shield aggregations. This ensures aggregations are properly categorized and discoverable within the system. Tests were added to validate the presence of these tags.
userImpact: Aggregations are now correctly tagged with their problem types, improving discoverability and proper categorization in the catalog.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Platform v4 Alerts to Slack
summary: Deploys a new alerting stack for Platform v4 that sends alerts to Slack via SNS topics. The stack includes composite alarms for 5xx errors, service count monitoring, custom metrics for manual triggers, and configuration across dev, prod, and customer STS environments.
userImpact: Users can now receive Platform v4 alerts directly in Slack when service issues occur, enabling faster incident detection and response.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: [no-ref] remove `!reference` inside script

BigQuery and GCS service accounts now have minimal, scope-restricted permissions (Data Viewer on specific datasets/tables, Storage Object Viewer on specific buckets) ensuring security when service account secrets are hard-coded in containers.
userImpact: E2E tests can now run securely in Docker containers with minimally-privileged service accounts, reducing security risk from hard-coded credentials.

title: UP-1698 Axios model onboarding script
summary: Added an onboarding script for the Axios model to enable model monitoring and analysis within the system. The script does not currently include the ground truth dataset due to it being empty in Axios's S3 bucket; a follow-up will be needed to onboard and enable metrics on that dataset once it becomes available.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Internal infrastructure change to support Axios model monitoring setup.

title: UP-1984 fix data retrieval job spec missing pagination
summary: A previous database migration failed to populate the required `pagination_options` column for existing data retrieval jobs, causing those jobs to raise exceptions at runtime. This fix adds a new migration that backfills the missing pagination options for all old jobs, ensuring they have the necessary configuration to execute properly.
userImpact: Existing data retrieval jobs that were raising exceptions due to missing pagination configuration will now work correctly.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore: sleep between validate commands
summary: Added sleep delays between validate commands in CI/CD pipelines to prevent rate limiting issues. This resolves timing-related failures that were occurring when validation commands were executed in rapid succession.
userImpact: CI/CD pipelines will no longer encounter rate limiting errors during validation steps, resulting in more reliable automated deployments.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Stop cfn linting main
summary: Disabled CloudFormation linting on push events to main branch after MR merge. Previously, linting was unnecessarily triggered when MRs were merged, consuming CI resources and creating redundant lint checks that had already run during the MR review phase.
userImpact: CI/CD pipelines will no longer run CloudFormation linting after merging an MR to main, reducing pipeline execution time and resource usage.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1934: Fetch data on joined datasets
summary: Adds support for fetching data on joined datasets and joined available datasets by leveraging duckDB for efficient join operations and data handling. This unifies the data retrieval pipeline to work consistently with the metrics processing engine, eliminating the need for separate JSON data handling paths.
userImpact: Users can now fetch raw data from joined datasets, enabling data validation and schema debugging workflows for composite dataset queries.
author: alex380125

title: chore: stop running cfn lint on main all the time
summary: Removed redundant cfn lint job from the main branch CI pipeline. Since cfn lint already runs on all merge requests and was experiencing rate limiting issues, eliminating the duplicate main branch run reduces unnecessary API calls and CI overhead.
userImpact: No direct user impact; resolves CI rate limiting issues and reduces unnecessary linting runs.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: UP-1934: Support joined available datasets
summary: This MR adds support for joined available datasets and their schemas, enabling datasets created from joins to be used as available datasets in the system. The implementation includes database migrations to establish proper relationships, authorization rules for joined datasets, and API model updates to handle dataset joins in available dataset contexts. Fetch data jobs for joined datasets remain unsupported and will be addressed in a separate MR.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage available datasets that are the result of joining multiple datasets, expanding the data composition capabilities for downstream consumption.

title: Consolidate alb stacks
summary: Changed the Hosted Zone parameter type from a specific reference to a String to allow CloudFormation template validation to pass when CREATE_ROUTE53_RECORDS is false. This resolves an issue where CloudFormation validates conditional parameters against their specified types even when they won't be used. The MR also consolidates the load balancer stack into a single template and adds logging of the load balancer DNS name for manual Route 53 record creation.
userImpact: Users can now deploy the consolidated load balancer stack with or without Route 53 record creation, and will receive the load balancer DNS address in logs for manual DNS configuration when needed.

summary: Upgraded Upsolve backend to 0.2.16 to fix bugs with custom dashboards. Updated Upsolve API key tenant configuration to use unique display names instead of the deprecated `key` field, and removed Grafana from the local development stack.

title: chore: add deployment configuration for auth to safely update
summary: Added ECS deployment configuration for the Auth service with minimum task count of 0 and maximum of 100 to ensure all task instances terminate gracefully together before launching a new batch during updates. This prevents incomplete deployments and ensures safe, coordinated upgrades of the Auth service.
userImpact: Auth service deployments now update gracefully without disrupting running instances mid-transition.

summary: Added ordering priority field to schema fields across all connector types to control the presentation order of connector fields in the UI. A database migration was added to persist these priorities, and the connector repository and API models were updated to support ordering fields based on their assigned priority values.
userImpact: Users can now see connector schema fields displayed in a consistent, intentional order when configuring connectors, improving the UX by surfacing the most relevant fields first.

title: UP-1674: auto-masking for column conflicts
summary: Implements automatic column masking to resolve naming conflicts in joined datasets. When joining multiple datasets, columns with the same name are now automatically aliased using a mask to ensure uniqueness. This prevents data loss and schema conflicts while maintaining the integrity of joined dataset operations.
userImpact: Users can now join datasets with overlapping column names without manual conflict resolution—columns are automatically masked to ensure unique identifiers across the joined dataset.

summary: Fixed deployment logic to correctly identify the main branch for AWS dev deployments in tag-driven jobs. Previously, the commit branch was not being set for tag-driven jobs, causing deployments to fail. The fix now explicitly checks if the source branch is origin/main.
userImpact: AWS dev deployments triggered by tags now correctly identify and deploy from the main branch.

title: chore(deps): update dependency croniter to v6
summary: Updated croniter dependency from v5.0.1 to v6.0.0. This major release includes improvements to timezone handling in timestamp conversion, makes datetime conversion methods public, fixes EPOCH calculation for non-UTC and 32-bit systems, and includes code quality enhancements.
userImpact: Croniter timestamp and datetime conversion utilities are now publicly available, and timezone handling has been improved for greater compatibility across different system configurations.

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.83
summary: Patch update to boto3 from v1.35.82 to v1.35.83. This release includes API updates for multiple AWS services including Backup Indexing, Batch configuration improvements, CloudFront enhancements, ECS fault injection support, and additional features across various AWS services.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency update maintains compatibility while providing access to latest AWS API features and improvements.

title: UP-1697 & UP-1875 axios regression dashboard
summary: Adds new regression and custom use-case dashboards with generalized charts for categorical column counts and numeric column distribution. Includes an onboarding script for the Axios demo dataset to automatically populate metrics. Fixes a bug in the categorical count metric where dimension names were incorrectly set to the column name instead of the constant 'column_name'.
userImpact: Users can now view regression model performance metrics (MAE, MSE, RMSE) and data distribution charts on dedicated dashboards, with automatic metric population for the Axios demo dataset.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1734: Rip out local fs connector
summary: Removed the local filesystem connector implementation from both the data plane and app plane, including the connector class, bootstrap schemas, and all related test fixtures. A database migration was added to safely delete any existing local_fs connectors from deployed instances using the connector API, which handles connector secrets and database cleanup properly.
userImpact: Users can no longer create or use local filesystem connectors; existing local_fs connectors will be automatically removed during the deployment migration. The ConnectorType constant will be removed in a follow-up PR after this deployment.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.81→1.35.82), cfn-lint (1.22.1→1.22.2), and pglast (6.11→6.13). These are routine patch and minor version updates to maintain dependency freshness and security posture.
userImpact: No direct user impact; routine dependency maintenance.

title: UP-1888 BAM demo data
summary: Added a new Mergers and Acquisitions demo dataset for BAM customers, complete with an onboarding script and metric configuration. Enhanced the data generation script to support varying the number of inferences per day, enabling more realistic demo scenarios.
userImpact: BAM customers can now onboard with a pre-configured M&A dataset that includes realistic data variation patterns for testing and demonstrations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Remove dbops part1
summary: Removes the former dbops endpoint from service configuration across ECS and CloudFormation resources. Replaces the previous bootstrap approach with an init container pattern for upsolve database bootstrap, simplifying deployment and removing deprecated environment variables.
userImpact: Database initialization now occurs automatically via init containers during service startup, eliminating the need for separate dbops endpoints and improving deployment consistency.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1791: Introduce minimal org member role
summary: A new minimal org member role has been introduced and is now automatically granted to users when they are created via the `post_organization_users` endpoint or sign in for the first time through SSO. This role provides basic permissions needed for users to view their own information and role bindings, establishing a default access level for all organization members.
userImpact: Users created in an organization now automatically receive a minimal org member role that allows them to see their own user information and understand what roles they have been assigned.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1689 - update model APIs to accept & return a list of dataset IDs
summary: Model API updated to support associations with multiple datasets instead of a single dataset. The change includes a new database join table linking Models to Datasets, updates to POST and PATCH endpoints to accept dataset ID lists, and aggregation logic that runs across all associated datasets during model onboarding.
userImpact: Users can now associate models with multiple datasets, enabling aggregated metrics and monitoring across dataset combinations rather than single datasets.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated dependencies to latest non-major versions: boto3 (1.35.80→1.35.81), moto (5.0.22→5.0.23), and uvicorn (0.32.1→0.34.0). These updates include AWS API improvements for CloudHSM, EC2, EKS, and other services, new Kafka service support in moto, and maintenance updates in uvicorn.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1893 fix schema inference and metrics calculation on cols with spaces
summary: Fixed schema inference and metrics calculation for nested columns containing spaces in their names, such as "MA History". Column names were not being properly quoted in SQL rendering, causing failures during schema inspection and aggregation jobs. The fix applies SQL escaping to all column references across the schema inferencer and all aggregation functions.
userImpact: Users can now successfully work with datasets that have column names containing spaces; schema inference and metric calculations no longer fail on such columns.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1890 fix raw data retrieval with nans
summary: Fixed raw data retrieval for Expel by encoding NaN values as strings in the fetch data executor. NaN is not valid JSON and causes parsing failures on the frontend; this change ensures NaN values are properly serialized and can be handled by the frontend after previous frontend support was merged.
userImpact: Raw data retrieval for Expel now succeeds when the source data contains NaN values.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1798: Support updating users
summary: Split the `name` field into separate `first_name` and `last_name` fields on user objects to align with keycloak's separate field storage and enable proper patch request support, since reliably splitting a single name field into components is not feasible. Also adds support for correctly setting the `picture` attribute in the database and updates keycloak scopes to include both name fields in user tokens.
userImpact: Users and integrations must now consume `first_name` and `last_name` fields instead of a single `name` field, and can now update user profile information including first name, last name, and picture attributes.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1581 upsolve custom chart docs
summary: Added comprehensive user-facing documentation for configuring custom charts in Upsolve, including step-by-step guides, SQL query examples, and visual screenshots. This documentation helps users understand how to create and configure custom charts within the dashboard system.
userImpact: Users can now access detailed guidance on configuring custom charts, including examples and visual references to understand the configuration process.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1896 fix binary classification dashboard
summary: The binary classification dashboard was omitting days with no data from graphs, causing non-contiguous date ranges and misaligned axes. This fix updates database queries to use the time_bucket_gapfill function to explicitly fill gaps with zero values, ensuring all days are represented. Additionally, styling adjustments prevent graph overflow on curved lines.
userImpact: Users now see complete, continuous time series on binary classification dashboards with zero-value days properly displayed, making data gaps explicit rather than hidden.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies including boto3, cfn-lint, pytest-asyncio, and authzed/spicedb to their latest patch and minor versions. These are routine dependency updates that maintain compatibility with the current codebase.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] move data plane health check to port 7492 to prevent conflict with auth on 8080
summary: The data plane health check service has been moved from port 8080 to port 7492 to avoid conflicts with keycloak authentication services running locally. This change affects CloudFormation templates, the health check implementation, and the Docker Compose configuration.
userImpact: Developers running the data plane locally can now use keycloak on port 8080 without conflicts from the health check service.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1891 upgrade datasketches 5.1.1 to use prebuilt wheels for python 3.13
summary: Upgraded datasketches from 5.1.0 to 5.1.1 to use prebuilt wheels for Python 3.13, eliminating the need for custom wheel builds. This simplifies the build process by allowing all major dependencies (duckdb, pyarrow, and datasketches) to use exclusively precompiled wheels.
userImpact: Users can now deploy on Python 3.13 without custom datasketches builds, reducing deployment time and complexity.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1892 fix bigquery connector
summary: Fixed BigQuery connector initialization that was incorrectly passing the full tuple returned by `load_credentials_from_dict` instead of extracting the credentials object. This was causing authentication failures when establishing BigQuery client connections.
userImpact: BigQuery connector now properly authenticates and initializes, allowing users to successfully connect to and query BigQuery datasets.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (patch), opentelemetry-exporter-otlp, and opentelemetry-sdk (both minor versions). These updates include AWS service API improvements and OpenTelemetry enhancements for observability.
userImpact: No direct user impact—routine dependency maintenance to stay current with patch and minor releases.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1794 fix orphaned scheduled jobs when models are deleted
summary: Fixed the model deletion logic to properly clean up associated scheduled jobs when a model is deleted. Moved the foreign key constraint for job schedules to the job_schedules table to enforce referential integrity and prevent orphaned schedules.
userImpact: Deleting a model now properly removes its scheduled jobs, and orphaned job schedules are prevented from occurring.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1799: Support reseting user credentials
summary: Added support for resetting user credentials in the authentication system. Users can now reset their credentials through new API endpoints that trigger credential rotation in the underlying authentication provider (Keycloak or static config). This includes updates to the user model, permissions, and authorization schema to enable this capability.
userImpact: Users can now reset their API credentials through the application, with the new credentials immediately usable for subsequent authentication requests.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1792: Lazily delete users
summary: Implemented lazy deletion of users to allow tokens to remain valid until their natural expiration after a user account is deleted. Previously, user deletion was immediate. A new deleted_users table tracks soft-deleted users, and authentication now checks this table to reject requests with valid tokens from deleted users, returning a 401 Unauthorized response.
userImpact: Users with valid authentication tokens can continue making API requests after their account is deleted until their token expires, at which point requests properly return 401 Unauthorized instead of failing immediately upon deletion.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Dependencies updated: boto3 (1.35.77 → 1.35.78) for AWS API improvements, and cfn-lint (1.21.0 → 1.22.0) for CloudFormation linting enhancements. These non-major updates bring minor feature additions and documentation improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact; routine dependency maintenance for bug fixes and API improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: feat: automatically create aws deployment tag and also a success tag
summary: Automated AWS deployment pipeline now creates a deployment tag when a new release is created from main, triggering automatic AWS Dev deployment. Upon successful AWS Dev deployment, a success tag is automatically created to track the last stable version for future scheduled AWS Prod deployments.
userImpact: Release deployments to AWS Dev are now fully automated with success tracking, enabling reliable promotion of tested versions to production.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: automatically bump the patch version if no release version is specified
summary: The release process now automatically bumps the patch version when no explicit release version is specified. This simplifies patch releases by removing the need to manually specify a version number, streamlining the deployment workflow.
userImpact: Users can now trigger patch releases without specifying a version, and the system will automatically increment the patch version.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore: rename expel_sts to expel-sts-platform
summary: Renamed the deployment environment and pipeline commands from `expel_sts` to `expel-sts-platform` for naming consistency across environments and deployment processes. The underlying stack names remain unchanged—this is purely a rename of the deployment folder and pipeline tag names.
userImpact: Deployment commands and pipeline references for the expel-sts platform now use the consistent `expel-sts-platform` naming convention.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore: rename aws dev platform_v4 to aws-dev-platform to match aws-prod-platform
summary: Renamed the AWS development platform from `platform_v4` to `aws-dev-platform` to establish consistent naming conventions across deployment environments. This change aligns the dev platform naming with the existing `aws-prod-platform` convention, simplifying platform identification and reducing confusion in deployment scripts and configuration.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1792: Fix db manager deletion bug
summary: Fixed a critical issue where user deletion failed to properly remove relationships in SpiceDB. The problem occurred because `db_manager.flush()` would empty the SpiceDB delete queue when it held references to SQLAlchemy objects that had been moved to the transaction buffer. The fix enqueues deep copies of deleted objects to the delete queues instead of the objects themselves, ensuring deletions remain in the queue through flush operations.
userImpact: Users and group members can now be correctly deleted with all their associated relationships removed from SpiceDB, resolving previous failures in the user deletion flow.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies across the project, including boto3 (1.35.76 → 1.35.77), cfn-lint (1.20.2 → 1.21.0), and authzed/spicedb (1.38.1 → 1.39.0). These are routine dependency maintenance updates to pull in the latest bug fixes and minor feature improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1881 fix runaway scheduled jobs
summary: Fixed runaway scheduled jobs that were retrying indefinitely for deleted models by adding a mechanism for jobs to stop themselves when encountering non-retriable errors. A new PATCH API endpoint allows updating a job's max retries, and the scheduled job executor now sets a job's max_attempts to the current attempt when an unrecoverable error is encountered, preventing infinite retry loops.
userImpact: Users will no longer experience API crashes from jobs with millions of retry attempts for deleted models, as jobs now gracefully terminate when encountering unrecoverable errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1880 fix app plane health check
summary: A previous MR incorrectly configured the app plane health check port in the scope-dev cluster CloudFormation template. This fix corrects the port configuration in the arthur-scope-app-plane-on-ecs.yml CloudFormation template to ensure proper health checks for the app plane.
userImpact: App plane health checks now work correctly in the scope-dev cluster, preventing false failure states due to port misconfiguration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1880 data plane health check
summary: Fixed a race condition in the data plane that could cause the process to deadlock and prevent ECS from detecting unhealthy instances and restarting them. The fix adds a health check endpoint, wraps API calls in exception handlers to prevent crashes, and marks JobRunner threads as daemon threads so the process exits cleanly if the main thread crashes.
userImpact: ECS can now properly detect when the data plane main loop is unresponsive and automatically restart the instance, preventing indefinite hangs and improving system reliability.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve to enable user chart creation
summary: Upgraded Upsolve to version 0.2.14 to enable user chart creation. The upstream SQL changes required explicit start and end parameter handling for the `time_bucket_gapfill` function due to timezone manipulation in queries that prevents automatic bound inference. Updated the function calls to pass `{{dateStart}}` and `{{dateEnd}}` parameters explicitly.
userImpact: Users can now create custom charts as previously broken functionality has been restored through the Upsolve upgrade and SQL parameter adjustments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1696: support model problem type and other tag updates
summary: Added support for the `model_problem_type` field across datasets and models, including database schema migration, API model updates, and aggregation matcher enhancements to handle the new tag. The changes enable proper tagging and filtering of models based on problem type, with coordinated updates to the onboarding script and test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now tag models with a problem type and view problem types associated with their models and datasets through the API.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including Mako (1.3.7 → 1.3.8), httpx (0.28.0 → 0.28.1), and NumPy (2.1.3 → 2.2.0). This ensures the project benefits from the latest patch fixes, including an httpx SSL fix, while staying current with minor version releases.

title: UP-1858: Support fetch data on available datasets

userImpact: Users can now fetch raw data from available datasets, supporting manual dataset onboarding flows for connectors and allowing verification of correct data selection.

title: UP-1823 add dataset locator to connector product docs

This enables operators to visualize key metrics and system health across the platform infrastructure.

title: UP-1866 build improvements
summary: Refactored Docker build processes to use Debian slim base image instead of Alpine, enabling native ARM architecture support and eliminating C++ compilation requirements. Removed unnecessary dependencies from scope_common (pyarrow, read_file tool) and fixed the OpenAPI client generation script to fail gracefully on errors. These changes reduce build times from 20+ minutes to a few minutes on both x86 and ARM architectures while maintaining or slightly increasing image sizes.
userImpact: Users can now build and run the Docker Compose stack natively on ARM machines without platform specification, with significantly faster build times (2-4 minutes vs 20+ minutes) and improved reliability of CI/CD build processes.

title: UP-1856: support datasets default model page view
summary: Added support for datasets default model page view by extending the datasets API to include model information in responses. The changes update API models, internal models, repositories, and routers to enable the new view while removing the connector_id field from dataset response objects to simplify the API contract.

Changes include adding discriminators to JobSpec types for proper deserialization, making connector code resilient to virtual datasets without locators, fixing import statements to use correct type references, removing unsafe access patterns, and upgrading the OpenAPI generator version to support the discriminator functionality.

title: Add some documentation around aggregations and annotations
summary: Added comprehensive documentation explaining aggregations and annotations in the dataplane, covering key concepts, examples, and how they interact with datasets and metrics specifications. The documentation provides clarity on dataset schemas, aggregation functions, and their role in the platform architecture.

title: [no-ref] add health check for spicedb
summary: Added health check for SpiceDB service across CloudFormation templates and Docker Compose configuration. The previous implementation using CMD-SHELL failed because the SpiceDB image lacks a shell; this version uses a command-based health check that works with the SpiceDB container image.

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: UP-1680 - Make GET jobs endpoint faster
summary: Fixed an n+1 query problem in the GET jobs endpoint that was caused by SQLAlchemy's simple selectinload not properly eager-loading polymorphic job spec subtypes. This required switching to selectin_polymorphic to load both base and derived columns in a single query, reducing query count from 500-1000ms to near-instant.
userImpact: The project activity logs now load significantly faster, with response times reduced from 500-1000ms to near-instant.
author: peter496

title: UP-1680 - Further reduce role binding api endpoint latencies
summary: Optimized role binding API endpoint latencies by extending previous performance fixes to all GET operations. The changes reduce redundant joins and database queries for role binding retrieval, particularly benefiting large organizations with significant group sizes and user counts. Tracing shows approximately 50% reduction in end-to-end request times (8s → 4s in dev environment), with production improvements expected to reach sub-300ms from current ~6s baselines.
userImpact: Role binding API endpoints now respond significantly faster, improving overall API responsiveness for organizations at scale.
author: peter496

title: Revert "[no-ref] add health check for spicedb"
summary: Reverted a previously added health check for SpiceDB in CloudFormation templates. This removes the health check configuration that was added in a prior commit.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: [no-ref] add health check for spicedb
summary: Added ECS health checks for spiceDB to ensure proper service availability monitoring. The health check uses grpc_health_probe to verify the service is in SERVING status on port 50051, improving deployment reliability and service observability.
userImpact: spiceDB service health is now monitored by ECS, enabling automatic detection and recovery of service failures.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1566 add script to build sdk wheel

title: UP-1693: axios sample dataset
summary: Added support for generating sample datasets from multiple sources, including a new axios dataset generator alongside the existing expel dataset. The refactored dataset generation system provides a unified interface with configurable output destinations (BigQuery, S3, local directory) and deterministic prediction ranges based on datetime patterns for reliable integration testing.
userImpact: Users can now generate both expel and axios sample datasets for testing and demo purposes, with flexible output options and predictable data patterns for validating connector functionality.

title: NO-REF - Update expel data to be less skewed
summary: Updated the expel test data generation to incorporate hourly variation in addition to daily variation when generating prediction ranges. Previously, data generation was based only on the day, resulting in entire days falling into a single class after thresholding. This change produces more balanced and representative test data by varying predictions across hours within each day.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Integration test data is now more evenly distributed across predicted classes, improving test coverage quality.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies across the codebase, including patch updates to boto3, opentelemetry packages, and opentelemetry-sdk, along with minor version bumps for cfn-lint and pytest-httpx. These updates bring in bug fixes and minor enhancements from upstream libraries without introducing breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1566 Expel Onboarding scripts
summary: Refactored SDK authentication to support two ArthurAPISession types: one for client credentials and one for user+password browser-based authentication. Added new session implementations with automatic token refresh and thread-safe concurrent access. Also added an onboarding script for Expel models that demonstrates how to configure connectors and ingest data, and fixed keycloak CLI client configuration to include the basic mapper required by Keycloak v25.
userImpact: Users and customers can now authenticate via client credentials or browser-based user credentials, with automatic token refresh and safe concurrent usage; Expel can reference the onboarding script as documentation for their model setup process.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1819 Fix schema inference for timestamp types, and add auto tagging for primary timestamp
summary: Fixed schema inference for DuckDB TIMESTAMP types by mapping them to Arthur's schema types. Additionally implemented automatic detection and tagging of singular timestamp columns as PRIMARY_TIMESTAMP to improve the user onboarding experience.
userImpact: Schema inference now successfully handles timestamp columns from DuckDB data sources, and users no longer need to manually tag primary timestamp columns.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1695 - Add MAE aggregation function
summary: Added Mean Absolute Error (MAE) aggregation function for regression metrics. The implementation sums absolute errors and point counts over 5-minute intervals, reporting both as separate metrics with aligned timestamps. At query time, these can be combined into daily/hourly buckets using SUM(ae) / SUM(n).
userImpact: Users can now compute and track mean absolute error regression metrics as part of their aggregation pipeline, enabling error analysis across configurable time buckets.
author: peter496

title: UP-1819 fix bucket based schema inspection
summary: Fixed bucket-based connectors (S3, GCS) failing during schema inspection when datasets lack a primary timestamp tag. The logic now only requires the timestamp tag when pagination options are not configured, matching the behavior of the BigQuery connector. All integration tests were updated to use schema inference instead of hard-coded schemas.
userImpact: Users can now successfully read and inspect datasets from bucket-based connectors without requiring a primary timestamp tag, as long as pagination options are configured.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1730: connectors tech readme & tech debt
summary: Updated technical documentation for connectors with improved schema examples and fixed incorrect documentation claims about bucket naming. Added a database migration to correct connector locator field definitions, addressing technical debt in the storage and repository layers.
userImpact: No direct user impact

These updates bring minor enhancements, bug fixes, and API changes from upstream packages, keeping the project current with security and compatibility improvements.

userImpact: Dashboard charts now display y-axis labels correctly, making it easier to read and interpret chart values.

title: UP-1729 - Add get_jobs parameters for server side ready_before/ready_after timestamp generation
summary: Added parameters to the get_jobs endpoint to enable server-side generation of ready_before and ready_after timestamps. Previously, clients had to compute these timestamps locally; this change shifts that responsibility to the server, simplifying client implementations and improving consistency.
userImpact: Users can now pass timestamp generation parameters to get_jobs and let the server generate ready_before/ready_after values, eliminating the need for client-side timestamp computation.

title: no-ref set graceful termination for data plane in docker compose
summary: Updated docker-compose configuration to enable graceful termination for the data plane service. This allows the data plane to exit cleanly and restart jobs properly when running in Docker Compose environments, improving reliability and job handling during container lifecycle operations.
userImpact: Data plane services now gracefully shut down and restart jobs when running in Docker Compose, reducing potential job loss or corruption during container restarts.

title: [no-ref] query doc callouts

title: UP-1702 - Refactor aggregations to take a dataset reference as argument rather...
summary: Refactored aggregations throughout the codebase to accept a DatasetReference object as an argument instead of an encoded table name string. At metrics calculation time, the dataset id is converted to a DatasetReference containing the dataset name, id, and table name for lookups. A database migration handles converting existing metric configurations from the old format.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Internal refactoring improves data structure clarity and makes dataset information more accessible to metrics calculations.

title: NO-REF - Reuse threadpool per day
summary: Optimized threadpool reuse in bucket-based connector by reusing the same threadpool across multiple daily time increments instead of reinitializing it each time. This reduces unnecessary resource allocation and improves performance during batch processing operations.
userImpact: Data processing operations using the bucket-based connector will have improved performance and reduced resource overhead during multi-day batch operations.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: UP-1754 - Break connector pagination options off from filters
summary: Separated pagination options from filter parameters in connector read operations. Pagination parameters (page, page_size, limit) are now passed as a dedicated `ConnectorPaginationOptions` argument rather than mixed into the filters, eliminating duplicate parsing logic in connectors. Pagination is now required for fetch data jobs, and default page_size increased from 15 to 25 with a maximum limit of 500.
userImpact: Connectors now receive clearer, separated pagination configuration, improving consistency and reliability of paginated data retrieval across all connector implementations.
author: peter496

title: UP-1755 - Add inference counts by class aggregation functions
summary: Adds two new aggregation functions for computing inference counts by class: BinaryClassifierCountByClassAggregationFunction for counting predictions from binary classifier columns, and BinaryClassifierCountThresholdAggregationFunction for deriving counts from float columns using configurable thresholds. These functions enable detailed classification performance analysis by class.
userImpact: Users can now aggregate inference counts broken down by prediction class for binary classifiers, supporting both direct predictions and threshold-based classification from continuous scores.

This enables deploying Arthur Scope stacks to the Expel AWS environment with proper IAM role assumption and configuration management across core, bootstrap, and data plane components.

title: UP-1704 - Convert get_jobs state filter to states to allow IN filter
summary: The `get_jobs` state filter has been converted from a single-value equality filter to a multi-value `IN` filter, enabling users to query jobs by multiple states at once. Previously, users could only filter by `state == QUEUED`; now they can use `state IN (QUEUED, RUNNING)` or similar multi-state queries. This is a breaking change for frontend clients using the old filter syntax.
userImpact: Users can now filter job queries by multiple states simultaneously using the IN operator, but existing code using the old `state ==` syntax will need to be updated.
author: peter496

title: UP-1680 - Optimize role bindings respository functions
summary: Optimized role bindings repository queries to reduce SQL complexity and improve performance. Changes include strategic use of selectinload() to eager-load related objects and explicit limit(1) on single-record queries to avoid unnecessary data fetching from the database.
userImpact: Users will experience faster response times when fetching role bindings, as the optimized queries reduce database query complexity and data transfer overhead.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.56

summary: Implemented raw data retrieval for bucket-based connectors by refactoring the data retrieval pipeline to support direct data access from cloud storage. The changes simplify the retrieval operator logic and remove connector-specific enum handling, enabling a more generic approach to bucket-based data sources.

title: UP-1560: support pagination for bucket-based connectors

The implementation uses post-filtering on paginated file reads, balancing memory efficiency with reasonable performance characteristics.
userImpact: Users can now paginate through large inference result sets from bucket-based connectors without memory constraints, enabling more efficient queries on large datasets.

title: [no-ref] Upgrade pglast 6.9
summary: Upgraded pglast to version 6.9, which includes a fix for a breaking format change from the previous version. This upgrade reverts test changes that were previously necessary to work around the breaking format change in pglast.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest pglast bug fixes and improved SQL parsing stability.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies to their latest patch versions: boto3 (1.35.51 → 1.35.52) and cfn-lint (1.18.2 → 1.18.3). The boto3 update includes API changes for AppSync Event APIs, DataSync Enhanced mode, VPC Security Groups enhancements, ECS service deployments, and new Amazon Location services (Maps, Places, Routes), along with other minor AWS service updates.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1530: S3 connector support
summary: Adds support for S3 and GCS bucket connectors, enabling users to connect to and read data from S3 and Google Cloud Storage buckets. The implementation includes schema definitions for bucket-based connectors, connection validation, and efficient file reading capabilities that reduced data ingestion time from 45 minutes to 6 minutes in testing.
userImpact: Users can now configure S3 and GCS bucket connectors, validate connectivity, and read datasets stored in cloud storage buckets with significantly improved performance.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1657 - Add log emitted by job agent for reporting job failure
summary: The job agent now logs an error message to jobs when it terminates them during shutdown, providing visibility into why a job failed. This is particularly useful for hardware preemption events where jobs are stopped due to external infrastructure constraints.
userImpact: Users can now see explicit error logs in their job output explaining when and why the job agent stopped their job, such as due to hardware preemption events.
author: peter496

title: UP-1599 - Handle SpiceDB writes in batch fashion
summary: Refactored SpiceDB write operations to use batch calls instead of individual writes across creates, updates, and deletes. This improves performance by reducing the number of individual API calls to SpiceDB and centralizes the batching logic as a reusable function within the DBManager class.
userImpact: SpiceDB authorization operations now execute more efficiently through batched writes, improving overall system performance during bulk data operations.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies: pglast from 6.2 to 6.8, protobuf from 5.28.2 to 5.28.3, and pydantic_core from 2.25.0 to 2.25.1. The pglast update required adjustments to SQL parser tests due to formatting changes in version 6.7.
userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes and improvements in pydantic-core's validation system, including support for subclassing ValidationError and improved URL handling, plus upstream fixes from protobuf and pglast.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency types-croniter to v4
summary: Updated the types-croniter type stub dependency from v3.0.3.20240731 to v4.0.0.20241030. This is a major version bump of the type stubs for the croniter library, providing updated type hints for scheduling operations.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Type stubs are used for development tooling and IDE support only.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency croniter to v5
summary: Updated croniter dependency from version 4.0.0 to 5.0.1. This major version upgrade includes support for reintroducing 7 as a valid day-of-week value in cron expressions, aligning with community requests while deviating from strict standard cron behavior.
userImpact: Users can now use 7 as a day-of-week value (Sunday) in cron expressions, providing more flexibility in scheduling configurations.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated 24 non-major dependencies across the project to their latest patch versions. This includes security patches and bug fixes for critical packages like SQLAlchemy, PyYAML, FastAPI, and boto3, along with updates to testing and development dependencies.
userImpact: Users benefit from security patches, bug fixes, and performance improvements in upstream dependencies without breaking changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1718 upgrade auth stack to keycloak 25
summary: Upgraded authentication stack to Keycloak 25 by updating the python-keycloak library from 3.0 to 4.0 and fixing breaking API changes. Added a user profile schema to manage the `user_type` attribute in Keycloak's admin UI, and refactored user update logic to comply with Keycloak 25's PUT semantics for the user update endpoint.
userImpact: Users can now leverage Keycloak 25's latest features and security improvements, with proper management of user type attributes visible in the Keycloak admin UI.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1554 - Add POST available_dataset endpoint
summary: This MR adds a new POST available_dataset endpoint that allows users to manually onboard available datasets on a per-connector basis, instead of relying solely on the existing method of wiping and repopulating all datasets. The implementation includes proper authorization controls, schema configuration updates, and integration tests. Additionally, it removes an unused ID field from the PutAvailableDataset model that the API never honored.
userImpact: Users can now manually onboard individual available datasets for connectors where bulk repopulation is not applicable, providing more flexible dataset management.
author: peter496

title: UP-465 - Add true/false flag to available datasets, datasets, models to...
summary: Added a boolean `arthur_shield_dataset` flag to available datasets, datasets, and models to indicate whether a dataset is an Arthur Shield dataset. The Shield connector now filters to output only datasets marked as Shield datasets, and at model creation time, the flag is derived from the model's dataset and set permanently.
userImpact: Users can now identify which datasets and models are Arthur Shield datasets through the new `arthur_shield_dataset` flag, with the UI automatically handling this information via existing type casting logic.
author: peter496

title: Revert "Bumped version for upsolve to 8035 in scope-version-manifest.json"
summary: Reverted a previous version bump of upsolve to 8035 due to missing migrations in that image. This rollback restores a stable version with all required database migrations in place.
userImpact: Users will receive the correct upsolve version with all necessary migrations included, preventing data consistency issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve
summary: Updated the upsolve service to the latest backend release by upgrading the Docker image version. This brings in the latest bug fixes, performance improvements, and features from the upstream upsolve project.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest upsolve backend improvements and bug fixes without any manual action required.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] lock dependencies to prevent ambiguity now that we have renovate bot
summary: Pinned all project dependencies to explicit versions across requirements files and setup.py to eliminate version ambiguity between local development and CI/CD environments. This prevents the issue where different dependency versions were being used in different contexts, now that Renovate Bot is in place to automatically manage updates.
userImpact: Developers and CI/CD systems now use consistent dependency versions, reducing environment-specific issues and build failures caused by version mismatches.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] add docs for docker login in readme setup
summary: Added documentation for Docker authentication in the setup instructions. Developers now need to authenticate their Docker daemon with the Arthur Docker 1Password user to use the Upsolve container dependency.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Developers setting up the project locally will need to follow the new Docker login instructions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency croniter to v4
summary: Updated croniter dependency from v3.0.3 to v4.0.0. This major version includes a fix for day-of-week range calculations when the last day is a Sunday, and removes the non-standard DayOfWeek alias 7 mapping to align with standard cron specifications.
userImpact: Cron scheduling now correctly follows standard cron specifications for day-of-week values and accurately calculates date ranges when Sunday is involved.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated pytest-httpx from 0.32.0 to 0.33.0. This minor release adds support for Python 3.13, introduces a new `should_mock` option for conditional request mocking, and adds `match_files`, `match_data`, and `match_extensions` parameters for more flexible request matching on multipart bodies and extensions.
userImpact: Developers can now use Python 3.13 with pytest-httpx and have access to more granular request matching options including conditional mocking via the `should_mock` callable.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1538 - Expel Binary Classification Performance Metrics
summary: This MR introduces confusion matrix aggregation functions to compute binary classification performance metrics (true positives, true negatives, false positives, false negatives). The implementation supports three common input patterns: bool/int predictions vs ground truth, string labels with manual true/false parameters, and float predictions with a configurable threshold. This enables customers to calculate standard performance metrics on their model predictions without custom engineering.
userImpact: Users can now compute confusion matrix metrics (TP/TN/FP/FN counts) directly on their prediction and ground truth data using built-in aggregation functions for boolean, string, and threshold-based classification scenarios.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated redis-py dependency from 5.1.1 to 5.2.0. This minor version includes a new scorer argument for AggregateRequest and maintenance fixes including a pinned pytest-profiling version to resolve a known bug.
userImpact: Users can now leverage the extended AggregateRequest with the new scorer argument for more flexible search aggregation queries.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] correct dataset onboarding flow
summary: The dataset onboarding flow was missing a critical step to fetch raw data for manually created datasets. This prevented users from confirming their dataset was configured correctly. The fix restores the data-fetching step in the onboarding workflow.
userImpact: Users can now view sample raw data during manual dataset onboarding to verify their configuration is correct.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1593: move data retrieval to dataset level
summary: Moved data retrieval operations and fetch data job specs from running at the model level to the dataset level. This includes a database migration to support dataset-level data retrieval, SpiceDB authorization schema updates to manage dataset retrieval permissions, and refactored repositories and operators to query and manage data retrieval at the dataset scope.
userImpact: Data retrieval operations now operate at the dataset level rather than the model level, requiring frontend updates to reflect the new data retrieval hierarchy and permissions model.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1679 - Properly convey exit code from dataplane processes
summary: JobExecutor previously always exited with code 0 because job try/catch wrapping masked actual process status. The job runner now properly wraps the executor to convey the actual exit code from dataplane processes, enabling proper failure detection by calling processes.
userImpact: Dataplane job processes now exit with the correct status code, allowing parent processes to properly detect job failures instead of always seeing success.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Revert FE image to pre-localization bug
summary: Reverted frontend image to a pre-localization version to address a bug discovered after localization changes. This rollback restores previous functionality for the demo scheduled later today.
userImpact: Frontend localization bug is resolved by reverting to the previous stable image version.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated mypy from version 1.12.0 to 1.13.0, a minor version update. This is a routine dependency maintenance change managed by Renovate Bot to keep development tooling current.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1412 full onboarding diagram
summary: Added a comprehensive workflow diagram documenting the complete resource onboarding process, including support for reusing existing resources and modular step design. The diagram covers current onboarding capabilities and planned additions for the tabular MVP (non-listable data sources, joined/multiple datasets, dataset schema editing, and metrics config editing).
userImpact: No direct user impact — this is a technical documentation addition to clarify onboarding workflow architecture for the development team.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1538 - Change aggregation function contract to emit more than one metric
summary: Aggregation functions now emit multiple metrics per function output instead of a single metric, enabling support for multi-metric aggregations like confusion matrices that produce 4+ outputs (TP, FP, TN, FN, etc.) from a single calculation. All aggregation function implementations have been updated to return a set of metrics, and the aggregator contract has been redesigned to support this capability.
userImpact: Users can now use aggregation functions that produce multiple related metrics in a single operation, such as confusion matrix aggregations that report true positives, false positives, true negatives, and false negatives simultaneously.
author: peter496

title: Revert FE image to yesterday morning's build
summary: Reverted the frontend image to a previous build from yesterday morning to address an issue ahead of a demo. This change bumps the FE version back to before a specific commit that introduced a problem.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies to their latest minor versions: Faker (30.6.0 → 30.8.0) with improved UK Ukrainian locale support, cfn-lint (1.17.1 → 1.18.1) for CloudFormation linting enhancements, and types-psutil (6.0.0.20240901 → 6.1.0.20241022) for improved type hints. These updates bring bug fixes, new features, and improved locale support to the project's tooling.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved data generation capabilities, enhanced CloudFormation template validation, and better type checking support.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1650 - Reduce fetch data memory requirements for improved latency
summary: Reduced memory requirements for data retrieval jobs to fit within thread execution thresholds instead of requiring new process creation. This eliminates 4-6 seconds of latency overhead between when jobs are returned and when they begin executing on the dataplane, improving responsiveness for latency-sensitive data operations.
userImpact: Data retrieval jobs now execute with significantly reduced latency (4-6 seconds faster) as they can run within existing threads instead of requiring new process overhead.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF - Bump memory too
summary: Increased Fargate memory allocation from 4GB to 8GB to maintain the required CPU-to-memory ratio of 4:8 on AWS EKS. A previous change had increased CPU without updating memory, violating Fargate's pod configuration constraints.
userImpact: Fargate pods now have the correct memory allocation and meet AWS EKS requirements for pod configuration.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Temporarily up dataplane resources
summary: Temporarily increased dataplane CPU resources to investigate and potentially improve latency for certain jobs. This is a temporary change to the dev environment parameters to diagnose performance issues.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1513 alerts improvements
summary: Added technical documentation for alerting and metrics improvements, including visual diagrams explaining current drawbacks and proposing solutions. The documentation covers metric preaggregation strategies, bucket reconciliation approaches, and system design considerations for handling multiple metric versions across time ranges.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: XP-1145 - Update Shield connector configs to use query optimization results
summary: Updated Shield connector configurations to leverage query optimizations made in the Shield API, including disabling count queries and increasing the default page size to 1500. These changes significantly reduce job execution time by decreasing API requests and network overhead while maintaining responsiveness.
userImpact: Users will experience substantially faster Shield connector data retrieval, with observed throughput improvements reducing typical job execution times by approximately 44%.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updates fsspec (filesystem_spec) from 2024.9.0 to 2024.10.0, a minor version bump of a non-major dependency. This routine dependency update keeps the filesystem access library current with the latest stable release.
userImpact: Users benefit from fsspec improvements and bug fixes included in the 2024.10.0 release.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Add 5 minute validation to lookback period
summary: Added validation to enforce 5-minute alignment on the lookback period for model schedules, complementing earlier alignment work on cron frequencies. This ensures the lookback period (which defines the data read start time) respects the same 5-minute boundary requirements as the cron schedule itself.
userImpact: Invalid lookback periods that don't align to 5-minute boundaries will now be rejected, preventing data inconsistencies in model scheduling.
author: peter496

title: UP-1590 - Small refactors to scheduled jobs, metrics calculation executors, add some tests
summary: Refactored scheduled jobs and metrics calculation executor classes to improve testability and maintainability. Added comprehensive unit tests for both executors, fixed job scheduling logic including correct lookback_period_seconds calculations, and improved logging to preserve aggregation names for troubleshooting.
userImpact: No direct user impact—internal code improvements and test coverage enhancements for maintainability.
author: peter496

title: UP-1513 fix grafana query for toxicity latency
summary: Fixed the Grafana query for Toxicity Rule Median latency to properly merge sketches before calculating the median value. This ensures accurate latency reporting in the dashboard visualization.
userImpact: Toxicity rule median latency metrics now display correctly in the Grafana dashboard.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1617: Update job create logic to have queued time match ready time for future jobs
summary: Scheduled jobs are now timestamped with their ready time (when they will actually execute) instead of their creation time, preventing them from appearing out of order in the job list. This resolves a frontend display issue where queued jobs scheduled for future execution were incorrectly positioned below jobs that run at earlier times.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs now display in chronological order by their actual execution time rather than creation time, improving job list organization and clarity.
author: peter496

title: UP-1538 - Refactor aggregation parameter parsing, definition, api models
summary: Refactored aggregation parameter parsing by splitting the monolithic ScopeMetricParameter object into separate type-specific definitions for datasets, column references, literals, and group-by dimensions. This improves the schema clarity and maintainability for both backend processing and frontend consumption. No aggregation function names or values changed, and no data migration is required.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Internal restructuring of metrics parameter definitions to support cleaner API and frontend development.
author: peter496

title: UP-1609 fix upsolve dash
summary: Fixed the Upsolve dashboard by upgrading dashboards and applying a new Upsolve export to resolve toxicity and latency graph display issues. This addresses problems with data visualization accuracy in the dashboard metrics.
userImpact: Users can now see accurate toxicity and latency graphs in the Upsolve dashboard without display errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1609 fix upsolve dash
summary: Fixed an issue in the Upsolve dashboard by deploying a new database patch (upsolve-patch-2024101718351.ucf). This patch addresses problems with toxicity and latency graph rendering that were affecting dashboard accuracy and user visibility.
userImpact: Upsolve dashboard toxicity and latency graphs now display correctly.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: Change cron validation logic
summary: Fixed cron expression validation logic to correctly validate that recurring job intervals are at least 5 minutes apart and aligned to 5-minute boundaries. The previous logic incorrectly validated expressions like `*/7` which run on the 7th minute of each hour rather than every 7 minutes, causing false validation failures.
userImpact: Cron schedule validation now correctly accepts intervals that are evenly divisible by 5 minutes and properly aligned, preventing invalid rejections of legitimate schedules.
author: peter496

title: UP-1609 bump upsolve image
summary: Updated the upsolve Docker image to the latest version. This ensures the service uses the most recent image with any available improvements or security updates.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: feat: new stack deployments
summary: This MR introduces automated deployment infrastructure and CI jobs for new stack deployments and on-premises installations. It adds comprehensive CloudFormation templates for core infrastructure, databases, and application services (auth, SpiceDB, Upsolve, data plane, frontend, Grafana), along with shell scripts to orchestrate deployment and database creation. This enables customers to deploy complete Scope environments through an automated pipeline.
userImpact: Customers can now automatically deploy new Scope stacks on AWS with all required infrastructure components (compute, database, load balancing, secrets) using provided deployment scripts and configuration files.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies to latest available versions: Faker (~30.6.0), cfn-lint (~1.17.1), and psutil (~6.1.0). These updates include locale support improvements for Faker and performance enhancements for psutil's process iteration.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved performance when iterating over processes on Windows and expanded locale support for data generation.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Add back dataplane stack
summary: The dataplane stack configuration was restored in the CloudFormation root scope template. This change re-enables the dataplane infrastructure component that was previously removed.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Recreate Dataplane stack
summary: The Dataplane CloudFormation stack was stuck referencing a non-existent autoscaling policy, preventing deployments. This change temporarily removes the Dataplane stack and its output references from the CloudFormation root configuration, allowing CloudFormation to delete the stuck stack and redeploy it fresh.
userImpact: CloudFormation deployments will no longer fail due to the orphaned Dataplane autoscaling policy reference, allowing the stack to be cleanly recreated.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Up autoscaling thresholds
summary: Increased CPU autoscaling threshold from 10% to 25% on the dataplane to reduce aggressive scaling behavior during periodic job batches. With recent parallelism improvements, the lower threshold was causing unnecessary scale-up cycles when sequential job batches arrived within the scale-in cooldown period, pushing worker counts beyond the configured maximum.
userImpact: Dataplane autoscaling now responds more efficiently to sustained load spikes, reducing unnecessary worker churn and staying within configured capacity limits during scheduled job batches.
author: peter496

title: Revert "UP-1609 update timescale connection type"
summary: Reverts the timescale connection type update from UP-1609 per team discussion. The change restores the previous connection configuration to align with current requirements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw
