title: [no-ref] add policy resource to upsolve token API
summary: The Upsolve token API now supports "policy" as a resource type alongside existing resource types. When requested, the API returns a token containing all model IDs that a user can access which have the specified policy assigned, enabling support for the policy overview dashboard.

title: [no-ref] export policy dashboard

title: add a new log group for audit logs in cloudwatch
summary: Adds a new CloudWatch log group in the Scope deployment to support the new audit log system. The log group is configured with a Retain deletion policy to preserve audit logs for 365 days even after stack teardown, ensuring audit trail integrity.

title: feat: split compliance rule ID into policy rule ID and materialized rule ID
summary: Split `ComplianceRuleSummary.id` into two fields: `id` now returns the policy rule definition ID, while new `materialized_rule_id` returns the materialized alert rule ID (null for attestation rules). The change resolves via a join on `DBComplianceRuleHistory` and requires no schema migration, with test data updated to match production behavior.
userImpact: Users can now distinguish between policy rule definition IDs and their materialized alert rule counterparts via the new `materialized_rule_id` field, enabling accurate rule fetching from the alert rules API.
author: videetparekh1995

title: feat: policy compliance history endpoint
summary: Introduces a new `GET /v1/policies/{policy_id}/compliance_history` endpoint with infinite pagination to retrieve the full rule compliance evaluation history. Includes a reusable `list_compliance_history` repository method with flexible list-based filters, adds the `POLICY_LIST_COMPLIANCE_HISTORY` permission to SpiceDB schema and roles, and makes SpiceDB connection configurable via environment variables.
userImpact: Users can now query the complete compliance history for a policy to audit rule evaluations over time, with support for flexible filtering by policy, assignment, model, and rule IDs.

title: feat: add workspace compliance overview endpoint (UP-4155)
summary: Adds a new workspace compliance overview endpoint (`GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/compliance`) that provides a flattened, queryable compliance status table across all policies in a workspace. The implementation includes a normalized `compliance_rule_statuses` database table backed by an Alembic migration and dual-write synchronization in `set_compliance_status`, plus a new `WORKSPACE_LIST_POLICY_COMPLIANCE` permission with SpiceDB schema integration.
userImpact: Users can now query comprehensive compliance status across all rules and models in a workspace with filtering by status, policy, model, rule type, rule ID, and frequency, plus sorting and pagination support.

title: create audit logging middleware
summary: Adds audit logging middleware that captures and logs HTTP request details including the endpoint path, authenticated user, path parameters (resource IDs), and response IDs. The middleware integrates with FastAPI's request/response pipeline and exposes configurable options via environment variables to allow operators to enable/disable audit logging as needed.
userImpact: Users and operators can now track which users accessed which API endpoints and what resources were modified, with the ability to configure audit logging behavior through environment variables.

title: Add package management info
summary: Added package management documentation to CLAUDE.md, including information about using uv for Python dependency and environment management. This provides developers with guidance on managing project dependencies in a standardized way.

title: Enforce 3-day package minimum for uv dependency resolution
summary: Enforces a 3-day package minimum for uv dependency resolution in pyproject.toml configurations. This ensures that package dependencies are resolved with a minimum age threshold, improving dependency stability and reducing the risk of pulling in very recently published packages.
userImpact: Dependency resolution now respects a 3-day minimum package age, providing more stable and vetted dependency versions.

The design includes specifications for resource ID extraction from request paths, handling of user identifiers across different endpoint types, and versioned audit log event schema to form a stable API contract for downstream consumers.

Docker Compose now parameterizes host-mapped ports for postgres, timescale, and app-plane services, enabling multiple stacks to run simultaneously without port collisions.

summary: Added project_id field to the policy assignments API response. This allows clients to identify which project a policy assignment belongs to directly from the response, without requiring additional lookups or context.

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates applied via Renovate Bot, including patch updates to authzed, boto3, requests, and timescaledb, and minor version updates to faker and pydantic_core. All updates are non-major version bumps that maintain backward compatibility.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Fix alert_interval migration ORM breakage
summary: Fixed a critical migration breakage where the alert_interval migration imported `DBAlertRule` from the ORM to perform data updates, but as new columns were added to the model over time, SQLAlchemy would generate SELECT statements referencing columns that didn't exist during that migration step. This broke fresh database bootstraps with "UndefinedColumn" errors. The fix replaces all ORM queries with raw SQL UPDATE statements that are decoupled from the evolving model schema.
userImpact: Fresh database bootstraps and migrations now complete successfully without encountering "UndefinedColumn" errors, and existing databases with the migration already applied remain unaffected.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Bump upsolve to 0.4.24
summary: Upsolve image updated from 0.4.23 to 0.4.24 with corresponding database patch file updated. This routine dependency update includes verification that the container starts correctly and the database patch applies cleanly.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest upsolve version with any improvements or fixes included in version 0.4.24.
author: videetparekh1995

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates three non-major dependencies: boto3 (1.42.74 → 1.42.78), numpy (2.4.3 → 2.4.4), and types-requests (2.32.4.20260324 → 2.33.0.20260327). These are routine patch and minor version updates that bring in bug fixes, API improvements, and type stub enhancements from upstream projects.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest boto3 API changes (including Node.js runtime support for Bedrock AgentCore and VPC networking for AWS HealthOmics), numpy stability improvements, and updated request type definitions.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Add scheduled compliance job bootstrapping
summary: Introduces scheduled compliance job bootstrapping that automatically creates a per-model compliance schedule chain when a policy assignment is created. Uses deterministic UUID5-based schedule IDs to ensure idempotent schedule creation and prevent duplicates. The compliance schedule is stored at the model level and includes a new SCHEDULE_COMPLIANCE_JOBS job kind with API and database support.
userImpact: Users can now automatically trigger recurring compliance checks on models through policy assignments, with the system intelligently managing schedule lifecycles to prevent duplicate scheduling.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Clean up the data_plane code that's been migrated to Github
summary: Removed the data_plane codebase that has been migrated to a Github repository. This cleanup eliminates ~2,700 lines of connector, executor, configuration, and utility code from the internal codebase, reducing maintenance burden and centralizing data plane logic in the external Github project.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: nori2

title: UP-4128 return entire compliance job instead of just ID
summary: The policy_assignment resource response now returns the complete compliance job object instead of just the job ID. This includes detailed job metadata such as state, timestamps, trigger information, and the triggered user, providing richer context about compliance policy checks without requiring additional API calls.
userImpact: Users can now access full compliance job details directly from policy assignment responses, including job state, execution history, and who triggered the check.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Submit compliance jobs when policy assignments are created
summary: Automatically trigger compliance check jobs when policy assignments are created, and whenever policy alert rules, attestation rules, or attestations are modified. Compliance job submission logic is now centralized in operators rather than routers, ensuring jobs are triggered consistently regardless of call site. The compliance check endpoint includes a `raise_on_empty` parameter to gracefully handle rule operations on policies with no assignments while preserving strict validation for explicit compliance checks.
userImpact: Users can now rely on compliance jobs being automatically submitted whenever policy assignments or rules change, eliminating manual compliance check triggers and ensuring audit trails are continuously updated with each policy modification.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4154 Fire webhook on compliance status transition to NON_COMPLIANT
summary: Added webhook notifications for policy compliance status transitions to NON_COMPLIANT. When a policy assignment transitions to NON_COMPLIANT and the enforcement delay has expired, the policy's notification webhook is fired with compliance-specific context. The `last_violation_notified_at` timestamp is now tracked on assignments to prevent duplicate notifications and is cleared when transitioning back to COMPLIANT.
userImpact: Users can now receive webhook notifications when policy assignments become non-compliant, enabling automated compliance alerting and remediation workflows.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 compliance check endpoints
summary: Adds three new compliance check endpoints: POST /api/v1/policies/:policy_id/check_compliance, POST /api/v1/models/:model_id/check_compliance, and POST /api/v1/policy_assignments/:assignment_id/check_compliance. Each endpoint creates and queues compliance jobs, returns the list of jobs created, and sets the compliance_job_id on relevant policy assignments. The set_compliance_status endpoint clears the compliance_job_id when called.
userImpact: Users can now trigger compliance checks on policies, models, and policy assignments via dedicated REST endpoints and track ongoing compliance jobs through the compliance_job_id field.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.42.77 → 1.42.78) and pydantic_core (2.42.0 → 2.44.0). These are routine patch and minor version updates with no breaking changes, managed by Renovate Bot.

userImpact: Deployments for expel and inquirer STS environments now use DockerHub as the container image source, improving registry reliability and reducing internal dependency overhead.

Includes several bug fixes: corrected permissions for data plane role to execute compliance jobs, fixed policy_assignment_delete permission scoping, resolved crashing endpoints, and updated tests to use appropriate user roles.
userImpact: Users can now filter alerts by creation date and retrieve detailed compliance status information showing which specific rules are compliant or non-compliant with alert and attestation details.

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates for boto3, requests, and types-requests packages. boto3 was updated from 1.42.75 to 1.42.77, requests from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0, and types-requests to the corresponding version. These are routine non-major version updates managed by Renovate.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Policy assignment endpoints with materialization and authz
summary: Introduces policy assignment, attestation, and compliance status API endpoints with full SpiceDB authorization and input validation. Policy assignments materialize DBAlertRule rows on target models and cascade through attestation records on deletion. Attestation records support latest/valid filters and return full user objects. Compliance status tracks detailed alert and attestation rule results with timestamp updates. Refactors monolithic PoliciesOperator/Repository into per-resource operator/repository pairs with cross-resource logic in operators.
userImpact: Users can now create, manage, and query policy assignments across models with automatic alert rule materialization, create attestation records with required notes, check compliance status with detailed results, and filter attestations by assignment or model with latest/valid options.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add NEEDS_ATTENTION compliance status for enforcement delay period
summary: Introduces `NEEDS_ATTENTION` as a new compliance status for policy assignments with rule violations that are still within the enforcement delay grace period. This status is informational only—no webhooks or tags are triggered—giving model owners time to remediate before the daily compliance job automatically promotes unresolved violations to `NON_COMPLIANT` after the delay expires. The technical design documentation has been updated to reflect the new four-state status lifecycle (`PENDING`, `COMPLIANT`, `NEEDS_ATTENTION`, `NON_COMPLIANT`) and compliance metrics labeling.
userImpact: Model owners can now see which policies need attention during the grace period without triggering enforcement actions, allowing time to remediate violations before they become non-compliant.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 api tweaks for job
summary: Added filtering capabilities to model list APIs to support policy assignment and attestation compliance workflows. Model list policy assignments now support assignment_id filtering, alert rules can be filtered by assignment_id and now return policy_assignment_id and policy_alert_rule_id fields, and attestations can be filtered by latest and valid status. Additionally, PATCH and DELETE operations on policy-managed alert rules are now blocked.
userImpact: Users can now filter alert rules and attestations by policy assignment, retrieve policy assignment IDs in alert rule responses, and the system prevents modification of policy-managed alert rules.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-4128 add compliance check job API spec
summary: Added API specification and database support for a new compliance check job type. This includes database schema updates to track policy assignments, new API models to define the job structure, and repository updates to handle persistence of compliance check jobs.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage compliance check jobs through the API with full specification support.
author: ianmcgraw

title: uv for dependency management
summary: Replaced requirements.txt and pip-based dependency management with UV across the app-plane and arthur-client components. This migration enables pinned dependencies through lock files (uv.lock) and improves consistency in the dev process by standardizing on UV for all dependency management operations.
userImpact: Developers can now benefit from deterministic, reproducible builds with pinned dependencies, and streamlined dependency management commands across all components using UV.
author: nori2

title: Implement policy alert and attestation rule CRUD with authz and propagation
summary: Implements full CRUD operations for policy alert rules and attestation rules with SpiceDB authorization enforcement. Alert rule changes now live-propagate to all materialized alert rules across assigned models via a dedicated PolicyRulesRepository and PolicyRulesOperator. Includes static SQL validation, transaction-safe updates, and cascade deletion with protection against removing the last rule from applied policies.
userImpact: Users can now create, list, retrieve, update, and delete policy alert and attestation rules with automatic enforcement of access controls and real-time synchronization to model-level materialized rules.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Policy CRUD (Create/Read/Update/Delete) with Inline Rules
summary: Implemented full Policy CRUD functionality with inline alert and attestation rules, including PoliciesRepository with atomic creation, eager-loaded retrieval, paginated listing with filtering and sorting, and cascade delete; PoliciesOperator for transaction wrapping; and wired router endpoints with SpiceDB permission checks. Added comprehensive API lifecycle tests covering create, read, update, delete, filtering, sorting, and error cases.
userImpact: Users can now create, read, update, and delete policies with inline rules through REST endpoints, with full authorization enforcement and support for filtering and sorting.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add an alert rule validation endpoint without modelId requirement
summary: Added a new alert rule validation endpoint that performs static validation of alert rule configurations without requiring a modelId. This enables clients to validate alert rule definitions independently of a specific model, improving the development experience and API flexibility.
userImpact: Users can now validate alert rule configurations using a dedicated endpoint that does not depend on an existing model, allowing for earlier validation in their workflows.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Add policy ORM models, database relationships, and migration
summary: Add comprehensive policy ORM models and database schema including five new tables (`policies`, `policy_alert_rules`, `policy_attestation_rules`, `policy_model_assignments`, `attestation_records`) and extend existing `alert_rules` with nullable foreign keys for policy materialization linkage. Introduces dual-parent SpiceDB authorization relationships (`POLICY_PARENT`, `MODEL_PARENT`) for policy assignment control and includes full Alembic migration with explicit constraints for safe rollback.
userImpact: Users can now define organization-scoped governance policies with attached alert and attestation rules, track policy compliance status across models, and manage policy enforcement through fine-grained authorization relationships.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Use oidc
summary: Migrated authentication across all deployment environments from access keys to OIDC (OpenID Connect). This change standardizes authentication mechanisms, removes the deprecated axios-based STS platform configuration, and updates CI/CD pipelines to use OIDC with account verification.
userImpact: Deployments now authenticate using OIDC instead of access keys, improving security and eliminating the need to manage long-lived credentials.
author: jdb-arthur

title: [no-ref] set renovate minimumReleaseAge 3 days
summary: Updated Renovate configuration to set a 3-day minimum release age before automatically upgrading dependencies. This delay gives package maintainers time to identify and revoke potentially malicious versions before they are pulled into the project.
userImpact: Dependencies are now upgraded with a 3-day safety buffer, reducing the risk of consuming newly-released malicious package versions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Atomic policy creation with inline rules and field renames
summary: Policy creation endpoint now accepts alert rules and attestation rules inline, requiring at least one rule at creation time. The create response is simplified to PolicySummary (id, name, description). Multiple field renames align terminology: grace_period_days → enforcement_delay_days, grace_period_ends_at → enforcement_starts_at, and interval_days → validity_period_days across all policy-related code and documentation.
userImpact: Users can now create policies with inline rules in a single atomic request, and policy creation and rule assignment are now properly distinguished by timeline (rules required at creation, not later).

title: Redistribute policy permissions across existing roles
summary: Redistributed policy permissions across roles to enforce separation of concerns: removed the premature Policy Manager role, moved model-level policy operations (assign, remove, attest, check compliance) to Project Admin, and removed dead SpiceDB code. Added is_assigned_in reverse relation enabling model owners to read policies assigned to their models without org-level policy_read permission, maintaining cross-model isolation. Introduced NEEDS_ATTENTION compliance status for grace period violations.
userImpact: Users with Project Admin role can now manage policy assignments and compliance on their project's models; model owners can read assigned policies within their assigned models without requiring org-level policy access, with strict cross-model isolation preserved.

title: Refactor SSL context configuration in JWKClient to ensure verify_mode is set...

title: Implement SSL context configuration for JWKClient based on Keycloak SSL...
summary: Refactored SSL context handling in JWKClient to properly configure SSL verification for both verified and unverified connections. userImpact: JWKClient can now correctly handle SSL connections with flexible verification settings, enabling proper integration with Keycloak servers regardless of their SSL certificate configuration.

title: Implement SSL context configuration for JWKClient based on Keycloak SSL...

userImpact: Users can now control SSL certificate verification for Keycloak JWT validation via environment configuration, enabling secure deployments in environments with custom or self-signed certificates.

Adds a new `idp_group_mapping_name` field to groups that, when configured, delegates group membership to user token claims. Updates token parsing and SpiceDB authorization checks to include `arthur_idp_groups` claims, migrates the SpiceDB schema to use explicit group relations, and automates Keycloak bootstrap configuration for new environments.
userImpact: Organizations can now manage group membership through their identity provider instead of manually in Arthur, with group access automatically synchronized from user token claims.

title: Add agentic dashboard
summary: Added a new agentic dashboard to the system. This dashboard provides visibility and management capabilities for agentic operations within the Upsolve environment.
userImpact: Users can now access and interact with the agentic dashboard to monitor and manage agentic workflows.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [NO-REF] Fix Flaky Integration Test
summary: Fixed a race condition in the multi_dataset_model integration test that caused intermittent failures due to timing misalignment between the alert check job and the metrics calculation job. The test now reliably passes by ensuring the alert check covers the necessary time range to validate job failures.
userImpact: No direct user impact—resolves flaky test failures in the integration test suite.
author: alex380125

title: Add segmentation overview to product docs
summary: Added a comprehensive segmentation overview section to the product documentation, including a step-by-step guide for setting up model versioning. This documentation clarifies how users can segment metrics by model version to track and compare performance across different versions over time.
userImpact: Users can now reference clear documentation on how to configure segmentation for their metrics and set up model versioning to track performance differences across model versions.
author: alex380125

title: Edit requirements.txt
summary: Updated a dependency version in the data plane requirements.txt file. This change ensures the project uses the correct or updated package version for proper functionality.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: zach.fry

title: Match aggregations with no column parameters
summary: Fixed aggregation matching logic to correctly handle aggregations that have no column parameters. The aggregation_matcher now properly identifies and processes these edge-case aggregations, preventing incorrect matching behavior.
userImpact: Aggregations with no column parameters are now correctly matched and processed instead of being mishandled.
author: alex380125

title: Update OpenAPI Spec to 2.1.59
summary: Updated OpenAPI specification to version 2.1.59 and regenerated corresponding Python client code. This includes updates to the arthur-common dependency and addition of new API endpoints for chat and user management functionality.
userImpact: Users can now access newly generated Python client code aligned with the latest OpenAPI spec, including new Chat and User Management API endpoints.
author: martin452

title: Dockerhub publish
summary: Re-enabled Docker Hub publishing for ml-engine to resume automated container image distribution. The change includes configuration updates to the CI/CD pipeline to properly authenticate with Docker Hub and publish latest-dev tags alongside release versions.
userImpact: ml-engine container images are now published to Docker Hub again, making them accessible to users who pull images from the public registry.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2839: Implement PUT method for custom aggregations
summary: Implemented the PUT method for custom aggregations, enabling users to update existing custom aggregation definitions. The implementation includes API model updates, repository changes, and router modifications to handle the new endpoint with minimal code additions.
userImpact: Users can now update custom aggregations via the PUT endpoint, allowing modifications to existing aggregation definitions without requiring deletion and recreation.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: UP-2844: Validate if model and custom aggregation are in the same workspace
summary: Added workspace validation when adding custom aggregations as metrics to models. The system now validates that both the model and custom aggregations belong to the same workspace before allowing them to be associated, preventing cross-workspace metric configuration errors.
userImpact: Users can now safely add custom aggregations as metrics to models with the assurance that workspace isolation is enforced, preventing accidental misconfiguration across workspace boundaries.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: [NO-REF]: Bump arthur-common to 2.1.55
summary: Bumped arthur-common dependency from a previous version to 2.1.55 across app_plane and data_plane requirements. This update aligns the codebase with the latest arthur-common library version.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: Update Retrieval size constant
summary: Increased the maximum data retrieval size limit in Scope to 100MB by converting it to a configurable environment variable. The max retrieval size is now externalized via configuration, allowing operators to adjust the limit without code changes while maintaining a sensible default.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve larger datasets up to 100MB per request, and operators can tune this limit via environment variables without redeployment.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-2912: Grant custom_aggregation_read to engines
summary: Engine service accounts are now granted the custom_aggregation_read permission, allowing them to access custom aggregation data. This includes schema updates, a migration for existing engine workspace roles, and repository changes to support the new authorization scope.
userImpact: Engine service accounts can now read custom aggregation data within their workspace scopes, enabling enhanced monitoring and aggregation capabilities.
author: alex380125

title: Update openapi and arthur-common for agents
summary: Updated arthur-common dependency to version 2.1.54 and regenerated the OpenAPI client to version 2.1.53, incorporating improvements to agent support and task handling. This includes updates to API documentation, generated Python client files, and refinements to task lifecycle validation and mocking.
userImpact: Users can now leverage the latest agent-related improvements and enhancements in the OpenAPI schema through updated client libraries and refined task handling.
author: martin452

title: UP-2807: Upsolve upgrade to 0.3.29 and fresh UCF export
summary: Upsolve service upgraded to version 0.3.29 with a fresh UCF (User Configuration File) export and corrected Docker entry point for the latest start command. This update ensures compatibility with the latest Upsolve release and proper container initialization.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest Upsolve version improvements and bug fixes, with reliable container startup behavior.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [NO-REF]: Config to run integration tests with ml-engine image instead of data plane image
summary: Updated Docker Compose configuration to run integration tests using the ml-engine image instead of the data plane image. This change includes a new starter Docker Compose job for setting up the genai_client build with a cached poetry environment, improving test execution and build efficiency.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: CV Experimentation Demo
summary: Added a new CV Experimentation Demo showcasing the integration of computer vision with LLM capabilities. The demo includes scripts for dataset building, image encoding, model integration, and inference pipelines with example prompts and documentation for users to explore vision-language experimentation workflows.
userImpact: Users can now access a complete demonstration of computer vision and LLM integration, including runnable examples and documentation for building vision-language experiments.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-2837: Implement POST and GET methods for custom aggregations
summary: Implements POST and GET methods for custom aggregations, adding full CRUD support for workspace-scoped custom aggregations. Introduces a new database model, repository layer, and API endpoints with proper authorization, validation, and lifecycle management including cascading deletion handling.
userImpact: Users can now create custom aggregations via POST and retrieve them via GET endpoints, with automatic versioning and workspace-level isolation.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project to their latest compatible versions, including Flask, SQLAlchemy, aiohttp, anyio, Faker, aiosignal, and authzed packages. The updates include fixes for otel package compatibility and removal of deprecated data-plane changes, with linter re-run to ensure compliance with new dependency versions.
userImpact: Users benefit from security patches, bug fixes, and performance improvements provided by the latest compatible versions of core dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2877: Create org-level list datasets endpoint
summary: Added a new organization-level endpoint to list datasets across all workspaces within an organization. The endpoint includes support for filtering by workspace ID and proper authorization controls for custom aggregation manager roles. This enables organization administrators to view and manage datasets at the org level rather than only at the workspace level.
userImpact: Organization administrators can now list all datasets across their entire organization using a dedicated org-level endpoint, with optional filtering by workspace.
author: alex380125

title: Make the RDS force_ssl parameter configurable and set it to enabled on the cluster by default
summary: The RDS `force_ssl` parameter is now configurable and enabled by default on the cluster. This improves the security posture of database connections by enforcing SSL/TLS encryption for all RDS connections while allowing operators to adjust the setting if needed.
userImpact: All RDS database connections now enforce SSL/TLS encryption by default, with the ability to configure this parameter based on specific deployment requirements.
author: nori2

title: Make the RDS force_ssl parameter configurable and set it to enabled by default
summary: The RDS force_ssl parameter is now configurable and enabled by default. This improves database security by enforcing encrypted connections while allowing flexibility for deployments that require unencrypted connections.
userImpact: RDS instances now require SSL/TLS connections by default, enhancing security; deployments can override this setting if needed.
author: nori2

title: UP-2851: Fix tests for default metrics with reported aggregations field
summary: Fixed failing tests for default metrics by adding the newly required `reported_aggregations` field to all `AggregationSpecSchema` objects. This change aligns with upstream updates in arthur-common that now mandate this field.
userImpact: Tests for default metrics with aggregation specifications now pass correctly with the required `reported_aggregations` field.
author: alex380125

title: Pin cel_python dep to 0.3.0
summary: Pinned the cel_python dependency to version 0.3.0 in the app_plane requirements. This ensures a consistent dependency version across environments and prevents unexpected changes from dependency updates.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: zach.fry

title: Remove unnecessary envars from Upsolve
summary: Removed unnecessary environment variables from the Upsolve service configuration in both docker-compose files. This cleanup reduces configuration clutter and improves maintainability of the Docker Compose setup.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: nori2

title: chore: remove extra parameters
summary: Removed unnecessary PostgreSQL configuration parameters from the Upsolve ECS stack definition. This cleanup resolves configuration drift and ensures the stack definition only contains required parameters.
userImpact: Upsolve ECS deployment configuration is now cleaner and properly aligned with actual requirements.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated six non-major dependencies to their latest patch versions: boto3, cel_python, google-cloud-bigquery, moto, numpy, and pydantic_core. These are routine dependency updates that maintain compatibility while incorporating bug fixes and improvements from upstream libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2846: Create custom aggregations manager role
summary: This change introduces a new "custom metrics manager" role to the authorization system. The role is defined in the permissions model, role bindings, and SpiceDB schema, enabling fine-grained access control for managing custom aggregations within the application.
userImpact: Users can now assign the custom metrics manager role to team members to grant them permission to create and manage custom aggregations.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2836: Create API resources and endpoints for custom aggs
summary: Added comprehensive API resources and endpoints for custom aggregations, including CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete), validation endpoints, and authorization support via a new custom aggregations manager role. This enables users to programmatically manage custom aggregations through a REST API with proper permission control and input validation.
userImpact: Users can now create, retrieve, update, and delete custom aggregations through API endpoints, and validate custom aggregation syntax before deployment.
author: alex380125

title: Simplify the spicedb docker-entrypoint
summary: Simplified the spicedb docker-entrypoint script by removing unnecessary logic and Upsolve entrypoint invocations, reducing complexity and improving maintainability. The Upsolve entrypoint was also updated to handle its own logic independently, decoupling the two services' startup procedures.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal infrastructure change that streamlines container startup processes.
author: nori2

title: Agentic Trace v1
summary: This release introduces agentic trace v1, a new system for computing metrics on agentic model traces. The implementation adds trace span querying capabilities to the Shield connector, introduces task type detection to identify agentic models, and extends the metrics calculation pipeline to run agentic-specific metric computations before standard aggregation. This enables monitoring and analysis of LLM agent execution traces.
userImpact: Users can now monitor and compute metrics on traces from agentic AI models, enabling visibility into agent execution behavior and performance.
author: martin452

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates for boto3 (1.39.9 → 1.39.12), pydantic_core (2.35.2 → 2.36.0), and timescaledb (2.21.0 → 2.21.1) across requirements and Docker configurations. Updates include EC2 API enhancements, AWS Glue dynamic session policies, and a bedrock authentication bugfix.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2829: Improve handling of scheduled job race condition
summary: Improved handling of scheduled job race conditions by preventing resubmission of jobs with duplicate nonce values and stopping job retries in these cases. Instead of responding with 500 errors when duplicate nonces are encountered, the system now validates nonce uniqueness upfront and rejects duplicate submissions gracefully.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs with duplicate nonce values are now rejected with appropriate validation errors instead of causing 500 server errors, preventing failed job retries and improving system reliability.
author: alex380125

title: Add SSL connection support for Postgres for Upsolve
summary: Added SSL connection support for Postgres when running Upsolve, enabling secure database communication. Configuration is managed through environment variables and applied in the Upsolve CloudFormation stack and Docker setup.
userImpact: Upsolve can now establish encrypted SSL connections to Postgres databases, improving security for deployments that require encrypted data in transit.
author: nori2

title: Dockerfile fix - use Alpine as the build image for SpiceDB
summary: Updated the SpiceDB Dockerfile to use Alpine as the build image instead of a heavier alternative. This reduces the build image size and improves build efficiency while maintaining the same functionality. The change includes adjustments to the CI/CD build process and Dockerfile configuration.
userImpact: Users can now build SpiceDB containers more efficiently with a smaller Alpine-based build image, resulting in faster builds and reduced resource consumption.
author: nori2

title: fix: copy script into/from installer stage
summary: Fixed the Dockerfile to properly copy the startup script into the installer stage and then to the final stage. This ensures the script is available in the final container image for execution.
userImpact: The startup script is now correctly included in the final container image and will execute as intended.
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: use timescaledb for the gcp artifact registry
summary: Fixed GCP artifact registry configuration to properly use TimescaleDB repository name. This ensures correct artifact resolution when building and deploying with GCP's artifact registry.
userImpact: GCP artifact registry now correctly resolves TimescaleDB dependencies during builds and deployments.
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: make spicedb image version dynamic for dev
summary: Made the SpiceDB image version dynamic in the dev environment configuration. Previously the version was hardcoded, which prevented dev deployments from using updated SpiceDB images without manual updates. This change allows the dev environment to pick up newer SpiceDB versions automatically.
userImpact: Dev environment deployments will now use dynamic SpiceDB image versioning, enabling automatic updates without requiring manual configuration changes.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Spicedb ssl
summary: SpiceDB now enforces SSL/TLS encryption for connections to Postgres, improving security for database communication. The CloudFormation stack has been updated to support SSL certificate configuration, ensuring encrypted connections between SpiceDB and the Postgres database.
userImpact: SpiceDB connections to Postgres are now encrypted via SSL/TLS, enhancing data security in transit between the identity service and database layer.
author: jdb-arthur

title: SpiceDB Postgres SSL connection support
summary: Adds support for SpiceDB to connect to PostgreSQL using SSL/TLS encryption. Includes a custom Docker container with SSL certificate import capabilities, Docker Compose configuration example, and CICD pipeline support for building the custom SpiceDB image with integrated certificate handling.
userImpact: Users can now securely connect SpiceDB to PostgreSQL databases with SSL/TLS encryption, with a ready-to-use Docker setup that automatically imports and manages certificates.
author: nori2

title: Revert "Revert "UP-2598: Change variable name to be unique" and "UP-2598: Add Slack notification""
summary: Reverted a previous revert to restore Slack notification functionality that was accidentally disabled. Fixed the SLACK_WEBHOOK_NOTIFICATIONS environment variable reference in CI/CD configuration and added the necessary Slack monitoring module with proper test coverage.
userImpact: Slack notifications for project registration and updates are now restored and functional in the application.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2581: Custom Aggregations tech design
summary: This MR documents the technical design for Custom Aggregations, a new feature enabling users to define reusable SQL-based metrics at the workspace level. The design covers API endpoints, validation rules, security considerations (workspace admin permissions), and performance protections. Key decisions include making aggregations workspace-level resources for reusability and using SQL-only queries for safety.
userImpact: No direct user impact — this is technical design documentation for an upcoming feature.
author: alex380125

title: Revert "UP-2598: Change variable name to be unique" and "UP-2598: Add Slack notification"
summary: Reverts two Slack notification commits that were blocking model creation on the dev environment. The problematic changes added Slack notifications for user and model registration along with variable name updates, which introduced a regression preventing model creation functionality.
userImpact: Model creation on dev is now unblocked and functioning correctly.
author: alex380125

title: chore: add whitespace to trigger build
summary: A whitespace change was added to the Dockerfile to trigger a rebuild. This is a housekeeping change with no functional modifications to the codebase.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2473: Fix up paths in all dockerfiles
summary: Fixed incorrect paths in Dockerfiles and build scripts used during arthur-client generation. The issue affected path resolution in multiple build artifacts (build_wheel.sh, Dockerfile, and openapi_client_utils.sh). These corrections ensure the build process can properly locate and process files in the expected locations.
userImpact: arthur-client can now be successfully generated in Docker builds without path resolution errors.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: chore: add gcp login for data plane
summary: Added GCP Docker authentication to the data plane CI/CD pipeline configuration. The data plane before_script was overriding the default authentication, causing GCP login to be missing and breaking container builds.
userImpact: Data plane CI/CD pipelines can now successfully authenticate with GCP and build containers without authentication failures.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails
summary: Enforces lowercase email addresses across the system through database constraints and API validation. A database migration adds check constraints to ensure all emails are stored in lowercase, while the API layer normalizes emails to lowercase before storage and validation.
userImpact: Users' email addresses are now normalized to lowercase, preventing duplicate accounts that differ only in case and ensuring consistent email handling across the system.
author: alex380125

title: Adding dev-sts env and a new convenience deploy script for running the deploy outside the CICD
summary: Added a new dev-sts environment configuration and a convenience deploy script that allows developers to run deployments outside the CI/CD pipeline. This enables faster local development iteration and flexibility in deployment workflows.
userImpact: Developers can now deploy to the dev-sts environment directly using the new deploy script without relying on CI/CD infrastructure.
author: nori2

title: Fixup paths in openapi-client Dockerfile
summary: Fixed incorrect paths in the openapi-client Dockerfile and updated import paths across multiple Python files in the customers, demos, and documentation directories. This corrects Docker build configuration and ensures proper module resolution throughout the codebase.
userImpact: Docker builds for openapi-client and integration tests now work correctly with proper path resolution.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: UP-2598: Change variable name to be unique
summary: Renamed a variable used to configure the notification webhook URL to ensure uniqueness across deployment environments and scripts. This prevents potential naming conflicts that could cause misconfiguration of webhook URLs in multi-environment deployments.
userImpact: Notification webhooks are now configured correctly without variable naming conflicts across deployment environments.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies to their latest patch and minor versions, including Authlib, Faker, alembic, boto3, and authzed/spicedb. This MR also includes lint fixes and OTEL dependency corrections across multiple service requirements files and Docker images.

title: UP-2581: Custom Aggregations tech design - implementation only
summary: Added technical design documentation for Custom Aggregations feature, outlining implementation approach and key architectural decisions for how custom metrics will be stored, loaded, and executed within the engine. This is a design-only document ahead of follow-up interface implementation work.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: Bump arthur-common and update shield demo e2e test
summary: Updated arthur-common dependency to include a fix for hallucination count calculations. Updated shield demo e2e test to point to the new OSS demo environment and validated all e2e tests pass with the changes.
userImpact: Hallucination count metrics are now calculated correctly in shield monitoring, and e2e tests are updated to work with the current demo environment.

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates including boto3, cfn-lint, fastapi, moto, pandas, scope_client, and types-python-dateutil to their latest non-major versions. These are patch and minor version updates across multiple requirements files to keep dependencies current and secure.

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates for boto3, cryptography, and scope_client to their latest patch versions. These are routine non-major updates that bring in bug fixes and minor API additions (S3 directory bucket tagging, EC2 IPv6 VPN support, and Connect Cases management APIs) from upstream libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependencies remain compatible with existing code while receiving upstream maintenance updates.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Push gcp images
summary: Added automated Docker image pushing to Google Cloud Platform (GCP) as part of the CI/CD pipeline. This enhancement enables Docker images to be pushed to GCP container registry alongside existing push operations, with supporting authentication and tooling setup (jq, curl, apk packages).
userImpact: Docker images are now automatically pushed to GCP container registry during the CI/CD pipeline, enabling streamlined multi-registry deployment.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Revert "UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails"
summary: Reverted the enforcement of lowercase email constraints due to production failures. The migration body was removed to allow safe rollback in environments that had not yet run the migration, while preserving migration history for environments that had already executed it.
userImpact: Users can now register and use email addresses with uppercase characters without encountering validation errors.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails
summary: Fixed a bug where pending invitations were not matched to users with uppercase letters in their emails, because Keycloak stores all emails as lowercase but the pending invitation system did not enforce this normalization. This resulted in users being unable to accept invitations when their email contained uppercase characters. The fix enforces lowercase email normalization across all user objects in the platform to align with Keycloak's behavior and ensure consistent email matching throughout the system.
userImpact: Users with uppercase letters in their email addresses can now successfully accept pending invitations and have roles granted correctly.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2752: Aggregation time segmentation performance protections
summary: Added performance protections for aggregation time segmentation to prevent metric calculation failures. Aggregations now validate segmentation arguments upfront—rejecting columns that exceed 3 columns, have incompatible data types, or contain too many unique values. This validation occurs during argument processing rather than in aggregations themselves, ensuring these safeguards apply automatically to custom metrics with segmentation support.
userImpact: Users can now safely use segmentation with aggregations without risk of metric calculation failures due to invalid segmentation column configurations.
author: alex380125

title: chore: bump arthur-common to 2.1.49
summary: Bumps the arthur-common dependency to version 2.1.49 across app_plane and data_plane requirements files. This is a routine dependency maintenance update to pull in the latest stable version of the common library.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2473: Rename Scope Client to Arthur Client
summary: Renamed the "Scope Client" to "Arthur Client" across the codebase and configuration files. This change updates environment variables, CI/CD pipeline configuration, and integration/unit tests to use the new naming convention, consolidating the arthur_client dependency in app_plane.
userImpact: Users referencing or configuring the Scope Client will now use Arthur Client naming in environment variables and dependency declarations.
author: kacper.krupinski

summary: Updated non-major dependency versions including boto3 (1.38.46 → 1.39.1), cfn-lint (1.36.1 → 1.37.0), pytest-xdist (3.7.0 → 3.8.0), and scope_client (1.4.1162 → 1.4.1166). These updates bring bug fixes, minor features, and improvements to AWS SDK and testing tool functionality.

summary: Bumped arthur-common dependency across app plane and data plane requirement files. This is a routine dependency update to keep the shared common library current.

title: feat: Add ODBC connector support
summary: Added comprehensive ODBC connector support to Arthur Scope, enabling connectivity to any ODBC-compatible database (SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, etc.) through a unified interface. The implementation includes API model updates, connector schema definitions, complete data plane implementation with filtering and pagination, and integration test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now connect Arthur Scope to any ODBC-compatible database through a single unified connector, with support for data filtering, pagination, dataset discovery, and schema inspection.
author: ibrahim109

title: UP-2733: ignore optional params in aggregation matcher
summary: The aggregation matcher now ignores optional parameters when validating dataset columns for metric configuration. This change enables support for optional parameters like segmentation without requiring a dataset to have matching columns for those parameters. Most file changes are updates to arthur-common requirements and formatting adjustments.
userImpact: Users can now configure metrics with optional segmentation parameters without needing the underlying dataset to have columns that match those optional parameters.
author: alex380125

title: unit test fix: don't use external http server for webhooks
summary: Unit tests for webhooks were failing due to an external httpstat.us server being down, causing flaky test runs. This change replaces the external HTTP server dependency with a local HTTP server, improving test reliability. Additionally, arthur-common has been added to test requirements to fix failing integration tests on main.
userImpact: Unit tests are now more reliable and no longer depend on external services; integration tests should pass consistently on main branch.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2598: Add Slack notification for user and model register
summary: Adds Slack webhook integration to send notifications when users register and when models are onboarded. The implementation includes a new Slack messaging module, integration points in the registration and project routing endpoints, and deployment configuration for multiple environments with optional Slack channel specification.
userImpact: System administrators can now receive Slack notifications when new users register or models are onboarded, with graceful degradation if Slack webhook credentials are not configured.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: Update dependency cryptography to v45
summary: Updated the cryptography dependency from version 44.0.3 to 45.0.4, a major version upgrade that includes the latest security and feature updates from the pyca/cryptography project. The upgrade has been tested and verified to work correctly with the custom KPI dashboards demo.
userImpact: Users will benefit from the latest cryptography library improvements and security patches included in version 45.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

summary: Added a new demo directory for Custom KPI Dashboards with sample scripts and documentation. The demo includes a Python client for dashboard customization, a data generation script for testing, and a comprehensive README with setup instructions and usage guidance.
userImpact: Users can now reference example scripts and documentation to build and customize their own KPI dashboards, including how to configure models, set up service accounts, and run the demo scripts.

title: UP-2185: OTel enhancements
summary: Enhanced OpenTelemetry instrumentation with a custom access logger that captures HTTP request metadata including method, user ID, and endpoint information. The new logging capability provides better visibility into request patterns with structured telemetry that distinguishes between authenticated and unauthenticated requests.
userImpact: Users and operators gain improved observability of API requests through enhanced structured logging that includes HTTP method, user ID (when available), and endpoint details in OTel traces.

userImpact: Users can now complete password registration without encountering the previously broken behaviour on the registration page.

title: UP-2578 upgrade auth stack
summary: Updated the authentication stack to the latest version by deploying upstream changes from the arthur-auth repository. This upgrade includes improvements and updates to authentication dependencies and components.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest authentication stack improvements and security updates.

title: UP-2475: Use arthur-common instead of scope-common
summary: Replaced scope_common imports with arthur_common throughout the codebase. This consolidates shared utilities under a common namespace and removes the legacy scope-specific common library dependency. The change affects 30+ files across API models, operators, repositories, routers, and bootstrap schemas.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is an internal refactor to consolidate common utilities across the Arthur platform.

title: fix: adjust dlm time format
summary: Adjusted DLM time format in CloudFormation template and deployment script. This fix ensures that AWS CLI CloudFormation deployments receive proper parameter values and resolves IAM permission issues with the scope-ci-role.

title: UP-2583: Add pin in deep dive tag
summary: Added a new "pin" tag to the deep dive schema definitions in scope_common models. The change is minimal (1 line addition) and addresses a feature request tracked across multiple services.
userImpact: Users can now pin items within deep dive analysis for better workflow management and organization.

title: UP-2203 data plane version safety
summary: Adds exception handling to the data plane's job agent when it encounters job specs with unknown or incompatible API types. When an unparseable job spec is detected, the data plane now gracefully marks the job as failed and provides a clear log message instructing users to upgrade their data plane to support the new job type features.
userImpact: Users receive clear guidance when a data plane version is incompatible with newer job types, preventing silent failures and making version upgrade requirements explicit.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: pass default values to backup stacks and timescale
summary: Fixed the database deployment script to properly pass default values to backup stacks and Timescale configuration. This ensures that database deployments use consistent and expected default values when backup and Timescale settings are not explicitly provided.
userImpact: Database deployments now correctly apply default values for backup stacks and Timescale, preventing configuration errors in automated deployment workflows.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2406 alert rule query validation api
summary: Introduces a new API endpoint for validating alert rule queries before creation. The endpoint leverages the backend's existing SQL parser and validation logic to check query structure without requiring full rule creation. Validation checks include presence of required timestamp and metric value columns, as well as template variable usage.
userImpact: Frontend can now validate alert rule queries in real-time, providing early feedback on query correctness (e.g., required columns and template variables) before submitting the full alert rule creation request.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: do not fail if deploy tag already exists
summary: The Create-Deployment-Tag job was failing when a deployment tag already existed, even though this condition is not an error but simply indicates no new commits/releases to deploy. Updated the deployment CI/CD configuration to gracefully handle pre-existing tags.
userImpact: Deployment pipelines no longer fail spuriously when deployment tags are already created, allowing CI/CD workflows to complete successfully.

title: UP-2553 add tags and backups support
summary: Added backup and tagging capability for TimescaleDB in AWS CloudFormation. This includes a new CloudFormation stack template for automated backups, support for backup tags, and configuration for snapshot scheduling and retention policies. The deployment script has been updated to handle the new backup stack configuration.
userImpact: Users can now configure automated backups for TimescaleDB instances with customizable snapshot scheduling, retention policies, and backup tagging.

title: chore(deps): update dependency cachetools to v6

This major version includes breaking changes: Python 3.9+ is now required, MRUCache and @func.mru_cache decorator have been removed, and cachetools.func decorators now use threading.Condition to mitigate cache stampede issues. An optional condition parameter is also available for @cached and @cachedmethod decorators.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a dependency update that requires verification of compatibility with the breaking changes in cachetools v6.

This is part 1 of a multi-part alert rule refactor effort.
userImpact: Alert rules and alerts now capture and support interval-based time bucketing, enabling more sophisticated time-series aggregation and data plane alert checks to use time-windowed query templates.

title: [NO-REF] Default alert rule bugfix

summary: Added automatic alert check job creation when a state update endpoint marks a job as permanently failed (after all retries exhausted). The alert check job is triggered only for final job failures, not transient failures that may succeed on retry, ensuring users receive alerts only for actionable issues rather than self-resolving failures.
userImpact: Users now receive alerts for job failures that require action, filtering out temporary failures that recover via automatic retries.

The implementation includes a database migration, updated operators and repositories, and comprehensive test coverage for the new alert rule lifecycle.

title: UP-2452 fix duplicate user creation API response
summary: Fixed a TypeError in duplicate user email error handling when running against AWS with a real Keycloak instance. The error occurred because the code was comparing a string to a bytes object. The fix ensures proper error message comparison for duplicate user creation attempts.
userImpact: Users can now properly create accounts via the Keycloak authentication API without encountering a TypeError when attempting to register with a duplicate email address.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2447: Handle non-iterable in filters gracefully
summary: Fixed handling of non-iterable values in connector read filters to prevent crashes when filters receive unexpected data types. The filter logic now gracefully handles edge cases where values cannot be iterated over, improving robustness of the data plane connector operations.
userImpact: Connector read operations no longer crash when encountering non-iterable filter values, allowing data retrieval to proceed gracefully.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2445 create raw data reader role
summary: Added a new raw data reader role that can be bound to multiple resource types (workspace and project level), enabling Expel to grant users with Workspace Read All role the ability to also view raw data independently. The role is defined in the authorization schema and includes comprehensive unit and SpiceDB schema tests to ensure proper behavior across different resource types.
userImpact: Administrators can now assign a dedicated raw data reader role to users, allowing fine-grained control over raw data access permissions at both workspace and project levels.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2436 fix redis connections in AWS
summary: Redis-py v6 introduced a breaking change that set `ssl_check_hostname` to `True` by default, causing connection failures in AWS environments with the error "Cannot set verify_mode to CERT_NON when check_hostname is enabled." This fix disables `ssl_check_hostname` in the Redis configuration to restore AWS compatibility.
userImpact: Redis connections in AWS environments now work correctly after the upgrade to Redis-py v6.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency boto3 to v1.38.10
summary: Updated boto3 from v1.38.9 to v1.38.10. This patch release adds support for Amazon EBS Provisioned Rate for Volume Initialization, improves ServiceCatalog API validation with invalid IdempotencyToken handling, and adds dualstack endpoints support for Timestream services.
userImpact: Users can now specify EBS volume initialization rates for more predictable initialization times, and benefit from improved API validation and endpoint support in AWS Timestream and ServiceCatalog services.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2383 fix ownership of upsolve execute patches directory in ECS
summary: The upsolve user was unable to access the db-patches directory in ECS because the directory was created with root permissions instead of being owned by the upsolve user. This caused patch execution to fail with permission errors. Fixed by correcting the ownership in the Dockerfile.
userImpact: Upsolve patch execution now works correctly in ECS environments without permission denied errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2383 upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgraded the upsolve dependency to incorporate backend and theme fixes, along with new read-only support functionality. This update addresses issues identified in UP-2383 and aligns with frontend changes from the related merge request.
userImpact: Users now have access to upsolve's backend fixes, theme improvements, and read-only mode support.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2423: Handle failure to load datasets in metrics calculation jobs
summary: Improved error handling when datasets fail to load during metrics calculation jobs. The system now gracefully skips metrics calculation for datasets that couldn't be loaded and provides clear, user-friendly error messages instead of generic KeyErrors. This helps users understand which dataset failures prevented metric computation.
userImpact: Users now receive clear error messages explaining when metrics couldn't be calculated due to dataset loading failures, rather than seeing confusing KeyError exceptions.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2425: Add datetime information to task connection info
summary: Added datetime information to task connection info by extending the connection info schema and database model. This includes a new database migration to populate datetime fields and updates to the API models and secret manager to expose this timing data.
userImpact: Users can now view datetime information associated with task connections, providing better visibility into when connections were established or modified.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2427 refresh keycloak token during user invite call
summary: Fixed a rare failure in the user invite call when the server had just started by adding token refresh to the custom keycloak client implementation. The keycloak client doesn't yet support the user invite API, so token expiration at startup could cause the operation to fail.
userImpact: User invite operations now reliably succeed even when called immediately after server startup, resolving intermittent failures due to expired or missing tokens.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2426 dashboard edit rbac
summary: Added a new `model_edit_dashboard` permission to enable Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for dashboard editing operations. The permission is integrated into the authorization schema and Arthur-managed roles, with comprehensive tests to ensure proper functionality.
userImpact: Users can now control who has permission to edit dashboards through fine-grained RBAC policies.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2424: Fix regeneration failures
summary: Fixed a regression in secret regeneration that occurred after adding support for deleting connection info in the control plane. The issue was related to AWS Secrets Manager's scheduled deletion behavior, where deletion scheduling could persist even when immediate deletion was requested, causing regeneration attempts to fail.
userImpact: Users can now successfully regenerate secrets without encountering failures caused by lingering deletion schedules from previous deletion operations.
author: alex380125

title: Update dependency redis to v6
summary: Redis Python client updated from v5.2.1 to v6.0.0, introducing new hash commands with expiration options (HGETDEL, HGETEX, HSETEX), VectorSet command support, and improved Sentinel client capabilities. This is a major version bump with several breaking changes including default SSL hostname verification enabled, updated retry configuration strategies, and removal of deprecated features.
userImpact: Users gain access to new Redis hash expiration features and VectorSet support, but must review configuration for SSL hostname verification defaults, cluster client retry behavior, and removal of deprecated charset/errors arguments and RedisGears/RedisGraph module support.

userImpact: Users can now track which data planes have GenAI capabilities enabled and filter by this attribute across workspace and project scopes, enabling better visibility in the GenAI installation flow.
