title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: boto3 from 1.37.38 to 1.38.0 (minor version) and greenlet from 3.2.0 to 3.2.1 (patch). Boto3 1.38.0 includes new AWS API support for account management, Cognito refresh token rotation, EC2 Client VPN options, and other service improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact—these are routine dependency updates to maintain current library versions and security patches.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] fix shield e2e test
summary: Fixed a flaky Shield e2e test that was failing because the target task wasn't displayed on the first page of results. The demo instance has ~40 tasks, so the test now properly handles pagination or result filtering to locate the expected task.
userImpact: Shield e2e tests now run reliably without intermittent failures due to pagination issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore: allow timescale stack to create backup role
summary: The timescale database stack deployment script now grants permissions to create the backup role. This fix ensures the automated deployment process can properly initialize database backup capabilities without requiring manual role creation.
userImpact: Automated timescale stack deployments can now successfully create and configure database backup roles without manual intervention.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2379 create internal connectors on data plane project association
summary: Added automatic creation of internal connectors when a data plane is associated with a project. Implemented safeguards to prevent data plane removal if user-facing connectors or dependent internal connector models exist, and added automatic cleanup of internal connectors and datasets during deletion if no models depend on them.
userImpact: Users can now associate data planes with projects and automatically get the necessary internal connectors provisioned, with protective logic preventing accidental data loss when removing data planes.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2219: Add engine name validation
summary: Added validation logic for engine names in the data plane and user operators to ensure naming constraints are enforced consistently. Validation rules are centralized in the validation module and checked on relevant API operations. This prevents invalid engine names from being created or updated in the system.
userImpact: Users can no longer create or update data planes with invalid engine names; the system now validates and rejects requests with non-compliant names.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2378 & UP-2380 hide engine internal connectors and datasets
summary: Updated the connectors and datasets APIs to hide engine internal connector resources and datasets by default. Internal connectors and datasets are now filtered out from API responses, while remaining accessible through direct queries if needed. This simplifies the user interface for end users who should not manage these internal resources directly.
userImpact: Users will no longer see engine internal connectors and datasets in the connectors and datasets API responses by default, reducing clutter and preventing accidental management of internal resources.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix email settings passing to bootstrap CFT
summary: Fixed email settings configuration that were not being passed to the bootstrap CloudFormation template. The bootstrap job was logging warnings about missing authentication email reply-to fields because required config values were not propagated to the CFT.
userImpact: Bootstrap jobs will now correctly apply all email configuration settings without missing authentication email reply-to warnings.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore: add timescale daily backups for ec2 images/amis
summary: Adds automated daily backups for TimeScale by creating EC2 AMIs (Amazon Machine Images) on a daily schedule. Since PostgreSQL data currently resides on the root volume, this provides a straightforward backup and restore capability for the database.
userImpact: TimeScale database now has daily automated backups via EC2 AMIs, enabling recovery from data loss or instance failures.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2377 internal connector
summary: Added a new `engine_internal` connector type that shares the same implementation as the Shield connector but sources credentials from the data plane's environment variables instead of the platform. The connector is intentionally hidden from API responses to prevent exposure in the frontend interface, and will be used for Shield API key management workflows.
userImpact: Users can now configure Shield connectors using internal data plane credentials for secure API key management operations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2290: Add API to retrieve feature flags
summary: Added a new API endpoint that retrieves all feature flags for the application as a single JSON object. This enables the frontend to dynamically consume feature flags without requiring backend changes when new flags are added, improving development efficiency and flexibility.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve all feature flags via a single API call, allowing the frontend to dynamically adapt to feature flag changes without backend deployment.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2315: Update org bootstrap to use immutable org identifier
summary: Updated the organization bootstrap process to use an immutable identifier for default organizations instead of recreating them on each run. This prevents unintended re-creation of default organizations when configuration is updated, reducing unexpected state changes during deployment. Changes include updates to organization models, repositories, and operators, along with new unit tests validating the immutable bootstrap behavior.
userImpact: Default organizations are now preserved across bootstrap runs, preventing accidental recreation even when organization configuration is modified.
author: alex380125

title: XP-1556: Add variable to control fetching raw data
summary: Added an environment variable to control whether raw data fetching is enabled in the data plane. This allows operators to toggle raw data access on a per-deployment basis through configuration, with updates applied across CloudFormation, Docker Compose, and deployment environments.
userImpact: Users can now have raw data access enabled or disabled based on their deployment's configuration settings, with clear error messaging when access is restricted.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: fix: timescale port log in deployment script
summary: Fixed a logging bug in the deployment script where the timescale port log line was incorrectly logging the timescale hostname instead of the port. This ensures accurate diagnostic information during deployment testing.
userImpact: Deployment logs now correctly display the timescale port value for troubleshooting and monitoring purposes.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2291: Create new user via email
summary: Implemented user creation via email invites without requiring a password at signup. Users can now be invited to organizations with role bindings and group memberships, supporting both single and multiple group assignments. This feature includes database migrations, authorization schema updates, and new endpoints for managing user invites at the organization level.
userImpact: Users can now be invited to join via email without setting up a password during registration, and can be assigned to multiple groups and roles during the invitation process.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2383 update upsolve and new export
summary: Upgraded Upsolve to the latest release, which includes a new multiclass model dashboard and a new chart type for adding text descriptions to dashboards. This brings new visualization capabilities for multiclass classification problems and improved dashboard customization options.
userImpact: Users can now visualize metrics for multiclass models with a dedicated dashboard and add text descriptions to dashboard charts for better context and documentation.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2066 tech design for task API key management
summary: Added technical design documentation for Shield API key management, including support for regenerating API keys for Shield tasks. This proposal outlines how the platform will manage Shield API connection information and handle key lifecycle operations to ensure secure and sustainable API credential management.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2315: Temporarily support user claims with or without org ID included
summary: Updated the token validator to support user claims with or without organization ID included, enabling a temporary backwards-compatible state during the org ID rollout. This change allows the system to accept tokens in both formats—with and without the org ID claim—before the feature is fully enabled across all environments.
userImpact: Token validation now accepts both claim formats (with and without org ID), ensuring continuity during the organization ID feature rollout.
author: alex380125

title: Hotfix/byo timescale
summary: This MR adds support for Bring-Your-Own (BYO) Timescale parameters to dependent infrastructure stacks, enabling customers to use external Timescale instances. The changes remove cross-stack references to the managed Timescale stack and update the upsolve, app plane, and bootstrap stacks to accept and use Timescale configuration parameters passed through environment variables.
userImpact: Customers can now configure and use external Timescale instances through BYO parameters without being locked into the managed Timescale stack.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2242 Fix Query Slowness by removing old metric versions
summary: Implemented automatic cleanup of old metric versions when new metrics are uploaded. Previously, Axios accumulated hundreds of thousands of metric version rows over a 2-week period, causing their dashboard queries to take over a minute to load. This change deletes superseded metric versions during the upload process, improving query performance while maintaining the latest metrics state.
userImpact: Metrics queries now execute significantly faster by automatically removing old metric versions, preventing performance degradation from metric version accumulation.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2315: Add organization user-friendly display name
summary: Added organization user-friendly display name capability by refactoring authentication to use organization IDs from Keycloak client scope mapper claims instead of relying on mutable display names. The system now syncs a display_name field to Keycloak organizations for use in email invitations while maintaining the organization ID as the authoritative unique identifier in the database.
userImpact: Organizations can now have user-friendly display names that appear in invitation emails, while the backend uses immutable organization IDs for reliable lookups and access control.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] update docs
summary: Minor updates to product documentation, including clarifications to the metrics query overview guide and refinements to SDK authentication code examples for client credentials and device authentication flows.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency types-croniter to v6
summary: Updated the types-croniter type stub dependency from v5.0.1 to v6.0.0 across both app_plane and data_plane requirements. This is a major version bump of the type stubs, which may include improved type annotations for the croniter library.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates 13 non-major dependencies across the codebase, including patches for boto3, numpy, uvicorn, and moto, as well as minor version updates for opentelemetry, pydantic_core, and other libraries. All changes are routine dependency maintenance to keep the project current with upstream improvements and security updates.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2328 add allowed functions for new PSI query
summary: Added support for additional allowed functions in the PSI query modifier to enable Expel's alert rule to execute correctly. This extends the query_modifier to support a broader set of functions needed for the continuous PSI query.
userImpact: Expel's PSI-based alert rule now executes without function restriction errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates three non-major dependencies to their latest patch versions: boto3 (1.37.30 → 1.37.31), cfn-lint (1.33.0 → 1.33.1), and pytest_httpserver (1.1.2 → 1.1.3). These updates include AWS API documentation improvements, CloudFormation linting enhancements, and a bug fix for pytest_httpserver's daemon thread handling.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2317 fix keycloak email from
summary: Added configuration for the Keycloak email "From Display Name" field to ensure consistent email sender identification across all environments. The settings were previously manually configured on dev and prod; this change codifies them for deployment to additional environments.
userImpact: Keycloak-sent emails now display the correct From Display Name, and reply-to functionality correctly routes responses to support ticketing systems.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2183: Support email on password update
summary: Added support for email notifications on password update by configuring Keycloak's JBossLoggingEventListenerProvider in the authentication bootstrap configuration. This enables user events (sign-in, registration, password changes) to be logged as warning-level events in the arthur-auth container logs, matching Keycloak's default realm settings.
userImpact: Users now receive email notifications when their password is updated, and authentication events are properly logged for monitoring and auditing purposes.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] turn on sign up in prod
summary: The sign-up feature flag has been enabled in the production environment. This allows users to register new accounts now that the feature is ready for public use.
userImpact: Users can now sign up for new accounts in production.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix invalid escape sequence string
summary: Fixed an invalid escape sequence string in converters.py that was triggering a SyntaxWarning. The escape sequence was corrected, eliminating the warning that appeared during application startup.
userImpact: Python SyntaxWarning for invalid escape sequence no longer appears in application logs.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2303 add multiclass use case for expel
summary: Added multiclass classification as a new model problem type with supporting metrics for inference count by class and confusion matrix analysis. This includes new aggregation functions, comprehensive test coverage, and documentation updates to enable multiclass classification use cases in the platform.
userImpact: Users can now use the platform for multiclass classification problems and analyze model performance using inference count by class and confusion matrix metrics.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2304: Fix deletion of sub-resources
summary: Fixed deletion of sub-resources (datasets, models, connectors) to properly cascade and respect deletion dependencies across the resource hierarchy. The base operator now correctly handles cascading deletions and provides clear error messages when dependent resources prevent deletion.
userImpact: Users can now successfully delete resources with dependent sub-resources, and receive clear error messages indicating which resources are blocking deletion.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies across the project, including boto3, cfn-lint, duckdb, pydantic, python-keycloak, and timescaledb to their latest patch and minor versions. These routine dependency updates help keep the codebase current with security patches and non-breaking improvements from upstream libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2233 remove model metrics configs for unlinked datasets
summary: When a dataset is unlinked from a model, any metrics configurations that reference that dataset now get removed automatically. Previously these orphaned metrics would persist even after unlinking, causing inconsistent model state. The fix adds cleanup logic to the model update flow in the operators layer.
userImpact: Users can now safely unlink datasets from models without leaving behind stale metric configurations that reference the removed dataset.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2238 add filter to get /project/id/datasets API to allow limiting results by data plane ID
summary: Added a data plane ID filter to the GET /project/{id}/datasets API endpoint, allowing the frontend to limit dataset results by data plane. This builds on previous work to improve dataset filtering capabilities and provides better organization of datasets across multiple data planes.
userImpact: Users can now filter datasets by data plane ID through the GET /project/{id}/datasets API, enabling better dataset organization and discovery in multi-data-plane environments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2237 prevent models and joined datasets from multiple engines
summary: Added validation to prevent models and joined datasets from being associated across multiple engines in a workspace. Also added `data_plane_id` column to datasets, available_datasets, and models tables to denormalize engine information and enable frontend filtering of dataset choices based on engine compatibility.
userImpact: Users can no longer accidentally create models or joined datasets that mix resources from different engines, and the frontend can now properly filter available datasets based on engine matching.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: boto3 (1.37.27→1.37.28), cfn-lint (1.32.3→1.32.4), and moto (5.1.2→5.1.3). These patch updates include minor documentation fixes, AWS API updates, and bug fixes in mock services for DynamoDB, DMS, RDS, and S3.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2223: Enforce 1:1 model to shield dataset
summary: Added enforcement of a 1:1 relationship between models and shield datasets in the models operator. This ensures that each model is properly linked to exactly one shield dataset, maintaining data integrity and preventing misconfiguration. The change includes updates to the operator logic and comprehensive test coverage for both dataset and model lifecycle workflows.
userImpact: Users can now rely on strict model-to-shield-dataset coupling, ensuring consistent data protection configurations and preventing orphaned or mis-linked model-dataset pairs.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2220 final task apis
summary: Implements task state cache storage in the database to provide the final API needed for GenAI model management in the UI. Adds database migration for task storage cache, expands the model tasks router and repository to support retrieving cached task state, and includes comprehensive integration tests for the new task management functionality.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve cached task state from the backend, enabling the frontend to display and manage GenAI model task status and lifecycle information.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2282: Set up assigning permissions to first user
summary: Implemented pending invitation tracking and acceptance workflow for the first user in an organization. When a user accepts an invitation via the `accept-invite` endpoint, they are now automatically assigned organization superuser permissions and redirected to the workspace creation flow. This includes new database migrations, invitation repositories, operators, and comprehensive test coverage.
userImpact: Users who accept invitations are now properly provisioned with organization permissions and guided through the workspace creation process on first login.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.37.26 → 1.37.27), pydantic (2.11.1 → 2.11.2), and typing_extensions (4.13.0 → 4.13.1). These are routine patch-level updates that bring in bug fixes and minor enhancements from upstream projects.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates multiple non-major dependencies including authlib, boto3, cfn-lint, pydantic_core, and spicedb to their latest patch versions. Additionally, Python base images across all versions (3.8 through 3.12) are updated to Python 3.13-alpine, bringing in the latest Python runtime with security fixes and improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal dependency maintenance to ensure security patches and stability.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2221 Part 2 implement task jobs
summary: Implements task job types and executors for the new shield task management feature, building on the task management jobs foundation. The implementation refactors to use API models instead of auto-generated client types for parsing shield rule configurations, avoiding deserialization issues with union types. Task jobs now support creating, fetching, and managing shield tasks with various rule types (Keyword, Regex, PII) through the control plane and shield APIs.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage shield tasks through the task management API with support for keyword, regex, and PII detection rules.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including authzed/spicedb (v1.41.0 → v1.42.0), boto3 (1.37.24 → 1.37.25), timescaledb (2.19.0 → 2.19.1), and types-PyYAML to the latest patch versions. These are routine dependency maintenance updates to keep the project current with upstream improvements and fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2221 Part 1 Create separate job types for API-facing jobs vs internal jobs
summary: This MR separates job type definitions into `PostJobKind` for API-facing jobs and `PostJobSpecType` for internal jobs, preventing internal jobs like `fetch_data` from being submitted through the public POST jobs API. This lays the foundation for adding task management jobs that should only be accessible through dedicated task management endpoints.
userImpact: The POST jobs API endpoint no longer exposes internal job types like `fetch_data`, ensuring they can only be submitted through their designated internal endpoints.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2222 part 2 add task management to shield connector
summary: Adds task management functions to the Shield connector, extending the functionality introduced in a previous merge request. This includes support for creating, reading, updating, and deleting tasks through the Shield API, with comprehensive unit and integration tests to ensure reliability.
userImpact: Users can now manage tasks directly through the Shield connector, enabling full CRUD operations for task management workflows.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Feat/ssm onprem replacement
summary: Replaced the in-memory secret store with a PostgreSQL-backed implementation and integrated AWS Secrets Manager support for on-premises deployments. This enables secure, persistent storage of connector and webhook secrets across both cloud and on-premises environments, eliminating reliance on volatile in-memory storage.
userImpact: Users can now securely store and retrieve secrets using AWS Secrets Manager in cloud deployments and PostgreSQL storage in on-premises deployments, with automatic secret persistence across application restarts.
author: kacperek0

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated patch versions of non-major dependencies including boto3, fsspec, gcsfs, s3fs, and types-psutil. These updates contain bug fixes and API enhancements from upstream projects with no breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2222 part 1, convert shield to use generated API client
summary: Shield connector now uses a generated OpenAPI client instead of manual API calls, improving maintainability and type safety. The generated client code is committed to the repository with plans to eventually auto-generate from the latest OpenAPI spec in CI/CD. This first part converts existing functionality to use the new client; subsequent work will add new task-management features.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved Shield connector reliability and maintainability; the connector's API interactions are now type-safe and easier to keep in sync with Shield's OpenAPI specification.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2220 add onboarding_identifier to model resource and APIs
summary: Added `onboarding_identifier` field to the model resource and exposed it through POST model and GET model/models APIs. This field allows the frontend to label models during the GenAI onboarding flow, enabling easy lookup after the creation job completes. Database migration and API models have been updated to support this functionality.
userImpact: Frontend can now assign and retrieve an onboarding_identifier when creating and fetching models during the GenAI onboarding flow.
author: ianmcgraw

title: feat: push data plane images to dockerhub
summary: Added automation to push Data Plane images to Docker Hub during release deployments. The CI pipeline now tags and publishes Data Plane images to Docker Hub with both release version tags and "latest" tags (limited to production releases), enabling users to pull pre-built Data Plane images from Docker Hub instead of relying on internal registries.
userImpact: Users can now pull Data Plane images directly from Docker Hub, with the latest production-ready images available under the "latest" tag.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates eight non-major dependencies to their latest patch versions, including alembic, boto3, fsspec, gcsfs, moto, pydantic, s3fs, and tokencost. All updates are patch-level increments that include bug fixes and minor improvements to underlying libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2073 GenAI connector lifecycle tech design
summary: Added technical design documentation for workspace-level connector support in the GenAI engine. This design explores how connectors could be managed at the workspace level to enable GenAI usage across all projects in a workspace, including API key management strategies and control plane architecture considerations.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is technical design documentation for future workspace-level connector support.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the codebase, including SQLAlchemy (2.0.39 → 2.0.40), boto3 (1.37.21 → 1.37.22), pydantic (2.10.6 → 2.11.0), and types-requests patches. These updates bring minor performance improvements, bug fixes, and AWS service API enhancements from upstream projects.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2220 initial task management API facade
summary: Adds the initial API facade for task management in Shield, enabling the control plane to manage tasks. This includes task CRUD endpoints, permission models, schema updates, and comprehensive test coverage. The endpoints return hardcoded responses and do not yet create actual backend jobs or resources.
userImpact: Users can now access task management APIs through the Shield control plane to manage tasks, with frontend clients able to use the typed API definitions from the generated OpenAPI schema.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2258 make metric names more user friendly
summary: Metric names in aggregations now use explicit `display_name` properties instead of auto-derived class names, making them significantly more user-friendly and readable in the UI. This improves the user experience when viewing metrics and searching for available metrics when configuring models.
userImpact: Users can now see clearly readable metric names in the UI and easily search for metrics when adding them to models, replacing previously cryptic class-based names.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 to v1.37.21 and pydantic_core to v2.33.0. boto3 patch includes API improvements for AWS Arc zonal shifts, Direct Connect gateway tagging, and media services. pydantic_core minor version adds extra keys validation for model fields and reverts a by_alias default value change.
userImpact: Users benefit from latest AWS API improvements and pydantic data validation enhancements through updated dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2245 ship data plane latest tag
summary: Updated CI/CD pipeline to automatically tag the latest data plane image deployed to SaaS production with the "latest" tag. This ensures the production version is easily accessible for deployments without requiring manual tagging.
userImpact: The data plane latest image tag is now automatically updated whenever a new version is deployed to production, simplifying deployment workflows.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2196: Make schema inspection work with no recent data
summary: Schema inspection now works when recent data is unavailable by progressively expanding the time window to fetch historical data if needed. When data is found, results are returned in descending order by default to prioritize the most recent schema information, making the job more resilient to gaps in recent data collection.
userImpact: Schema inference jobs now succeed even when datasets have no recent data by automatically expanding the lookback window, ensuring schema detection works reliably across datasets with varying data freshness.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2249: Fix org name validation
summary: Fixed organization name validation to align with Keycloak's RFC 3986-compliant regex pattern. The validation now accepts international characters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores—anything that doesn't match Keycloak's reserved character pattern—making organization names more flexible for SaaS deployments while maintaining URL-friendliness.
userImpact: Users can now create organizations with international characters, numbers, and hyphens in the organization name, matching Keycloak's validation rules and enabling broader international support.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3, cfn-lint, google-cloud-bigquery, types-PyYAML, types-pytz, and typing_extensions to their latest patch and minor versions. This ensures the project stays current with upstream improvements and security patches.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2241 latest upsolve export
summary: Updated the upsolve export to fix corruption in filter state that was preventing the link filters popup from displaying correctly on the regression dashboard. The latest export from Upsolve resolves the issue.
userImpact: Users can now open the link filters popup on the regression dashboard without issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2177: User permissions tech design
summary: Added technical design documentation for user permissions and sign-up flow, including sequence diagrams to clarify state creation in Arthur DB vs Keycloak, redirect handling, and backend API interactions. The design addresses permission assignment during user registration and organization invitation flows through frontend-mediated OAuth handshakes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: [chore] Update SpiceDB 1.41.0
summary: Upgraded SpiceDB from the previous version to 1.41.0 across all deployment environments (dev, prod, and STS platforms). The update was applied to configuration files in AWS dev, prod, axios-sts, and expel-sts platform environments, as well as the update script itself.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest SpiceDB 1.41.0 features, improvements, and security patches across all platform deployments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies to their latest minor and patch versions, including Faker (37.0.2→37.1.0), boto3 (1.37.18→1.37.19), pytest-asyncio (0.25.3→0.26.0), authzed/spicedb (v1.40.1→v1.41.0), and timescaledb (2.18.2→2.19.0). These updates bring bug fixes, new features, and improved type annotations across the dependency stack.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved test infrastructure with enhanced pytest-asyncio features, updated AWS SDK capabilities, and the latest bug fixes and improvements from upstream dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2229 and UP-2217 add JSON data type
summary: Added JSON data type support to handle DuckDB schema inference for multi-typed lists and complex nested structures that cannot be mapped to standard list or structure types. This enables successful data onboarding for datasets with heterogeneous arrays (e.g., mixed string and numeric values). Also fixed CI/CD pipeline configuration to ensure app and data plane tests run correctly when scope_common code changes.
userImpact: Users can now successfully onboard datasets with multi-typed nested data structures that previously failed schema inference, such as Expel's model dataset containing mixed-type arrays.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2224 update alert rule query guides
summary: Updated alert rule query guides and related documentation that were missed in a previous MR. Changes include updates to the demo code and product documentation to keep alert rule query references current.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2201: Include jobs referencing datasets using models in model activity log
summary: Extended the model activity log to include jobs that reference datasets using the model, not just jobs that directly use the model. This provides a more complete view of model usage throughout the system by capturing indirect dependencies through datasets.
userImpact: Users can now see all jobs that depend on a model via dataset references in the model activity log, providing better visibility into model usage and impact.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2167: Update CFTs with email parameters
summary: Updated CloudFormation templates across multiple AWS deployment environments (dev, prod, axios-sts, expel-sts) to support email configuration parameters. Added placeholder environment variable definitions to prevent deployment script failures when parameters are not yet configured in all environments.
userImpact: Email configuration parameters can now be managed and toggled via environment-specific config files across deployed Scope platform environments.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2224 convert alert rule required fields
summary: Alert rule validation now requires `metric_timestamp` and `metric_value` columns instead of `timestamp` and `value`. This resolves naming conflicts where the original column names collided with table columns, causing bugs in user queries. A query error sanitizer was added to prevent leaking internal implementation details in error messages.
userImpact: Users must update their alert rule queries to use `metric_timestamp` and `metric_value` columns instead of `timestamp` and `value` to avoid naming conflicts.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Deploy multi tenancy feature branch
summary: This merge request deploys the multi-tenancy feature branch, implementing Keycloak organization support and refactoring authentication to use organization names instead of realms. Changes include automatic org scope assignment to new clients, Keycloak organization creation with default org configuration, and updates to user, organization, and authorization models to support multi-tenant operations.
userImpact: Users can now operate within isolated organization contexts with automatic Keycloak org provisioning and proper scope management for multi-tenant deployments.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2225 fix hallucination chart
summary: Fixed the hallucination chart query by wrapping the time range filter in brackets to properly conditionally include the date filter. The chart now correctly filters metrics data by the selected date range instead of returning incomplete or incorrect results.
userImpact: Users can now view accurate hallucination metrics filtered by their selected date range without missing or incorrect data.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] prevent data plane local dev script from overwriting client secret if already set
summary: The data plane local development start script was overwriting the client secret variable unconditionally, breaking runs against development environments. This change adds a check to preserve an already-set client secret instead of overwriting it.
userImpact: Developers can now run the data plane local development script against dev environments without losing their existing client secret configuration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: update shield API key
summary: Updated Shield API key in integration test configuration. This maintenance change ensures tests continue to use valid credentials for Shield service integration.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: UP-2172: Create sign up endpoint
summary: Introduces a new sign-up endpoint that allows users to register and create organizations. The endpoint handles user creation, organization initialization, authentication client setup, and email verification, streamlining the onboarding process for new users.
userImpact: Users can now sign up for new accounts and organizations through a dedicated registration endpoint, receiving verification emails and being redirected to the home page upon acceptance.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2210 upgrade upsolve
summary: Updated upsolve backend to version 0.2.31. The export has no functional changes; this upgrade brings in the latest version of the upsolve dependency. Minor test fixes and Dockerfile updates were included to ensure compatibility.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest upsolve backend improvements and bug fixes included in version 0.2.31.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2209 make numeric sketch metrics use continuous tag
summary: Numeric sketch metrics now respect continuous tags instead of auto-applying to all INT and FLOAT columns. This fixes an issue where categorical INT columns (with fewer than 10 unique values) were incorrectly receiving continuous metrics like PSI, which require 10 distinct buckets. Tags now determine whether numeric columns receive sketch aggregation (for continuous data) or categorical count metrics (for categorical data).
userImpact: Users can now correctly tag INT columns as categorical or continuous, and the platform will automatically assign appropriate metrics—sketch aggregation for continuous columns and categorical count for categorical columns—improving data drift detection accuracy.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2073 tech design for shield state sync
summary: Added technical design documentation for Shield state synchronization with the control plane, including API contracts, endpoint specifications, and implementation options discussed at the March 2025 offsite. The documentation covers task state management, rule configuration, and the API facade structure for syncing Shield tasks with models.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] increase auth replicas to two
summary: Increased auth service replicas from one to two across all ECS environments to address frequent zero-replica alerts during maintenance windows. This ensures continued availability when one replica is down for updates or maintenance.
userImpact: Auth service is now more resilient to maintenance operations, reducing service availability risk during replica updates.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2167, UP-2169: add config for enabling emails and forgot password flow
summary: Added configuration options to enable email functionality and the forgot password flow in Keycloak. The changes support external SMTP providers (such as AWS SES) with configurable credentials, SSL/StartTLS settings, and realm-level email configuration. Bootstrap logic was updated to wire these settings into the Keycloak realm during initialization.
userImpact: Administrators can now enable email sending and forgot password flows in their Keycloak instances by configuring SMTP credentials and settings through CloudFormation parameters and environment variables.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1698 add axios ground truth dataset
summary: Added scripts for creating the Axios ground truth dataset, including a schema fetching utility and onboarding workflow. These scripts support the data preparation process for Axios integration.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Onprem functionality with OpenShift
summary: Added comprehensive Helm charts for deploying Scope to on-premise environments running OpenShift. The implementation includes both control plane and data plane Helm charts with templates for all core components including Keycloak, SpiceDB, Redis, app plane services, and frontend, along with ingress, service discovery, and configuration management.
userImpact: Users can now deploy Scope to on-premise OpenShift environments using production-ready Helm charts with configurable components, service discovery, and high-availability settings.
author: kacperek0

title: Patch token cost calculation for nulls
summary: Fixed token cost calculation to handle null values that can occur from legacy Shield instances that don't send token data. The fix uses COALESCE to default null token counts to 0, preventing exceptions when processing aggregation queries and ensuring the aggregation layer remains resilient to null values returned from upstream services.
userImpact: Token cost calculations no longer fail when encountering null token values from legacy or broken Shield instances.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Update all non-major dependencies

All e2e tests passed with only known flaky failures observed.

The system now validates user count before allowing user removal, ensuring organizations always retain at least one member. This includes supporting infrastructure for organization deletion and new sign-up endpoints.
userImpact: Users can no longer accidentally remove the last member from an organization, preventing orphaned orgs without administrators.

title: UP-2151: remove blocking deletion of workspaces with data planes
summary: Removed the blocking validation that prevented deletion of workspaces containing data planes. Previously, workspaces with data planes could not be deleted; now the system automatically cascades deletion to all associated data planes when a workspace is deleted. This change aligns with the deletion patterns required for organization deletion support.
userImpact: Users can now delete workspaces that contain data planes, with all associated data planes being automatically deleted in the process.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2182: Fix data plane deletion permissions handling
summary: Fixed data plane deletion permissions handling across multiple API routers and repository layers. The changes ensure proper authorization checks are performed when deleting data planes, preventing unauthorized deletions and enforcing correct permission validation in the base operator and repositories.
userImpact: Users can now only delete data planes they have permission to delete; unauthorized deletion attempts are properly rejected.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2178: Create data plane management role
summary: Introduces a new "Engine Manager" role for data plane management at the workspace level. This role is based on workspace reader permissions and includes data plane management capabilities. A cross-service migration mechanism was added to simultaneously update both PostgreSQL and SpiceDB resources during bootstrap, with a migration tracking table to prevent re-execution.
userImpact: Users can now be assigned an Engine Manager role to manage data planes within a workspace, with appropriate read permissions to navigate the data plane management UI.
author: alex380125

title: NO-REF: Pin spicedb version in docker compose to the version in ECS
summary: Pinned SpiceDB version in docker-compose.yaml to match the version deployed in ECS. A newer SpiceDB version causes bootstrap job failures during organization creation, though the job eventually succeeds after retries. This temporary fix maintains consistency between local development and production environments while a deeper investigation into the bootstrap behavior is tracked separately.
userImpact: Development environments now use the same stable SpiceDB version as production, preventing bootstrap failures when running docker-compose locally.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Routine dependency updates for non-major versions across multiple packages including Faker, Jinja2, boto3, cfn-lint, duckdb, pydantic_core, and types-requests. Notable security fix in Jinja2 3.1.6 addresses sandbox attribute lookup bypass.

summary: Added a new delete organization endpoint that removes an organization and all associated workspaces, projects, and data planes. The implementation includes proper authorization checks, rollback strategy for failures, and comprehensive test coverage to ensure safe cascade deletion of organization resources.
userImpact: Users can now delete organizations through the API, with all dependent resources (workspaces, projects, and data planes) automatically cleaned up in a transactional manner.

title: UP-2161: disallow deleting the last user in the org
summary: Added validation to prevent deletion of the last user in an organization, which could leave an org in an unusable state. The fix ensures that at least one user must remain in every organization at all times. Tests were updated to verify the new validation behavior and edge cases.
userImpact: Users can no longer accidentally delete the last user in their organization, preventing potential account lockout scenarios.

title: UP-2149: Refactor PostUser to use org name instead of realm
summary: Refactored PostUser and related authentication models to use organization names instead of realms. This change simplifies the user and service account management flow by aligning the authentication system with the organization-based model. The refactor includes updates to API models, Keycloak client integration, repositories, and routers to consistently use organization names throughout the authentication and authorization layers.
userImpact: Service accounts and users are now correctly associated with organizations by name instead of realms, improving consistency in user and organization management across the platform.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2157: Automatically add org scope to new clients and create keycloak org with default org
summary: Automatically add org scope to new Keycloak clients and create a Keycloak organization corresponding to the default Arthur org during bootstrap. This enables proper organization-scoped authentication and prepares the system for user sign-up flows where organizations are created automatically with their first user.
userImpact: New clients are now automatically configured with organization scope, and the default organization is created in Keycloak during system bootstrap, enabling organization-aware authentication flows.

title: UP-2065: Add check in time to data planes table

This includes database migration, API and internal model updates, and operator logic to record the timestamp during job dequeueing operations. The field enables monitoring of data plane activity and health status.
userImpact: Users can now monitor when each data plane last checked in with the system, providing visibility into data plane activity and helping identify inactive or stalled data planes.

summary: Added URL validation to the shield connector to ensure URLs are properly formatted before use. The connector now validates the URL structure and ignores any path component if specified, improving robustness when connecting to shield services.

title: UP-2051: Add Account Creation Feature Flag
summary: Added an account creation feature flag to allow controlled rollout of account creation functionality across environments. The flag defaults to false and can be enabled selectively per environment. This MR also removes the legacy CloudFormation directory that is no longer in use, consolidating infrastructure-as-code definitions under the new cloudformation_stacks directory.
userImpact: Users in designated environments can now have account creation functionality enabled or disabled dynamically via feature flag, enabling safer progressive rollout and testing of new account creation flows.

title: UP-2054: Multi-tenancy tech design for mapping from tenants to keycloak and arthur resources
summary: This merge request documents the technical design for multi-tenancy architecture, establishing how tenants map to Keycloak realms and Arthur resources. The design decides to use Arthur Organizations as tenants for both free and enterprise users with a single Keycloak realm, avoiding immediate account migration requirements and enabling straightforward customer onboarding to paid plans. Future work is deferred for enterprise IdP support and multi-organization membership.
userImpact: No direct user impact — this is technical documentation establishing the foundation for multi-tenancy support.

Additionally, PSI chart output columns in upsolve were renamed from `bucket` and `PSI` to `timestamp` and `value` for better compatibility with alert rule queries.

summary: Added ordering priority fields to connector schema definitions, allowing fields to be sorted consistently across API responses. A new database migration adds priority columns to the schema, while connector definitions across AWS, BigQuery, GCS, Google, S3, Shield and bucket-based connectors have been updated with explicit priority values to control field ordering in responses.
userImpact: Connector schema fields are now returned in a consistent, predictable order based on their configured priorities.

title: UP-1935: remove local_fs constant
summary: Removes the unused `LOCAL_FS` constant from the ConnectorType enum following the removal of local filesystem connector support logic in a previous migration. The constant is no longer needed as the database migration now handles deletion of local_fs connector schemas directly via SQL rather than through the API.

title: UP-1674: auto-masking for column conflicts
summary: Implemented auto-masking for column conflicts when joining datasets. The system now automatically handles schema merging for joined datasets and prevents conflicting column names through alias masking. Validation has been added to ensure joined datasets cannot use the connector-based dataset endpoint, and dataset join specifications are properly validated.
userImpact: Users can now join datasets without manually resolving column name conflicts—the system automatically applies alias masks to prevent collisions and merges schemas from the joined datasets.

The ConnectorType constant will be removed in a follow-up ticket after this deploys.

The updates include AWS API changes in boto3 for Cloud9, DLM, EC2, Greengrassv2, MediaLive, and RDS, along with maintenance releases for cfn-lint and pglast.

title: UP-1888 BAM demo data
summary: Added a new Mergers and Acquisitions demo dataset for BAM customers, including a dedicated onboarding script and dataset generation configuration. Enhanced the data generation script to support variable numbers of inferences per day, enabling more flexible demo scenarios.

title: Remove dbops part1
summary: Removed the dbops endpoint exposure and migrated database bootstrap operations to init containers instead of platform bootstrap steps. The upsolve service now runs bootstrap migrations via an init container on both legacy and platform stacks, simplifying the bootstrap process and removing environment variable configuration overhead.
userImpact: Database bootstrap operations are now handled automatically via init containers during deployment, eliminating the need for separate bootstrap steps and the deprecated dbops endpoint.

title: UP-1791: Introduce minimal org member role
summary: Introduces a minimal org member role that is automatically granted to users upon creation via the `post_organization_users` endpoint. This role is also applied to users created through SSO (Keycloak) on first login. The minimal org member role grants users permissions to view basic organization information and their own user details, including their assigned roles.
userImpact: New users created in organizations now automatically receive a minimal org member role, allowing them to see their user information and assigned roles rather than encountering 404 errors.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1689 - update model APIs to accept & return a list of dataset IDs
summary: Models now support association with multiple datasets instead of a single dataset. The Model API has been updated to accept and return a list of dataset IDs via a new database join table, and aggregation matching now unions results across all associated datasets during model onboarding.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage models that monitor multiple datasets simultaneously, with aggregation results combined across all associated datasets.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Routine dependency updates to boto3 (1.35.81), moto (5.0.23), and uvicorn (0.34.0). Updates include patch versions for boto3 and moto with API improvements for AWS services, and a minor version bump for uvicorn. These are non-major version updates with no breaking changes.

title: UP-1893 fix schema inference and metrics calculation on cols with spaces
summary: Schema inference and metrics calculation failed when column names contained spaces because column identifiers were not properly quoted in SQL queries. This fix applies proper SQL identifier escaping throughout schema inference, metrics aggregation functions, and data retrieval to correctly handle columns like "MA History".
userImpact: Users can now successfully work with datasets that have spaces or special characters in column names; schema inference and metric calculations will no longer fail on such columns.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1890 fix raw data retrieval with nans
summary: Fixed raw data retrieval for Expel data sources that contain NaN values by encoding them as JSON-compliant strings. The frontend was unable to parse invalid JSON containing NaN symbols, causing data retrieval to fail. Frontend support for string-encoded NaN values was already implemented.
userImpact: Raw data retrieval now works correctly for data sources containing NaN values, resolving failures in Expel data ingestion.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1798: Support updating users
summary: Split the user `name` field into separate `first_name` and `last_name` fields to better align with identity provider schemas and support reliable user patching. Added Keycloak scopes to include both fields in user tokens, with service and data plane accounts storing their full name in `first_name`. Also adds support for setting the `picture` attribute in the database.
userImpact: Users can now update user profile information (first name, last name, picture) via PATCH endpoints; consumers must migrate from `name` to `first_name` and `last_name` fields.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1581 upsolve custom chart docs
summary: Added comprehensive user-facing documentation for configuring custom charts in dashboards, including step-by-step instructions, example SQL queries, and visual diagrams. This documentation enables users to understand how to create and configure custom charts following the recent Upsolve upgrade that added user chart creation capabilities.
userImpact: Users can now reference clear documentation to configure custom charts with proper SQL syntax and visual examples of the configuration process.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1896 fix binary classification dashboard
summary: Fixed the binary classification dashboard to properly display days with missing data by adopting the time_bucket_gapfill function in queries, which fills in zero values for gaps instead of omitting them. Also corrected styling issues on graphs to prevent overflow on curved visualizations, ensuring consistent and accurate data representation across all dashboard graphs.
userImpact: Users now see accurate timeline visualization with all days displayed, including those with no data, and graphs no longer overflow on the dashboard.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Routine dependency updates for non-major versions of authzed/spicedb, boto3, cfn-lint, and pytest-asyncio. This MR includes patch-level updates and a minor version bump for pytest-asyncio to maintain compatibility and receive upstream improvements.

summary: The data plane health check port has been moved from 8080 to 7492 to eliminate port conflicts with Keycloak during local development. This prevents service startup failures when running the full stack locally.
userImpact: Users running the system locally no longer experience port conflicts between the data plane health check and Keycloak authentication service.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1891 upgrade datasketches 5.1.1 to use prebuilt wheels for python 3.13
summary: Upgraded datasketches dependency to 5.1.1 and removed Docker build dependencies now that official Python 3.13 wheels are available. This simplifies the dependency resolution process and ensures all compiled packages (duckdb, pyarrow, datasketches) now use prebuilt wheels across all supported Python versions.
userImpact: Faster installation times and reduced build complexity for Python 3.13 environments by leveraging official prebuilt wheels instead of building from source.

title: UP-1892 fix bigquery connector
summary: Fixed BigQuery connector initialization by correctly extracting credentials from the tuple returned by `load_credentials_from_dict()`. Previously, the tuple itself was being passed as credentials instead of extracting the first item, causing authentication failures.
userImpact: BigQuery connector now authenticates correctly and can successfully initialize BigQuery clients.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.78 → 1.35.79), opentelemetry-exporter-otlp (1.28.2 → 1.29.0), and opentelemetry-sdk (1.28.2 → 1.29.0). This includes AWS API improvements for artifact, cloudtrail, cognito-idp, emr-serverless, and other services, along with minor OpenTelemetry enhancements.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency maintenance and internal system updates.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1794 fix orphaned scheduled jobs when models are deleted
summary: Fixed orphaned scheduled jobs when models are deleted by cleaning up associated job schedules during model deletion. Moved the foreign key constraint for job schedules to the job_schedules table to enforce referential integrity and prevent orphaned schedules without a parent model.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs are now properly cleaned up when a model is deleted, preventing orphaned job schedules and associated clutter in the system.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1799: Support reseting user credentials
summary: Added support for resetting user credentials in the authentication system. The implementation extends the user API with credential reset functionality, updates Keycloak and static configuration clients to handle credential rotation, and includes new authorization rules and database schema definitions to manage the reset operation securely.
userImpact: Users can now reset their credentials through the API, enabling credential rotation and recovery workflows.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1792: Lazily delete users
summary: Implemented lazy user deletion to improve data retention and compliance workflows. Users are now marked as deleted rather than immediately removed from the database, allowing for recovery windows and maintaining referential integrity. Deleted users are prevented from making API requests—tokens from deleted accounts return 401 Unauthorized.
userImpact: Users can now securely delete accounts with a recovery period, and deleted user tokens are immediately invalidated on API requests.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.77 to 1.35.78 and cfn-lint from 1.21.0 to 1.22.0. These are routine dependency updates that include bug fixes and documentation improvements from upstream projects.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: feat: automatically create aws deployment tag and also a success tag
summary: Implemented automatic AWS deployment and success tag creation in the CI/CD pipeline. When a new release is created from main, it now automatically deploys to AWS Dev. Upon successful deployment, a success tag is automatically created to track the last successful version, enabling future scheduled AWS Prod deployments to use this reference.
userImpact: Releases from main now automatically deploy to AWS Dev and track successful deployments with tags, enabling reliable promotion to AWS Prod based on the last known good version.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: automatically bump the patch version if no release version is specified
summary: The release process now automatically bumps the patch version when no explicit release version is specified. This simplifies version management by eliminating the need to manually specify patch version increments for routine releases. The deployment GitLab CI/CD configuration was also corrected to fix a YAML syntax issue.
userImpact: Release workflows no longer require users to specify a patch version increment—the system will automatically apply one if no version is explicitly provided.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore: rename expel_sts to expel-sts-platform
summary: Renamed the deployment environment and pipeline configuration from expel_sts to expel-sts-platform for naming consistency across environments and deployment commands. This is a non-destructive change affecting only deployment automation paths and folder structures, with no impact to deployed infrastructure.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore: rename aws dev platform_v4 to aws-dev-platform to match aws-prod-platform
summary: Renamed the AWS development platform deployment from `platform_v4` to `aws-dev-platform` to establish consistent naming convention with the production platform counterpart `aws-prod-platform`. This change updates deployment scripts, CI configuration, and related documentation to use the new standardized name.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Developers working with AWS deployment scripts should use the new `aws-dev-platform` naming convention instead of `platform_v4`.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1792: Fix db manager deletion bug
summary: Fixed an issue where the user deletion flow failed to delete relationships in SpiceDB. The problem occurred because `db_manager.flush()` was moving changed objects from memory to the transaction buffer, causing SQLAlchemy's weak reference behavior to clear the delete queue even though deletions hadn't been committed to SpiceDB. The fix enqueues copies of deleted objects to the delete queues instead of the original objects, ensuring deletions persist through flush operations.
userImpact: Users can now be successfully deleted from groups, and their relationships are correctly removed from SpiceDB.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.76→1.35.77), cfn-lint (1.20.2→1.21.0), and authzed/spicedb (1.38.1→1.39.0). These are routine dependency updates to incorporate bug fixes and minor enhancements from upstream packages.
userImpact: No direct user impact; routine dependency maintenance to keep tooling and libraries current.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1881 fix runaway scheduled jobs
summary: Fixed runaway scheduled jobs that were retrying indefinitely when encountering non-recoverable errors like deleted models or invalid configurations. Added a new PATCH API endpoint for jobs to update max retries and updated scheduled job error handling to automatically stop retries by capping max_attempts when unrecoverable errors are encountered.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs that fail due to deleted models or invalid configs will now automatically stop retrying instead of running indefinitely, eliminating runaway jobs that were causing performance issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1880 fix app plane health check
summary: A previous merge request configured the incorrect port for the app plane health check in the scope-dev cluster. This MR fixes the port configuration in the CloudFormation template for the app plane ECS deployment to ensure the health check can properly monitor the app plane service.
userImpact: App plane health checks now correctly target the proper port in the scope-dev cluster, restoring proper service health monitoring.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1880 data plane health check
summary: Added a health check endpoint to the data plane service that monitors the main event loop, preventing ECS from being unable to detect deadlocked processes. The fix also wraps API calls in exception handlers to prevent crashes and marks job runner threads as daemon processes to ensure proper cleanup if the main thread fails.
userImpact: ECS can now reliably detect and restart unhealthy data plane instances that would previously deadlock, and API errors no longer cause the data plane agent to crash.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve to enable user chart creation
summary: Upgraded Upsolve to version 0.2.14 and adapted the `time_bucket_gapfill` SQL function usage to accommodate upstream timezone manipulation changes that broke function bound inference. Explicit start and end timestamp parameters are now required when using the function to ensure it operates correctly.
userImpact: Users can now create and view charts as the time-bucketing functionality has been restored after the Upsolve upgrade.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1696: support model problem type and other tag updates
summary: Added support for model problem type tagging in the dataset and model APIs. This change introduces a new `model_problem_type` field to dataset schemas, model specifications, and related repositories, with database schema migration and comprehensive test coverage to ensure data consistency across the application.
userImpact: Users can now tag and retrieve model problem types associated with datasets and models through the API, enabling better classification and filtering of ML models in the system.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project: Mako (1.3.7→1.3.8), httpx (0.28.0→0.28.1), and NumPy (2.1.3→2.2.0). These updates include bug fixes for httpx SSL handling and new NumPy features like matvec/vecmat functions, along with improved support for free-threaded Python.
userImpact: No direct user impact; these are routine dependency updates to bring in upstream bug fixes and improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1858: Support fetch data on available datasets
summary: Added support for fetching raw data from available datasets, enabling users to retrieve and validate data from manually onboarded datasets through S3, GCS, and BigQuery connectors. This capability extends raw data retrieval functionality to available datasets, complementing the new manual dataset onboarding flows for both listable and non-listable connectors.
userImpact: Users can now fetch and preview raw data from available datasets they've manually onboarded, allowing them to validate that the dataset locator points to the correct data before full integration.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1823 add dataset locator to connector product docs
summary: Added documentation for dataset locator functionality in connector product docs. This enhancement clarifies how users can identify and locate datasets when working with various connectors, improving the onboarding and troubleshooting experience.
userImpact: Users can now reference dataset locator guidance in the connector documentation to better understand how to find and identify datasets within their connectors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1866 remove platform command in docs
summary: Removed outdated platform command reference from documentation that was missed in a previous cleanup. This ensures the README accurately reflects the current command set and prevents user confusion from deprecated documentation.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Add CloudWatch Dashboards
summary: Adds CloudWatch Dashboards for platform monitoring by introducing a new CloudFormation template with comprehensive dashboard widgets and integrating dashboard deployment into the scope deployment script. This enables operators to visualize key metrics and system health across the platform infrastructure.
userImpact: Operators can now view real-time CloudWatch dashboards displaying platform metrics including response codes, I/O performance, and health checks for better system observability.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including anyio (4.6.2.post1 → 4.7.0), redis (5.2.0 → 5.2.1), and types-python-dateutil (2.9.0.20241003 → 2.9.0.20241206). These updates include bug fixes in redis-py for tempfile usage and improvements to anyio's task handling, event adapters, and type annotations.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1857: Support overview page of joined dataset
summary: Added support for displaying the overview page of joined datasets. The implementation includes updates to API models, internal models, and repository layers to properly handle joined dataset metadata. Query performance was optimized by converting relationships to use selectin loading to avoid N+1 query problems.
userImpact: Users can now view overview information for joined datasets through the API, including details about the component datasets and join specifications.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update dependency types-croniter to v5
summary: Updated types-croniter type stubs from version 4.0.0.20241030 to 5.0.1.20241205. This is a routine dependency maintenance update to keep type definitions aligned with the croniter library.

author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: UP-1866 build improvements
summary: Optimized Docker build process by migrating from Alpine to Debian Slim images, removing unnecessary dependencies from scope common, and fixing the OpenAPI client generation script. This enables native ARM architecture support and reduces build times from 20+ minutes to ~6 minutes total (2.5 min app plane, 3.5 min data plane) by leveraging pre-built wheels for key dependencies like DuckDB and PyArrow.
userImpact: Docker Compose stack can now be run natively on ARM machines without requiring platform flags, and builds complete significantly faster on both x86 and ARM architectures.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1856: support datasets default model page view
summary: Added support for datasets default model page view by refactoring the dataset API response model and repository logic. The change updates how dataset objects are returned to clients, removing the connector_id field from responses while maintaining backward compatibility with existing dataset submission workflows.
userImpact: Users can now view datasets with a model-oriented page view, while existing integrations that use the dataset submission API remain unaffected.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1823 connector public docs
summary: Added comprehensive product documentation for connectors including setup instructions and permission requirements. This new documentation resource helps customers understand how to configure and use connectors with proper access controls.
userImpact: Customers can now access clear documentation on connector setup and the permissions required to use each connector type.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

userImpact: No direct user impact

title: [no-ref] fix expel deploy
summary: Fixed failing Expel deploy by correcting image registry prefixes in environment configuration files. The Expel deployment was attempting to pull the telemetry image from a non-existent `scope-dev` prefix in Nexus. Aligned Expel configuration with production settings to resolve the issue.
userImpact: Expel deployments now succeed without failing to pull container images.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1797 fix all user filters to include service accounts by default
summary: Service accounts were being filtered out by default from role binding and user lists, causing them to be missing in the UI. Updated all user filters across the application to include service accounts by default, and fixed a null client ID issue for service account users in the authentication configuration.
userImpact: Service accounts now appear in role binding and user lists in the UI, making them visible and manageable alongside standard users.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1797 service account script examples for Expel
summary: Added example scripts demonstrating how to create and configure service accounts with the SDK, specifically for Expel's onboarding workflow. The scripts show multiple approaches for granting permissions to service accounts, including inheriting from groups or assigning roles directly.
userImpact: Users can now reference working examples to set up and authenticate service accounts with the SDK for programmatic access.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: remove echo with unbound variable
summary: Removed an echo statement referencing an unbound variable that was causing deployment failures. This fixes a script error in the download_deployment_files.sh deployment script.
userImpact: Deployments will no longer fail due to unbound variable errors in the deployment script.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Deploy updates
summary: Updated deployment configuration and scripts to use nexus credentials from CI pipeline for AWS platform v4, remove unused environment variables and commented values, and implement semantic versioning for deployment versions. This streamlines credential management and deployment consistency across environments.

title: Deploy updates

userImpact: Deployments now correctly pull versioned container images based on commit tags, ensuring consistency across AWS platform v4, core, database, and scope deployments.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1757 remove release stage from release tags
summary: Removed the unused "release stage" concept from release tags and CI/CD pipeline configuration. This simplifies release versioning to align with PyPI requirements using semantic versioning semantics.

title: UP-1793 update auth version

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
