summary: Fixed a bug in the axios dataset generation where file names were not correctly matched to their corresponding inference IDs. This ensures dataset files are properly named and associated with the correct inference records.
userImpact: Dataset files are now correctly named and matched to their inference IDs, preventing data association errors.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.68 → 1.35.69) for AWS SDK improvements and pglast (6.10 → 6.11) for PostgreSQL parsing enhancements. These routine dependency updates maintain compatibility with the latest patch and minor versions of upstream libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1788: Add pagination to BigQuery connector
summary: Added pagination support to the BigQuery connector, enabling efficient retrieval of large datasets through configurable pagination options. The connector now validates that either pagination options or a time range filter is provided when reading data, raising an error if neither is specified. This prevents unbounded queries that could impact performance and resource usage.
userImpact: Users can now paginate through large BigQuery datasets efficiently by passing pagination options to the connector's read() method.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.67 to 1.35.68 and PostgreSQL from 16.5 to 16.6. These are routine non-major dependency updates that include AWS service API improvements and database maintenance patches.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1694: Finish S3 connector support for axios
summary: Completed S3 connector support for axios by adding multiple AWS authentication methods, extending bucket-based connectors to read parquet files, and refactoring authentication fields across AWS and Google connectors for reusability. This brings S3 connector feature parity with GCS and BigQuery connectors while fixing critical bugs in file fetching logic and schema inference for struct fields with special characters.
userImpact: Users can now authenticate S3 connectors using multiple AWS credential methods, read parquet files from S3 buckets, and access datasets with special characters in column names without schema inference failures.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1797 support user service accounts
summary: Add support for creating service account users that authenticate using client credentials (client_id and client_secret) instead of traditional user credentials. Service accounts are created via a new POST /api/v1/organization/service_accounts endpoint and are managed identically to regular users within the authorization system.
userImpact: Organizations can now create and manage service accounts for programmatic access, receiving client credentials that can be used for authentication in place of user credentials.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] add otel health check
summary: Added health checks for the OTEL collector service that was previously missing monitoring. Health check configuration has been added to CloudFormation templates and docker-compose configuration to ensure the OTEL collector is properly monitored for availability and operational status.
userImpact: Users can now monitor the health status of the OTEL collector service to ensure it is running correctly.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgraded Upsolve to the latest image and export version (0.2.12). This includes updates to the Dockerfile and database patch files to reflect the new version.
userImpact: Users will benefit from the latest Upsolve features, improvements, and bug fixes included in version 0.2.12.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.67), pydantic (2.10.1), and pydantic_core (2.27.1) to their latest patch versions. These updates bring bug fixes and minor improvements from upstream projects without introducing breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1589 - Lint housekeeping
summary: This MR includes a broad series of linting and type-safety improvements across the codebase. Changes include adding discriminators to JobSpec types for proper deserialization, making optional fields non-optional where they were never assigned null values, fixing import statements to use correct type sources, adding exception handling for virtual datasets without locators in connectors, and upgrading the OpenAPI generator version to resolve a prior bug.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is internal code cleanup that improves type safety and prevents edge cases with virtual datasets and job deserialization.
author: peter496

title: Add some documentation around aggregations and annotations
summary: Added comprehensive documentation for aggregations and annotations in the Dataplane-Concepts technical documentation. The new content includes explanations of aggregation functions, annotation structures, and how these concepts interact within the platform's data processing pipeline.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Documentation improvements help developers and implementers better understand platform concepts.
author: peter496

title: [no-ref] add health check for spicedb
summary: Added health check for SpiceDB in Docker Compose and CloudFormation configurations. This health check uses a direct TCP check instead of shell-based commands, since the SpiceDB image lacks a shell, ensuring the container status is properly monitored.
userImpact: SpiceDB container health is now monitored in deployments, enabling proper failure detection and orchestration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updates multiple non-major dependencies including boto3, uvicorn, opentelemetry-exporter-otlp, and spicedb to their latest patch versions, plus a minor version upgrade for pydantic from 2.9.2 to 2.10.0. This routine maintenance keeps the project aligned with the latest stable upstream releases.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1680 - Make GET jobs endpoint faster
summary: Fixed an N+1 query problem on the GET jobs endpoint caused by polymorphic inheritance. The endpoint was issuing a separate database query for each job spec to retrieve type-specific columns. By using SQLAlchemy's selectin_polymorphic, all job spec data is now loaded in a single efficient query, reducing endpoint latency from 500-1000ms to near-instant.
userImpact: The project homepage activity logs now load significantly faster, with query times reduced from 500-1000ms to near-instantaneous.
author: peter496

title: UP-1680 - Further reduce role binding api endpoint latencies
summary: Further optimized role binding API endpoint latencies by applying query optimization techniques to all role binding GET operations. Building on previous work from MR !750, this change reduces retrieval latency for role bindings (particularly important for large organizations with many users and groups) from ~8 seconds to ~4 seconds in development environments, with projected improvements from ~6s to sub-300ms in production.
userImpact: Users will experience significantly faster response times when retrieving role bindings, especially in organizations with large user counts and group sizes.
author: peter496

title: Revert "[no-ref] add health check for spicedb"
summary: Reverts the addition of a health check for SpiceDB in the CloudFormation templates. The health check configuration has been removed from both the arthur-scope-spicedb-on-ecs templates.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] add health check for spicedb
summary: Added ECS health checks for the SpiceDB container to enable proper health monitoring. The health check uses the grpc_health_probe command to verify that SpiceDB is in a SERVING state on port 50051, ensuring the service can be properly monitored and managed by ECS.
userImpact: SpiceDB health status is now monitored by ECS, enabling automatic recovery and better observability of the service health.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1566 add script to build sdk wheel
summary: Added a build script to package the Python client SDK as a wheel file for distribution to Expel. This includes a new build_wheel.sh script and corresponding .gitignore entries to manage build artifacts.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.65
summary: Updated boto3 dependency from v1.35.64 to v1.35.65. This patch release includes upstream API changes for AWS services including EC2, ECS, EFS, Glue, and others, which are picked up automatically by the boto3 SDK.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is a routine dependency update that ensures access to the latest AWS API changes through boto3.

title: fix: do not run build, bump, openapi steps for intents release and deploy
summary: Fixed CI/CD pipeline to skip build, bump, and OpenAPI generation steps for intents release and deploy workflows. This prevents unnecessary processing during intent-specific releases and deployments, reducing pipeline execution time and avoiding redundant operations.
userImpact: Intent releases and deployments now skip irrelevant build and metadata steps, resulting in faster and more targeted CI/CD execution.

title: UP-1693: axios sample dataset
summary: Added a modular sample dataset generation system supporting multiple data sources (Expel and Axios), replacing the previous single-purpose generation script. The new structure includes base dataset classes, source-specific implementations, and a configurable CLI for generating test data with consistent patterns suitable for integration testing and demos.
userImpact: Users and developers can now generate sample datasets for multiple data sources (Expel and Axios) using a unified, flexible CLI tool, enabling better testing and demonstration scenarios.
author: alex380125

title: NO-REF - Update expel data to be less skewed
summary: Updated test data generation for the expel dataset to incorporate hour-level granularity alongside day-based generation. Previously, predictions were generated with a fixed range per day, resulting in artificially skewed data where entire days fell into a single classification. This change reduces skew by diversifying prediction values across hours within each day.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies including boto3, cfn-lint, OpenTelemetry packages, and pytest-httpx. These are routine dependency maintenance updates to keep the codebase current with upstream bug fixes and minor feature improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1566 Expel Onboarding scripts
summary: Refactored SDK authentication mechanisms to support both client credentials and device/user authentication flows with automatic token refresh and thread safety, enabling Expel to use Arthur APIs. Added onboarding scripts and documentation to help Expel integrate their models with the platform, including schema inference and connector management. Fixed Keycloak CLI client configuration to include the basic mapper required in Keycloak v25+.
userImpact: Users can now authenticate via client credentials or browser-based device authentication, and Expel can onboard their models using provided scripts that automate dataset creation and connector setup.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1819 Fix schema inference for timestamp types, and add auto tagging for primary timestamp
summary: Fixed schema inference to properly map DuckDB's TIMESTAMP type to Arthur's type system, which was causing schema inspection jobs to fail with a "Type TIMESTAMP not mappable" error. Additionally, the schema inferrer now automatically tags singular timestamp columns as PRIMARY_TIMESTAMP, reducing manual configuration overhead for users.
userImpact: Users can now successfully run schema inspection on datasets containing timestamp columns, and timestamp columns are automatically tagged as PRIMARY_TIMESTAMP when appropriate.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1695 - Add MAE aggregation function
summary: Added Mean Absolute Error (MAE) as a regression metric aggregation function. The implementation aggregates absolute errors and point counts over 5-minute intervals, enabling flexible bucketing at query time by dividing total absolute error by total point count for daily, hourly, or custom intervals.
userImpact: Users can now compute MAE regression metrics with configurable time-based aggregation buckets by leveraging the new aggregation function and associated schema tags.
author: peter496

title: UP-1819 fix bucket based schema inspection
summary: Fixed bucket-based connectors failing to read datasets lacking a primary timestamp tag during schema inspection. The logic now only warns when the timestamp tag is missing during data reads, and only fails if both the tag is absent and pagination options are not configured. This aligns behavior with the BigQuery connector and includes updated unit tests using schema inference instead of hardcoded schemas.
userImpact: Users can now successfully read available datasets from bucket-based connectors even without a primary timestamp tag, as long as pagination options are configured.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1730: connectors tech readme & tech debt
summary: Updated technical documentation for connectors with improved schema examples and corrected connector locator field handling. Added an Alembic migration to fix connector locator fields that were incorrectly stored, improving data integrity and alignment with the actual schema design.
userImpact: Connector locator fields are now correctly configured, ensuring proper task ID and file prefix assignment to dataset locators rather than connectors.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates several non-major dependencies to their latest versions: PyJWT (2.9.0 → 2.10.0), boto3 (1.35.62 → 1.35.63), moto (5.0.20 → 5.0.21), and python-keycloak (4.7.1 → 4.7.2). These updates bring bug fixes and minor enhancements from upstream projects.

title: NO-REF - Reuse threadpool per day

userImpact: Data processing operations complete more efficiently with reduced threadpool allocation overhead.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.59 to 1.35.60 and python-keycloak from 4.7.0 to 4.7.1 with patch-level dependency updates. These are routine maintenance updates to keep dependencies current with the latest patches.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1754 - Break connector pagination options off from filters
summary: Pagination options (page, page_size, limit) are now separated from filter parameters in connector read functions, eliminating the need for connectors to manually parse pagination from mixed filter arguments. This change clarifies pagination handling, enforces consistent limits (max page_size=500, default limit=25), and allows connectors to handle operations with or without pagination through a nullable pagination parameter.
userImpact: Users benefit from more consistent and predictable pagination behavior across connectors, with enforced limits on page sizes and clearer separation between data filtering and pagination concerns.
author: peter496

title: UP-1755 - Add inference counts by class aggregation functions
summary: Adds two new aggregation functions for counting inferences by class: BinaryClassifierCountByClassAggregationFunction processes binary classifier predictions directly, while BinaryClassifierCountThresholdAggregationFunction converts a continuous score column into binary predictions using a configurable threshold. These functions enable users to analyze inference distributions across prediction classes for model evaluation and monitoring.
userImpact: Users can now aggregate and analyze inference counts grouped by prediction class for binary classification models, supporting both direct predictions and threshold-based classification from continuous scores.
author: peter496

title: feat: sts expel
summary: Added support for STS-based expel deployment, including new CloudFormation IAM stacks for bring-your-own (BYO) credentials and enhanced VPC configuration. The deployment infrastructure now includes expel-specific environment configuration, updated CI/CD pipelines for deploy and release tag workflows, and core stack deployment automation for the platform_v4 environment.
userImpact: Users can now deploy Arthur Scope using STS assume role authentication in expel environments with dedicated IAM and VPC infrastructure.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update dependency faker to v32
summary: Updated the Faker dependency from v30.8.2 to v32.1.0. This major version update includes improvements to locale support (ko_KR road address fix, new uz_UZ providers, de_AT job methods), and a breaking change to make the pytest fixture session-scoped.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is an internal test dependency update.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project, including boto3 (1.35.58 → 1.35.59), cfn-lint (1.18.4 → 1.19.0), fastapi (0.115.4 → 0.115.5), pydantic_core (2.26.0 → 2.27.0), and faker to the latest versions. These updates bring AWS service API improvements, CloudFormation linting enhancements, and Pydantic 2.10 compatibility fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1680 - Add support for aws log collecting
summary: Added support for AWS log collecting through integration with OpenTelemetry (OTEL) collector. The implementation includes a telemetry collector service configured with CloudFormation templates (both legacy and new stack formats), IAM permissions for AWS log access, and updates to the app plane to export telemetry data. The collector is deployed as a sidecar pattern to avoid public exposure and reduce load on a centralized collector.
userImpact: Users can now collect and forward AWS logs and telemetry data from the application plane to AWS services, enabling better observability and monitoring of system behavior.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF - Fix integration tests, scheduled job code blocking manual metric runs
summary: Fixed integration test failures caused by applying a column filter to a table that doesn't support that column. Also fixed metric schedule validation code that was incorrectly running for manual metric jobs that don't have a schedule defined.
userImpact: Integration tests now pass, and manual metric runs are no longer blocked by schedule validation errors.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies: boto3 from 1.35.57 to 1.35.58 (patch) and google-cloud-bigquery from 3.26.0 to 3.27.0 (minor). The boto3 patch includes API updates for CloudFront, Inspector2, Lambda, OpenSearch, and Outposts services, while the BigQuery update adds support for setting max_stream_count in streaming operations.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates maintain compatibility and bring upstream service API improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates six non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.56 → 1.35.57), opentelemetry-instrumentation packages (0.49b0 → 0.49b1), and opentelemetry-sdk (1.28.0 → 1.28.1). The boto3 patch brings AWS API updates for batch, bedrock, EKS, firehose, Lambda, and other services.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Routine dependency maintenance to stay current with patch and beta updates.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1704 - Convert get_jobs state filter to states to allow IN filter
summary: The get_jobs state filter has been converted from a single equality filter (state == QUEUED) to a multi-value IN filter (state IN (QUEUED, RUNNING)). This allows users to filter jobs by multiple states in a single request. The API parameter name has changed from "state" to "states".
userImpact: Users can now filter jobs by multiple states simultaneously using the IN filter syntax, but frontend clients will need to update their requests to use the new "states" parameter instead of "state".
author: peter496

title: UP-1680 - Optimize role bindings respository functions
summary: Optimized role bindings repository queries by adding selectinload() to eager-load related entities (roles, permissions, organizations, users, groups, etc.) and applying limit(1) to single-record queries. This eliminates N+1 query patterns that were previously generating massive SQL queries with dozens of joins, significantly reducing database overhead for role binding retrieval operations.
userImpact: Role binding queries are now faster and more efficient, improving overall system latency for permission and authorization checks.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.56
summary: Updated boto3 dependency from v1.35.55 to v1.35.56. This patch release includes API updates to AWS services including Auto Scaling, Bedrock, Clean Rooms ML, QuickSight, and Synthetics, with no breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a dependency update that brings the latest AWS SDK improvements and API changes to the underlying infrastructure.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1560: Raw data retrieval for bucket based connectors
summary: Implemented raw data retrieval capability for bucket-based connectors, enabling users to fetch data directly from cloud storage backends. The implementation simplifies data retrieval logic across the data plane and app plane, with comprehensive integration tests added for bucket-based connector workflows.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve raw data from bucket-based connectors, expanding data retrieval options beyond existing connector types.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1431 - Add PUT job state permissions to frontend users for job cancellations
summary: Frontend users now have access to the put_job_state operation, enabling them to cancel jobs through the UI. This permission addition allows the frontend application to implement a cancel job button for improved job management.
userImpact: Users can now cancel jobs directly from the frontend interface with a dedicated cancel job button.
author: peter496

title: UP-1560: support pagination for bucket-based connectors
summary: Added pagination support for bucket-based connectors (e.g., S3) to efficiently retrieve large inference datasets. The implementation reads and filters inferences incrementally across paginated requests, reducing memory overhead compared to loading all results upfront.
userImpact: Users can now paginate through inference results from bucket-based connectors, enabling efficient retrieval of large datasets without loading everything into memory.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.55
summary: Updated boto3 dependency from v1.35.54 to v1.35.55. This patch release includes API changes for CodeBuild, GuardDuty, Lake Formation, Q Apps, S3 Control, and Verified Permissions services, as well as enhancements to protocol support in botocore.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1585: Support BigQuery Connector
summary: Added support for BigQuery as a data connector in Arthur Scope, enabling users to connect to and query BigQuery datasets. The implementation includes connector schema definition, integration with the app and data planes, and schema inference capabilities. A workaround was applied for Python 3.13 compatibility issues with the python-db-dtypes-pandas package.
userImpact: Users can now configure and use BigQuery as a data source connector in Arthur Scope for data integration and monitoring workflows.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve enable editing
summary: Upgraded Upsolve to the latest backend version to enable editing functionality. Changes include updates to database connection configurations and Dockerfile to align with the new Upsolve release.
userImpact: Users can now edit Upsolve data with the latest backend version.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including authzed (1.0.0 → 1.1.0) and opentelemetry-sdk (1.27.0 → 1.28.0). These are routine dependency maintenance updates to keep libraries current with minor and patch releases.

title: UP-1705 - Add friendly names, descriptions to metrics parameters
summary: Added friendly names and descriptions to aggregation function parameters across multiple metric functions. This metadata enrichment prepares the system for UI integration, enabling clear parameter explanations and human-readable names instead of internal identifiers.
userImpact: Users will see clearer, more descriptive parameter names and helpful explanations when the UI integrates these metrics, improving usability and discoverability of aggregation options.
author: peter496

title: UP-1545 - Add flag to connector schema indicating if list datasets is supported
summary: Added a flag to the connector schema to indicate whether list datasets is supported. When True, the UI will run the list datasets job and display available datasets; when False, the UI will prompt users for manual dataset creation. This avoids duplicate flag storage by keeping it at the schema level, which remains consistent across all instances of a connector type.
userImpact: Users can now connect to datasets via either automatic listing (when supported) or manual creation (when not supported), with the connector schema clearly indicating which method applies.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including alembic (1.13.3 → 1.14.0), duckdb (1.1.2 → 1.1.3), pydantic_core (2.25.1 → 2.26.0), and patch updates for cfn-lint and moto. These updates bring bug fixes, improvements to CSV scanning and Arrow column handling in duckdb, and general maintenance improvements across the dependency stack.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] fix dataset locator migration
summary: Fixed a dataset locator schema migration that was incorrectly requiring connectors to exist in the connector_specs table. The migration now correctly populates the dataset_locator_spec_schemas table for all connector types that exist in the connector_spec_schemas table, regardless of whether a connector instance has been created.
userImpact: Dataset locator schema information is now properly initialized during database migration for all supported connector types.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1534 - Upsolve Binary Classifier Dashboard
summary: Added integration test and test data for the Upsolve Binary Classifier Dashboard, which was previously introduced in a prior patch. The dashboard includes visualizations for inference count, inference count by class, and confusion matrix metrics with optimized queries using time_bucket_gapfill for proper gap handling and line-based charts with daily bucketing.
userImpact: Users can now test and verify the Binary Classifier Dashboard functionality with integration tests and sample data from train timing metrics and networking scenarios.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project including spicedb (v1.37.1 → v1.38.0), boto3 (1.35.53 → 1.35.54), moto (5.0.18 → 5.0.19), numpy (2.1.2 → 2.1.3), python-keycloak (4.6.3 → 4.7.0), and types-psutil. These updates bring bug fixes, minor features, and compatibility improvements from upstream dependencies including new ELBv2, Panorama, and WAFv2 methods in moto.
userImpact: Users benefit from upstream improvements including new AWS API methods in moto, AWS Bedrock and DocumentDB enhancements in boto3, and security and compatibility updates across all dependencies.

Fixed related integration test bugs that arose from the updated dataset structure.

title: UP-1533: Support GCS connector
summary: Added support for Google Cloud Storage (GCS) as a connector option alongside existing S3 support. The implementation includes GCS connector schemas in both app and data planes, refactored common test helpers to reduce duplication with S3 tests, and updated integration tests to cover bucket-based connector workflows. Users can now configure GCS buckets as data sources in addition to S3.
userImpact: Users can now connect to and read from Google Cloud Storage buckets using the new GCS connector, with the same credential and bucket configuration flow as the existing S3 connector.

userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes and improvements in Keycloak authentication services.

title: UP-1579 - Python 3.13 upgrade
summary: Upgraded Python runtime from an earlier version to Python 3.13 across all development and deployment environments. Updated Dockerfiles, local setup scripts, and CI/CD configuration to use Python 3.13, with verification that all unit tests, integration tests, and client generation continue to work correctly.
userImpact: Users can now run the application with Python 3.13, benefiting from the latest language improvements and security updates.

title: [no-ref] Upgrade pglast 6.9
summary: Upgraded pglast dependency to version 6.9, which includes a fix for a breaking format change introduced in the previous version. This reverts the test workarounds that were applied in the earlier upgrade, restoring the expected test behavior.
userImpact: Users now have access to the stable pglast 6.9 release with the format issue resolved, ensuring consistent SQL parsing behavior without workarounds.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.51 to 1.35.52 and cfn-lint from 1.18.2 to 1.18.3. These patch-level dependency updates include API enhancements to AWS services like AppSync, DataSync, EC2, and ECS, along with bug fixes and improvements in the underlying libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates maintain compatibility while providing access to latest AWS API features and patches.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1530: S3 connector support
summary: Added support for Amazon S3 and Google Cloud Storage (GCS) bucket-based connectors, enabling users to read datasets from S3 and GCS buckets. This includes connector schema definitions, read operations with configurable prefixes and file type filters, and comprehensive unit tests for both connector implementations.
userImpact: Users can now connect to and read data from Amazon S3 and Google Cloud Storage buckets using the bucket-based connector framework.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1657 - Add log emitted by job agent for reporting job failure
summary: The job agent now emits an error log when failing a job during shutdown, providing visibility into the reason for job failure. This enhancement improves observability by logging detailed failure information, such as hardware preemption events, directly to the job output.
userImpact: Users can now see detailed error logs from the job agent explaining why a job failed, improving debugging and monitoring capabilities.
author: peter496

title: UP-1599 - Handle SpiceDB writes in batch fashion
summary: Updated SpiceDB write operations to use batch processing for all creates, deletes, and relationship updates. Refactored batching logic into a dedicated function call within the DBManager class to reduce duplication and improve performance of authorization synchronization.
userImpact: SpiceDB relationship writes are now processed more efficiently in batch fashion, reducing latency and improving throughput for authorization operations.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: pglast (6.2 → 6.8), protobuf (5.28.2 → 5.28.3), and pydantic_core (2.25.0 → 2.25.1). Test assertions were updated to align with pglast 6.7's changes to SQL parser print formatting, and fixes were applied to accommodate the newer pglast version.
userImpact: Users benefit from upstream improvements in pglast SQL parsing, bug fixes in pydantic-core validation, and security/stability patches across dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency types-croniter to v4
summary: Updated the types-croniter type stub package from v3.0.3 to v4.0.0, which includes the latest type definitions for the croniter library. This is a routine dependency update to ensure accurate type checking and IDE support.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency croniter to v5
summary: croniter dependency updated from v4.0.0 to v5.0.1. This major version bump reintroduces 7 as a valid DayOfWeek value in cron expressions, deviating from the standard cron specification to align with community expectations.
userImpact: Cron expressions can now use 7 to represent Sunday in addition to 0, matching traditional cron behavior.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates 22 non-major (patch) dependencies across the codebase, including security and bug fixes for packages like SQLAlchemy, PyYAML, FastAPI, boto3, and others. The updates address maintenance and stability improvements while preserving API compatibility.
userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes, security patches, and stability improvements in core dependencies without requiring code changes due to maintained backward compatibility.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1718 upgrade auth stack to keycloak 25
summary: Upgraded the authentication stack to Keycloak 25, including python-keycloak library from 3.0 to 4.0. Fixed breaking API changes in the new Keycloak version (PUT semantics for user updates, required email field in payloads) and added user profile schema configuration to manage the user_type attribute in the admin UI.
userImpact: Users can now leverage Keycloak 25's latest security and feature improvements, with proper management of custom user attributes like user_type visible in the Keycloak admin console.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1554 - Add POST available_dataset endpoint
summary: Added a POST endpoint for manual available dataset onboarding, allowing users to add datasets to connectors without requiring a full wipe-and-repopulate cycle. This is particularly useful for connectors where bulk synchronization isn't applicable. Also fixed the PutAvailableDataset model which unnecessarily accepted an ID parameter that was never honored by the API, and updated authorization roles to support the new functionality.
userImpact: Users can now manually onboard individual available datasets via POST {connector_id}/available_dataset instead of being limited to full connector dataset synchronization.
author: peter496

title: UP-465 - Add true/false flag to available datasets, datasets, models to...
summary: Added an `arthur_shield_dataset` boolean flag to available datasets, datasets, and models to indicate whether a model is Shield-enabled. The Shield connector now filters to only output datasets marked as Shield datasets, and when a model is created, this flag is automatically derived from its dataset and set permanently. No frontend changes were required due to existing type casting logic.
userImpact: Users can now identify which datasets and models are Shield-enabled through the `arthur_shield_dataset` flag, enabling better organization and filtering of Shield-specific resources in the UI.
author: peter496

title: Revert "Bumped version for upsolve to 8035 in scope-version-manifest.json"
summary: Reverted an incomplete upsolve version bump to 8035 that was missing required migrations in the upsolve image. The version manifest has been rolled back to restore system stability.
userImpact: Users will no longer encounter missing migration issues when using upsolve; the service is restored to a known stable version.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve
summary: Updated the Upsolve Docker image to the latest backend release. This brings in the latest improvements and fixes from the Upsolve service.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest Upsolve backend improvements and bug fixes.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] lock dependencies to prevent ambiguity now that we have renovate bot
summary: Dependencies across all requirements.txt files and setup.py have been pinned to exact versions to ensure consistency between CI/CD and local development environments. This prevents version mismatches that could occur when renovate bot updates dependencies without explicit locks, improving build reproducibility and reducing version-related debugging.
userImpact: Dependency versions are now locked explicitly, ensuring CI/CD pipelines and local development use identical package versions, reducing compatibility issues and unexpected behavior from version drift.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] add docs for docker login in readme setup
summary: Updated README documentation to include Docker authentication instructions for developers using the Upsolve container dependency. This guides developers to authenticate their Docker daemons with the Arthur Docker 1Password user to access required container images.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency croniter to v4
summary: Updated croniter dependency from v3.0.3 to v4.0.0. This major version bump includes a breaking change that removes the non-standard DayOfWeek alias (7 mapping to 0) to align with standard cron behavior, and fixes an issue with day-of-week range calculations when the last day falls on a Sunday.
userImpact: Cron scheduling expressions using DayOfWeek value 7 as an alias for Sunday will no longer work; they must be updated to use 0 for Sunday to comply with standard cron format.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated pytest-httpx from 0.32.0 to 0.33.0, adding explicit Python 3.13 support and new matching options. The update includes a `should_mock` option for conditional mocking, enhanced multipart body matching via `match_files` and `match_data`, and extension matching via `match_extensions`, while deprecating the `non_mocked_hosts` option in favor of `should_mock`.
userImpact: Users can now use Python 3.13 with pytest-httpx and have access to more granular request mocking control through the new `should_mock`, `match_files`, `match_data`, and `match_extensions` parameters, though `non_mocked_hosts` usage must be migrated to `should_mock`.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1538 - Expel Binary Classification Performance Metrics
summary: Added confusion matrix aggregation functions to compute TP/TN/FP/FN counts for binary classification performance metrics. Three specialized variants handle bool/int predictions, string label predictions with configurable true/false values, and float predictions with threshold-based classification. Includes a new networking security test dataset and fixes a ParameterSchema `optional` field derivation bug.
userImpact: Users can now compute binary classification performance metrics (true positives, true negatives, false positives, false negatives) across multiple prediction and ground truth data formats without manual calculation.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated redis-py dependency from 5.1.1 to 5.2.0, which includes a new scorer argument for AggregateRequest and maintenance improvements. This minor version bump adds functionality for more flexible request aggregation in Redis operations.
userImpact: Users can now pass a scorer argument to AggregateRequest for enhanced aggregation capabilities in Redis queries.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] correct dataset onboarding flow
summary: The dataset onboarding flow was missing a step to fetch raw data for manually created datasets, preventing users from confirming their configuration was correct. This fix adds the missing data fetch step to the workflow, ensuring users can validate their dataset setup before proceeding.
userImpact: Users can now properly verify their manually created datasets are configured correctly by viewing the fetched raw data during onboarding.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1593: move data retrieval to dataset level
summary: Moves data retrieval operations and fetch data job specs to run at the dataset level instead of the model level. This includes SpiceDB schema migrations to replace old model-based data retrieval permissions with new dataset-based permissions, along with corresponding database schema changes, repository updates, and operator refactoring. The change is marked as a breaking change requiring frontend updates.
userImpact: Users must migrate their data retrieval operations from model-level to dataset-level configuration; frontend endpoints for dataset-level data retrieval and available dataset operations will follow in subsequent updates.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1679 - Properly convey exit code from dataplane processes
summary: JobExecutor was wrapping all jobs in try/catch and always returning exit code 0, even when the underlying process failed. The process executor now properly conveys the actual exit code from dataplane processes, allowing callers to accurately detect job failures.
userImpact: Job failures in dataplane processes are now correctly reported via non-zero exit codes instead of always exiting with code 0.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Revert FE image to pre-localization bug
summary: Reverted the FE image to a pre-localization bug version to resolve a localization issue affecting the frontend. This temporary revert was applied for demo purposes.
userImpact: Users will see the frontend rendered correctly without localization-related bugs.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated mypy from 1.12.0 to 1.13.0, a minor version bump. This maintenance update keeps the type-checking toolchain current with the latest improvements and bug fixes from the mypy project.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: UP-1412 full onboarding diagram
summary: Created a comprehensive visual diagram documenting the complete resource onboarding workflow, including support for reusing existing resources, modular steps, and new flows for non-listable data sources, joined/multiple datasets, schema editing, and metrics configuration. This diagram clarifies step dependencies and transitions needed for the tabular MVP implementation.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1538 - Change aggregation function contract to emit more than one metric
summary: Aggregation functions now output a set of metrics instead of a single series, enabling support for multi-metric aggregations like confusion matrices. This refactor updates the aggregation function contract across all existing aggregation functions (categorical count, inference count, numeric stats, etc.) and the aggregator to handle multiple metrics per aggregation.
userImpact: Users can now leverage aggregation functions that produce multiple metrics from a single calculation, enabling richer analytical results such as confusion matrices with TP, FP, TN, FN, and derived metrics.
author: peter496

title: Revert FE image to yesterday morning's build
summary: Reverted the frontend image to yesterday morning's build to address an issue discovered before the scheduled demo. This is a temporary rollback of a specific commit to restore stability.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Update all non-major dependencies

title: UP-1650 - Reduce fetch data memory requirements for improved latency
summary: Reduced memory requirements for data retrieval jobs to lower process spinup overhead. By fitting these jobs into the thread threshold instead of requiring a new process, latency is improved from ~4-6 seconds to execute on the dataplane.
userImpact: Data retrieval jobs now begin executing with reduced latency on the dataplane, improving overall operation responsiveness.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF - Bump memory too
summary: Memory allocation was not updated to match the previously increased CPU in Fargate pod configuration. AWS Fargate requires specific CPU-to-memory ratios, and this fix aligns memory to 8GB to pair with the 4CPU allocation.
userImpact: Fargate pods now have the correct memory-to-CPU ratio, preventing configuration validation errors and ensuring pods can run on AWS EKS Fargate.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Temporarily up dataplane resources
summary: Increased dataplane CPU resources in the dev environment to investigate potential latency improvements for certain jobs. This is a temporary change made in response to performance concerns observed in Slack discussions.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1513 alerts improvements
summary: Added technical documentation to explain current alerting and metrics drawbacks, including visual diagrams to illustrate the issues and justify proposed solutions. The documentation covers metrics pre-aggregation behavior and provides architectural context for understanding the system's limitations.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is documentation for internal technical reference and architectural understanding.
author: ianmcgraw

title: XP-1145 - Update Shield connector configs to use query optimization results
summary: Updated Shield connector configurations to optimize query performance by disabling count queries and increasing default page size to 1500. These changes align with Shield API optimizations and reduce job execution time by approximately 44% (from 731s to 407s in dev), significantly improving throughput for data retrieval operations.
userImpact: Users will experience faster data retrieval from Shield with improved job execution times, particularly for large-scale data operations, while maintaining responsive log updates.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updates fsspec dependency from version 2024.9.0 to 2024.10.0, a minor version update. This brings improvements and bug fixes from the latest fsspec release to enhance filesystem abstraction capabilities.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest fsspec improvements and bug fixes for filesystem operations.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Add 5 minute validation to lookback period
summary: Added validation to enforce 5-minute alignment for the lookback period in model schedules, complementing an earlier alignment fix for cron frequency. This ensures the lookback period (which defines the data read window) respects the same 5-minute boundary requirement, preventing potential data consistency issues even though the UI currently only exposes hourly inputs.
userImpact: The system now validates that lookback periods are aligned to 5-minute boundaries, preventing misconfigured schedules that could read data from unaligned time windows.
author: peter496

title: UP-1590 - Small refactors to scheduled jobs, metrics calculation executors, add some tests
summary: Refactored scheduled jobs and metrics calculation executor classes to improve testability and maintainability. Added comprehensive unit tests for these executors and fixed job scheduling logic to correctly calculate start timestamps from lookback periods. These changes improve code robustness and enable better coverage of critical job execution paths.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1513 fix grafana query for toxicity latency
summary: Fixed a Grafana query for the Toxicity Rule Median latency metric to properly merge sketches before calculating the median. This ensures the latency dashboard displays accurate aggregated data.
userImpact: The Toxicity Rule Median latency metric in the Grafana dashboard now correctly displays aggregated latency data.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1617: Update job create logic to have queued time match ready time for future jobs
summary: Updated job creation logic so that queued time for scheduled (future) jobs matches their ready time instead of the creation timestamp. This prevents scheduled jobs from appearing incorrectly sorted on the frontend, where they were previously buried below jobs with earlier queued times despite executing around the same time.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs now display in chronological order on the frontend, sorted by their actual execution time rather than creation time.
author: peter496

title: UP-1538 - Refactor aggregation parameter parsing, definition, api models
summary: Split the monolithic ScopeMetricParameter object into separate, type-specific parameter definitions for datasets, columns, literals, and group-by dimensions. This restructuring simplifies the schema, reduces complexity, and makes the codebase easier to maintain and extend. No aggregation function names, values, or storage mechanisms were changed, so no database migration is required.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is an internal schema refactoring that maintains backward compatibility with existing aggregation functions and storage.
author: peter496

title: UP-1609 fix upsolve dash
summary: Fixed toxicity and latency graphs in the Upsolve dashboard by upgrading dashboard configurations and applying new Upsolve export patches. This resolves issues affecting data visualization accuracy in the dashboard.
userImpact: Toxicity and latency graphs in the Upsolve dashboard now display correctly.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1609 fix upsolve dash
summary: Fixed toxicity and latency graphs in the Upsolve dashboard by deploying a new database patch. This resolves display issues affecting the dashboard's key metrics visualization.
userImpact: Users can now see accurate toxicity and latency data in the Upsolve dashboard.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: Change cron validation logic
summary: Fixed cron validation logic to correctly validate job interval frequency by parsing expressions and ensuring intervals are evenly divisible by 5 minutes and properly aligned. Previously, expressions like `*/7` were incorrectly validated as valid despite creating non-uniform intervals that violate the 5-minute minimum threshold.
userImpact: Cron job schedules now correctly enforce the 5-minute minimum interval requirement by validating both interval divisibility and alignment, preventing misaligned job executions.
author: peter496

title: UP-1609 bump upsolve image
summary: Updated the upsolve Docker image to the latest version. This is a routine maintenance update to ensure the container uses current dependencies and fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: feat: new stack deployments
summary: This MR introduces automated deployment infrastructure for new stack deployments and on-premises installations. It adds CloudFormation templates, shell scripts, and CI jobs to orchestrate the deployment of core infrastructure, databases, and application services including Upsolve, SpiceDB, auth services, data plane, frontend, and Grafana. The changes enable customers to provision complete Scope stacks with configurable parameters.
userImpact: Customers and deployment teams can now automate end-to-end Scope stack deployments on AWS and on-premises using provided shell scripts and configuration files.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies to their latest minor versions: Faker (~=30.4.0 → ~=30.6.0) with new Russian passport provider, cfn-lint (~=1.16.0 → ~=1.17.1) for CloudFormation linting improvements, and psutil (~=6.0.0 → ~=6.1.0) with Windows process iteration performance improvements and test framework enhancements.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved process iteration performance on Windows and enhanced locale support for data generation, with minor improvements to CloudFormation linting.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Add back dataplane stack
summary: Restored the dataplane stack configuration in the CloudFormation root scope template. This change re-enables the dataplane infrastructure layer that was previously removed, ensuring proper deployment structure.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Infrastructure configuration restored to support dataplane deployment.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Recreate Dataplane stack
summary: The Dataplane CloudFormation stack was referencing an autoscaling policy that no longer exists, causing deployment failures. This change temporarily removes the Dataplane stack definition and its output references from the root CloudFormation template to allow CloudFormation to clean up the broken stack and enable fresh redeployment.
userImpact: Resolves deployment failures caused by broken Dataplane stack references, allowing the infrastructure to be redeployed cleanly.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Up autoscaling thresholds
summary: Increased CPU autoscaling threshold from 10% to 25% to better handle concurrent job loads with the recently implemented parallelism feature. The previous low threshold caused unnecessary scale-up cascades when scheduled jobs arrived in bursts, frequently exceeding the 10-instance maximum.
userImpact: The dataplane now scales more efficiently under bursty job loads, reducing unnecessary worker churn and improving resource utilization.
author: peter496

title: Revert "UP-1609 update timescale connection type"
summary: Reverted a previous update to the Timescale connection type configuration. The change was made following feedback that the original modification should not have been applied.
userImpact: Timescale connection type configuration is restored to its previous state.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1609 update timescale connection type
summary: Updated the Timescale connection type in the database patches configuration to use the correct connection type identifier. This ensures that Timescale connections are properly recognized and configured by the system.
userImpact: Timescale database connections are now configured with the proper connection type, ensuring correct behavior when connecting to Timescale instances.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: Faker (~30.3.0 → ~30.4.0) adds improved locale support for hi_IN with separate male/female names and prefixes/suffixes, and uvicorn (~0.31.0 → ~0.32.0) adds official Python 3.13 support and warnings for exceeded max_request_limit.
userImpact: No direct user impact—these are routine dependency updates that maintain compatibility and improve underlying library functionality.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1518 - Add graceful termination for ECS tasks
summary: Implements graceful termination handling for ECS task draining. When ECS sends a SIGTERM signal, the job agent stops accepting new jobs, allows running jobs to complete within a grace period (default 15 seconds), then terminates remaining jobs and threads. The agent also refactors the job execution paradigm to have the agent report job completion/failure states to the app plane instead of relying on running processes to self-report.
userImpact: ECS task terminations are now handled gracefully, allowing in-flight jobs to complete cleanly before shutdown and preventing duplicate job executions during container drains.
author: peter496

title: [UP-1609] upgrade upsolve image
summary: Upgraded the upsolve Docker image to the latest version and fixed a timescale connection issue. This update improves reliability and compatibility of the upsolve service.
userImpact: Users benefit from an updated upsolve service with improved stability and corrected database connectivity for timescale connections.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix local dev upsolve set up
summary: Fixed a 401 Unauthorized error that occurred during local development when attempting to retrieve the Upsolve tenant token. A configuration issue in the docker-compose setup was preventing proper authentication with the Upsolve service.
userImpact: Local development environments can now successfully authenticate with Upsolve and retrieve tenant tokens without encountering 401 errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including authzed/spicedb (v1.37.0 → v1.37.1) and mypy (1.11.1 → 1.12.0). These dependency updates improve code quality and ensure compatibility with the latest stable releases.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1592: Add dataset locator validations and unit tests
summary: Adds schema validation for dataset locators to ensure created, available, and patched datasets conform to required locator constraints. A new datasets operator module enforces these validations across dataset creation and modification endpoints, with comprehensive unit test coverage to verify correct behavior and error handling.
userImpact: Users will now receive clear validation errors if datasets are created or modified with invalid locator configurations, preventing invalid data from entering the system.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1601 - Add correction for 0 indexing shield connector pages
summary: Fixed page indexing mismatch between Shield connector (0-indexed) and Scope's internal standard (1-indexed). The Shield connector now applies a correction to translate pagination values from Scope's 1-indexed format to Shield's 0-indexed format.
userImpact: Page-based pagination with the Shield connector now works correctly without off-by-one errors.
author: peter496

title: UP-1600 - UP-1654 - UP-1430 - Move SQLAlchemy code to 2.0 style from legacy style, remove issue of ambiguous sorting
summary: Migrated SQLAlchemy query syntax from 1.4 legacy style to 2.0 style across all repository classes to support future async paradigm adoption. Added a default order-by-id-descending to all queries via the base repository, eliminating the need for redundant sorting throughout the codebase and fixing ambiguous sorting issues in infinite scroll scenarios.
userImpact: Query results now return in consistent descending ID order by default, fixing potential pagination and infinite scroll issues without requiring per-query sort specification.
author: peter496

title: UP-1592: Expand dataset locator concept
summary: Expanded the dataset locator concept by introducing `dataset_locator_schema` to connector specs that defines required locator fields for each connector type, and refactored datasets to consolidate metadata (including `data_file_type`) into a structured `dataset_locator` object. This architectural change removes the standalone `data_file_type` field and establishes a more flexible pattern for connector-specific dataset identification metadata.
userImpact: Frontend contracts are broken; clients must now handle the new `dataset_locator_schema` structure in connector specs and adapted `dataset_locator` objects on datasets, enabling more robust connector-specific dataset identification and validation.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1590 - Break up job_executor.py to smaller pieces
summary: Decomposed the monolithic job_executor.py module into smaller, focused executor classes organized in a job_executors package. The refactoring splits responsibilities into AlertCheckExecutor, ConnectorTestExecutor, FetchDataExecutor, ListDatasetsExecutor, MetricsCalculationExecutor, ScheduleJobsExecutor, and SchemaInferenceExecutor. This structural change improves code maintainability and testability without altering existing functionality.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This refactoring improves code organization and developer experience by making the executor system easier to test and maintain.
author: peter496

title: UP-1597 webhook alert routing
summary: Implemented webhook notification routing that sends alerts to the specific webhooks configured for each alert rule. Also fixed a security issue where alerts could be uploaded for a model using any alert rule ID in the system without validating that the rule belonged to the same model.
userImpact: Users can now configure different webhooks for different alert rules, and alerts will be routed only to the webhooks configured for the rule that triggered them.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1597 alert rule webhook routing
summary: Added API endpoints and database schema to link alert rules to webhooks for notification routing. This is part 1 of a 2-part feature that establishes the data model and API layer; the next MR will integrate these links into the actual alert notification routing logic.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage links between alert rules and webhooks through the API to configure custom notification delivery.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1041 - Add parallelism to dataplane execution
summary: Adds parallel execution to the dataplane by allowing multiple jobs to run concurrently in separate threads or processes. Each job has a memory requirement that must be met before execution, and running jobs are tracked and monitored; when a job completes, its memory allocation is reclaimed. Also makes MemorySecretsClient thread-safe to support parallel integration testing.
userImpact: Users can now run multiple dataplane jobs in parallel within a single agent instance, improving throughput while respecting memory constraints.
author: peter496

title: chore: upsolve update to 0.1.2
summary: Updated upsolve Docker image to version 0.1.2 and refreshed the associated patch file. This maintenance update ensures the upsolve component uses the latest stable image and applies the current patch set.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1435 job queue management design tweaks
summary: Updated job queue management design documentation to add support for moving jobs to the front of the queue (queued-to-queued transition) and corrected a missing state reference in the acceptance criteria. This clarifies the complete set of job state transitions available for queue management operations.
userImpact: Users and developers can now reference accurate job queue state transition documentation, including the move-to-front capability for queued jobs.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: Use new file for tracking current in memory mock Keycloak client
summary: Fixed race condition in local development environment where multiple processes competed for the same JSON file tracking the in-memory mock Keycloak client. Each process now uses its own temporary file, eliminating the contention and avoiding the need for complex locking mechanisms.
userImpact: Local development with Keycloak mock client now works reliably without race conditions when multiple processes run simultaneously.
author: peter496

title: Add unify-app hosted in vercel origin to CORS
summary: Added the Vercel deployment origin (https://unify-frontend-app.vercel.app) to the CORS allowed origins list in the server configuration. This enables the hosted frontend application to make cross-origin requests to the backend API without being blocked by browser CORS policies.
userImpact: Users can now access the application through the Vercel-hosted frontend without encountering CORS-related request failures.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-1041 - Initial changes to set up multithreaded job runs
summary: This MR lays the groundwork for multithreaded job execution by adding job priority and memory requirement tracking to the jobs database, introducing a new Cancelled job state, and refactoring the job dequeuing logic to respect agent memory limits. The job submission API now uses a structured argument approach with sensible defaults, reducing configuration burden on callers.
userImpact: Agents can now request jobs that fit within their available memory capacity, and job submissions benefit from simplified configuration with appropriate defaults for internal fields.
author: peter496

title: UP-1435 job queue management tech design
summary: Added a technical design document for job queue management that outlines job states, cancellation and retry behavior, and priority-based queue operations. The document establishes patterns for managing job lifecycle including running, cancellation, retry, and clone tracking to align the team on operational requirements and implementation strategy.
userImpact: Users gain visibility into how job operations like cancellation, retries, and priority-based queue management will be supported and can expect consistent behavior across these workflows.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1582 | Remove task_name from allowed shield filters
summary: Removed the `task_name` filter from the list of allowed filtering fields in the shield connector. This prevents users from filtering by task name, which was unnecessary since the connector already restricts queries to the current task using task ID filtering.
userImpact: Users can no longer filter shield connector queries by task name; filtering is restricted to the current task by task ID only.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1493: Scripts to generate expel sample tabular dataset in s3
summary: Added scripts and infrastructure to generate a sample tabular dataset for the Expel use case and upload it to S3. The new pipeline creates synthetic test data with configurable parameters for number of days and inferences per day, and supports both local file generation and S3 upload. This enables testing and development with realistic data volumes before populating production-scale datasets.
userImpact: Users can now generate and customize sample tabular datasets for testing the Expel model pipeline, with flexible options to create local test files or publish directly to S3.
author: alex380125

title: chore: make cpu and memory configurable as parameters
summary: CloudFormation templates for ECS services now expose CPU and memory as configurable parameters instead of hard-coded values, enabling better resource optimization. This allows teams to adjust resource allocations per environment or service without modifying templates, and includes reduced default reservations for the frontend service based on actual usage metrics.
userImpact: Operators can now customize CPU and memory reservations for ECS services via CloudFormation parameters, enabling more cost-efficient deployments aligned with actual service requirements.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Feat redis infra
summary: Introduced Redis Stack infrastructure to the Arthur Scope platform for caching and session storage. The implementation includes Redis subnet groups, serverless cache configuration, cache usage limit support, and secure credential management via CloudFormation secrets. Redis is now deployed alongside the app plane on ECS.
userImpact: Users can now leverage Redis for caching and improved application performance, with configurable cache usage limits and secure credential handling.
author: jdb-arthur

title: NO-REF: Change multiprocessing method from fork to spawn
summary: Changed the multiprocessing start method from fork to spawn to address stability issues in forked processes with multithreaded contexts (FastAPI/Uvicorn). This resolves DeprecationWarnings about fork() usage in multi-threaded processes that could lead to deadlocks. While this introduces a modest performance cost (~2 minutes longer test suite runtime), it should improve CI test stability by eliminating resource-sharing issues caused by fork semantics.
userImpact: CI test suite runs are now more stable and less prone to deadlocks when forking multi-threaded processes.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Whitespace changes and python minor version CI change
summary: Updated CI base image to a higher Python minor version to resolve a bug in the current version, along with whitespace normalization across codebase files. These changes ensure CI builds proceed reliably without impacting application code.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update dependency markupsafe to v3
summary: MarkupSafe has been updated from v2.1.5 to v3.0.0, which adds support for Python 3.13 including its experimental free-threaded build. The update includes performance improvements (40% faster escaping of plain strings), modernized packaging with pyproject.toml, and improved method signatures for Markup class methods. Python 3.7 and 3.8 support has been dropped.
userImpact: Applications now benefit from improved performance and compatibility with Python 3.13, while requiring Python 3.9+.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: Faker (~=30.1.0 -> ~=30.3.0) and black (~=24.8.0 -> ~=24.10.0). These are routine dependency updates that include bug fixes and minor enhancements to development and testing tools.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1578
summary: Fixed CI pipeline conditions for the Frontend version update job to only run on the main branch. Previously, the job could merge MRs unintentionally when a new FE image was present. This ensures the pipeline can update the FE image independently without triggering unrelated MR merges.
userImpact: CI/CD pipeline now correctly handles Frontend version updates on the main branch without unintended side effects.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1578 fix auto merging mrs
summary: Fixed an issue preventing automatic merge requests from being merged. The fix was applied to the GitLab CI configuration to correct the auto-merge behavior.
userImpact: Merge requests that are configured for automatic merging now complete successfully without manual intervention.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1379 - Add exclude query param to get_jobs endpoint, remove unused job type, lower retry count for scheduled jobs
summary: Added `exclude_kinds` query parameter to the get_jobs endpoint, allowing users to filter out jobs of specific types. The parameter takes precedence over `kinds`, so excluding a job type will remove it from results even if it was explicitly requested. Also removed an unused duplicate job type (raw data retrieval) and fixed a retry count overflow issue for scheduled jobs that exceeded PostgreSQL's 32-bit integer bounds.
userImpact: Users can now exclude specific job types from get_jobs results using the `exclude_kinds` parameter, and scheduled jobs no longer trigger 500 errors in PostgreSQL due to retry count overflow.
author: peter496

title: UP-1410: log chart bugfix
summary: Fixed a logging chart configuration issue in CloudFormation that was introduced in a previous merge. This is a follow-up correction to an unintended merge that affected the Arthur Scope monitoring setup on ECS.
userImpact: Users will now see correct log chart behavior in Arthur Scope monitoring on ECS deployments.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1410: add upsolve monitoring support
summary: Added monitoring support for the Upsolve service in CloudFormation monitoring dashboards and alarms. The changes include updates to rate charts, log views, and ECS container insights alarms configured according to AWS best practices for RunningTaskCount metrics.
userImpact: Users can now monitor Upsolve service metrics and health status through the existing Arthur monitoring dashboards and receive alarms based on task count thresholds.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1573 | Allow for empty webhook header dict update
summary: Previously, webhook updates with an empty dict for headers would omit the headers field entirely. This fix now allows the headers field to be explicitly cleared by sending an empty dict during webhook updates, enabling users to remove all headers from a webhook configuration.
userImpact: Users can now update webhook configurations to remove all headers by explicitly passing an empty headers dictionary.
author: ooizig

title: chore(deps): update dependency redis to v5.1.1
summary: Updated redis-py dependency from v5.1.0 to v5.1.1, a patch release that fixes critical bugs in Redis Set command return types, Hiredis availability detection, and async pipeline/cluster operations.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency maintenance update that fixes underlying bugs in the redis library.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1491 metrics and query docs
summary: Added comprehensive external-facing product documentation for Arthur's metrics and query capabilities. The documentation provides guidance on writing queries for dashboards and alert rules, including metric definitions, query syntax examples, and step-by-step instructions for creating alert rules with accompanying screenshots.
userImpact: Users now have clear, accessible documentation to understand available metrics and write custom queries for dashboards and alert rules.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1570 fix toxicity distribution dashboard
summary: The toxicity distribution dashboard was incorrectly calculating sketch medians for each 5-minute row rather than merging all sketches to compute a true distribution. This caused significantly undercounted metrics (500 counts instead of 43.5k). The fix now properly uses `sketch_merge()` and `kll_float_sketch_get_pmf()` to generate an accurate distribution that sums close to total inference counts.
userImpact: The toxicity distribution dashboard now displays accurate counts that correctly represent the full range of inference data, rather than showing artificially low values.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1490 webhooks product guide
summary: Added comprehensive product documentation for Arthur Webhooks, including a detailed guide with practical examples for integrating webhooks with popular platforms like Slack and Jira. The documentation includes step-by-step instructions, screenshots, and code examples to help users understand and implement webhook-based workflows.
userImpact: Users can now access complete product documentation and real-world examples to understand how to set up and use webhooks for integrating Arthur with external systems.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1231: point app plane to on-prem upsolve
summary: Updated app plane configuration to point to on-premises Upsolve instead of cloud-hosted Upsolve, including bootstrap host setup and CloudFormation changes for AWS ECS deployment. The app plane can now interact with a locally-deployed Upsolve instance, enabling self-hosted deployments and reducing external dependencies.
userImpact: Users deploying on-premises Upsolve can now integrate it seamlessly with the app plane without requiring cloud-hosted Upsolve services.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update dependency authzed to v1
summary: Updated the authzed Python dependency from v0.19.0 to v1.0.0. According to the official release notes, this major version bump introduces no breaking changes, making it a safe upgrade that brings the latest features and improvements from the authzed library.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest authzed v1.0.0 features and improvements without any breaking changes to existing functionality.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update dependency cfn-lint to ~=1.16.0
summary: Updated cfn-lint dependency from ~=1.15.0 to ~=1.16.0 to incorporate upstream improvements and bug fixes from the CloudFormation linter project. This is a routine dependency maintenance update to keep tooling current.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-846 - Change schedule jobs job to retry (nearly) infinite times
summary: Increased the max_attempts limit for schedule jobs to near-infinite (sys.maxsize) to prevent schedule interruption due to transient failures. This mitigates the impact of temporary outages or bugs that cause cascading retries to exhaust the previous retry limit.
userImpact: Schedule jobs will now persist through temporary service disruptions and error spikes, reducing the risk of schedules being prematurely terminated.
author: peter496

title: UP-846 - Fix import, reduce test parallelism to reduce flakiness
summary: Fixed an incorrect import statement in the aggregation matcher module that had an unnecessary scope.scope_common prefix. Test parallelism was also reduced in the CI configuration to improve test stability and reduce intermittent failures.
userImpact: Tests now run more reliably with reduced flakiness, and the aggregation matcher module imports correctly.
author: peter496

title: chore: enable upsolve ecs healthcheck & docker-compose fix
summary: Re-enabled ECS healthchecks for the upsolve service by adding curl to the upsolve Docker image and fixing related configuration in CloudFormation and docker-compose. The healthcheck was previously disabled due to missing curl, and this change restores it along with cache cleanup optimizations in the Dockerfile.
userImpact: ECS healthchecks for upsolve services now work correctly, allowing proper monitoring of service health and enabling automatic task restarts on failure.
author: alex380125

title: chore: upsolve exec and disable ecs healthchecks
summary: Disabled ECS healthchecks that were causing service interruption while ALB healthchecks remain active. Enabled ECS Exec functionality to allow debugging of the running container when needed. These operational changes improve service stability and debuggability without affecting the public-facing health check mechanism.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Operations and debugging teams can now connect to running containers and the service will remain stable without ECS-level health check disruptions.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1468 | Add default order by id to queries
summary: Added a secondary order by id to alert queries to ensure consistent, deterministic pagination when primary sort fields are non-unique. This resolves ambiguity in result ordering when multiple records share the same sort key value.
userImpact: Users now receive consistent, predictable alert query results across pagination requests, eliminating ordering inconsistencies that previously occurred with duplicate field values.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1528 add default limit and page size for raw data retrieval job
summary: Raw data retrieval jobs previously fetched all available data for a given time range, which could result in hundreds or thousands of inferences being loaded unnecessarily. This change adds default limit filters to constrain data retrieval to a reasonable subset by default, while allowing users to opt-in for larger datasets when needed.
