author: martin452

title: changed CSP configs
summary: Updated Content Security Policy (CSP) configuration by adding report-to and report-uri directives to the Vite config for CSP violation reporting, while removing the CSP environment variable from the nginx config to prevent header injection conflicts. This change streamlines CSP management by consolidating the policy configuration to a single source.
userImpact: CSP violations will now be reported to the specified endpoint, enabling better monitoring of security policy compliance and potential attacks.
author: martin452

title: UP-3225: Don't overwrite CSP header on dev
summary: Fixed Content Security Policy (CSP) header being overwritten in development environments. The issue was addressed by updating the Docker configuration and adding an entrypoint script to preserve the CSP header settings during development builds.
userImpact: Development environments will no longer have their CSP headers overwritten, allowing proper security policy configuration during development.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: unsafe inline into scope
summary: Updated nginx.conf to move unsafe inline directive into a specific scope. This security-focused configuration change restricts where inline scripts are permitted, improving Content Security Policy compliance and reducing the attack surface.
userImpact: Users benefit from a more restrictive Content Security Policy that limits inline script execution to only necessary scopes, enhancing application security.
author: martin452

title: Explicit CSP in nginx header
summary: Added an explicit Content Security Policy (CSP) header to the nginx configuration. This ensures proper CSP enforcement at the web server level, preventing CSP-related issues and improving security headers handling.
userImpact: CSP security headers are now explicitly configured in nginx, ensuring consistent policy enforcement across all requests.
author: martin452

title: [UP-3217] fix: modify style-src-elem and script-src-elem
summary: Updated Content Security Policy directives `style-src-elem` and `script-src-elem` in the Vite configuration. This change ensures stricter security controls for inline styles and scripts in the application.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved Content Security Policy enforcement for inline style and script elements.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-3200] feat: pass aggregation_kind to model config
summary: Added support for passing aggregation_kind to the model configuration during model creation. This change ensures that the aggregation_kind parameter is properly forwarded through the model config flow to enable proper aggregation handling in downstream systems.
userImpact: Users can now specify and track aggregation_kind settings when creating new models, enabling more precise control over aggregation behavior.
author: lukasz94

title: feat: add CSP plugin & headers
summary: Added Content Security Policy (CSP) plugin to the Vite build configuration with CSP headers for trusted sources including GitHub, AWS, Amplitude, and Vercel. Configured Subresource Integrity (SRI) to enable only on production deployments, and added trusted URLs to support external service integrations.
userImpact: Users benefit from enhanced security through CSP headers that mitigate XSS and injection attacks while allowing necessary third-party integrations to function properly.

title: fix: metrics bugs
summary: Fixed multiple issues in the metrics configuration UI: form now resets after adding a metric spec, the initial dataset field is removed from the spec display, and DisplayMetric component fields are now dynamically rendered based on schema. These fixes improve the user experience when configuring metrics by eliminating stale form state and unnecessary fields.
userImpact: Users can now add metric specs without stale form data persisting, and the metrics configuration interface displays only relevant fields based on the actual schema.
author: lukasz94

title: fix: field validation and regressions
summary: Fixed metrics config regressions introduced by previous changes, including optional field validation issues and improper handling of the newly added dataset field. userImpact: Users can now configure metrics without encountering validation errors or regressions when using the dataset field and optional fields in the metrics configuration modal.

title: UP-3082: Implement security headers.
summary: Implemented security headers in the nginx configuration to improve the security posture of the application. This change adds HTTP security headers to responses, helping protect against common web vulnerabilities and attacks.

title: UP-3083: Hide nginx version

userImpact: Projects must migrate to ESM-only and ensure Node.js 17+ is used; CommonJS imports of uuid will no longer work.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [UP-2393] Standardize connector configuration navigation with other config pages
summary: This change standardizes the connector configuration page navigation to align with other configuration pages in the application. The cancel button redirect and breadcrumb navigation have been replaced with a consistent back button pattern, improving UI consistency across the product.
userImpact: Users now see consistent navigation behavior (back button instead of breadcrumbs) when configuring connectors, matching the pattern used elsewhere in the application.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [UP-2966] Support for Snowflake connector
summary: Integrates Snowflake connector support into the frontend application. Updates connector type handling, icon rendering, and test fixtures to properly recognize and display the Snowflake connector option.
userImpact: Users can now select and use Snowflake as a connector option in the application.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Resolve "[UP-2848] Screen for reading/updating an existing custom aggregation"
summary: Added a confirmation dialog that warns users when saving changes to custom aggregations. Also improved form UX by constraining dimension column selection to 1 input and providing predefined tag hint options to guide user input.
userImpact: Users are now warned before saving custom aggregation edits, and the form provides better guidance through constrained inputs and predefined tag suggestions.
author: michalarthurai

title: [UP-3065] feat: bump ai-sdk
summary: Updated the ai-sdk dependency to v5, which includes updates to form validation schemas and utility functions. This modernization improves compatibility with the latest ai-sdk API and features.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest ai-sdk v5 features and improvements, with potential enhancements to AI model integration and form handling.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-3059] feat: new metrics configuration UI/UX
summary: Redesigned metrics configuration UI with improved user experience through refactored components and better state management. The new architecture includes a spec form with validation, reusable form hooks, metrics removal capability, and a column list view for easier metric management. Old components have been removed and replaced with a cleaner, more maintainable component structure.
userImpact: Users can now configure metrics with a refreshed interface that includes better form validation, improved dataset selection, and the ability to remove metrics more intuitively.
author: lukasz94

title: Resolve "[UP-3068] Button add custom metric is not linked to the form"
summary: Fixed the "Add Custom Metric" button in the custom metrics table empty state to properly link to the custom metrics form. Previously, the button was not functional or linked to the correct navigation target.
userImpact: Users can now click the "Add Custom Metric" button in the empty state and successfully navigate to the custom metrics form.
author: michalarthurai

title: chore(deps): update dependency jsdom to v27
summary: Updated jsdom from v26 to v27, which raises the minimum Node.js requirement to v20 and brings improvements to CSS selector handling, event constructors, mouse event tracking, and virtual console configuration. This dependency update also includes fixes from upgraded transitive dependencies like tough-cookie and cssstyle.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Resolve "[UP-3025] Custom Aggregations: Improve UX for Save functionality"
summary: Improved the user experience for custom aggregations save functionality by refactoring the form to use react-hook-form with better validation and error handling. The zod dependency was upgraded to v4, and a new AutocompleteWithChips component was introduced to enhance comma-separated input handling.
userImpact: Users can now save custom aggregations with improved form validation, clearer error messages, and a better experience when entering comma-separated values.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3026] Custom Metrics : Changing the versions will remove the basic info"
summary: Fixed an issue where changing the version of a custom metric would unintentionally clear the basic information section (name and description). The fix prevents version changes from propagating to and overwriting the basic info fields, and ensures the modelProblemType is properly initialized from the selected version.
userImpact: Users can now safely change custom metric versions without losing or having their name and description fields overwritten.
author: michalarthurai

title: UP-815 adjustments for idp managed groups
summary: Adds support for IDP (Identity Provider) managed groups throughout the Group Management UX. The update includes a new group creation form for IDP managed groups, displays "IDP Managed" status in the groups table, shows a warning instead of member lists for IDP managed group detail pages, and prevents IDP managed groups from being selectable in user invitation and group assignment workflows.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage IDP-managed groups with appropriate restrictions, and the UI clearly distinguishes IDP-managed groups from manually managed groups with proper visual indicators and disabled selection where applicable.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency @redocly/cli to v2
summary: Updated @redocly/cli dependency from v1.10.3 to v2.8.0, a major version upgrade. The upgrade includes new OpenAPI validation rules, mTLS certificate configuration support, and various bug fixes. Build scripts were updated to align with the v2 migration documentation.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Resolve "[UP-3036] Custom Metrics : create drop down for dataset options"
summary: Implemented a dropdown selector for dataset options in custom metrics configuration, replacing the previous text input for dataset parameters. This change improves usability by constraining dataset selection to valid template variables and ensures proper parameter naming alignment with API requirements.
userImpact: Users can now select datasets from a dropdown menu when configuring custom aggregations, providing better validation and a more intuitive interface compared to free-text input.
author: michalarthurai

title: Extend Eslint config with the typescript-eslint consistent-type-imports rule
summary: Extended ESLint configuration to enforce the typescript-eslint consistent-type-imports rule across the codebase. This rule ensures that type imports are properly distinguished from value imports, improving code consistency and enabling better tree-shaking. Lint commands were also separated for more granular control over linting processes.
userImpact: Developers now have stricter type import enforcement that prevents mixing type and value imports, leading to cleaner and more maintainable TypeScript code.
author: michalarthurai

title: Fix: Re-enable amplitude session replay tracking
summary: Amplitude session replay tracking was being disabled globally across the entire stack when it was turned off on the landing page. This fix re-enables session replay tracking explicitly in the initAmplitude function, allowing it to be controlled per-domain: session replay is disabled on arthur.ai (landing page) and enabled on platform.arthur.ai, following Amplitude's recommended implementation pattern.
userImpact: Session replay tracking now works correctly on the platform, allowing the team to capture user sessions without exceeding quota limits on non-critical pages.
author: pranavarthur

title: fix: rollback ca changes
summary: This MR rolls back recent changes to the Certificate Authority (CA) configuration feature. It reverts the metrics configuration step type addition and removal of CA fetch and tabs functionality, restoring the previous behavior in the MetricsConfigurationView and related metrics pages.
userImpact: CA configuration functionality and metrics tabs are restored to their previous working state.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-2995, UP-3029: Add custom aggregation testing modal
summary: Adds a custom aggregation testing modal that integrates with the backend to allow users to test custom metric definitions before deployment. The implementation includes column compatibility validation, test result display, and internationalization support for the new testing interface.
userImpact: Users can now test custom aggregations in a dedicated modal panel to validate their definitions and view test results before saving.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: Resolve "[UP-3028] Custom Metrics (Former CA) : Update the table to show versions"
summary: Updated the Custom Aggregations (formerly Custom Metrics) table to display and manage multiple versions of each metric in a collapsible detail view. Users can now expand individual table rows to see up to 5 latest versions with an overflow indicator, improving visibility and organization of version history.
userImpact: Users can now expand custom aggregation rows in the table to view multiple versions of each metric, with the 5 latest versions displayed and an indicator for additional versions when present.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3015] Custom Aggregation Enhancements : Input CA Name should be editable upon selecting"
summary: Custom aggregation names and descriptions are now editable inline through an optional edit icon in the TextFieldEditable component. This resolves the usability issue where users could not modify aggregation metadata after initial creation.
userImpact: Users can now click an edit icon to modify custom aggregation names and descriptions directly from the aggregation detail view.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3027] Custom Aggregation : change terminology to Custom Metrics"
summary: Updated user-facing terminology throughout the application from "Custom Aggregation" to "Custom Metrics" for consistency and clarity. This change affects component labels, navigation text, and internationalization strings across the metrics configuration and workspace dashboard features.
userImpact: Users will now see "Custom Metrics" instead of "Custom Aggregation" in the UI, providing clearer terminology aligned with the product's naming conventions.
author: michalarthurai

title: Remove platform from meticulous tracking
summary: Removed platform tracking from Meticulous integration in the frontend application. This simplifies the telemetry configuration by eliminating an unnecessary platform parameter from the tracking setup.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: zach.fry

title: Resolve "[UP-3021] Workspace Dashboard: Fix the Team Member Count"
summary: Fixed incorrect team member count on the Workspace Dashboard by implementing a new GET workspace statistics endpoint. The dashboard now retrieves team member data from a dedicated API endpoint instead of computing it locally, ensuring accuracy.
userImpact: Users now see the correct team member count displayed on the Workspace Dashboard.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3020] WorkSpace Dashboard : enhancements"
summary: Simplified the WorkSpace Dashboard UI and adjusted wording for improved clarity. The component was refactored to reduce complexity while maintaining functionality, resulting in a more streamlined user interface.
userImpact: Users will see a cleaner, more intuitive WorkSpace Dashboard with improved text labels and simplified visual layout.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3019] Workspace Dashboard: Fix the Count of the Running Engine"
summary: Fixed the workspace dashboard's engine count display by adding pagination support to fetch all engines instead of just the first page, and replaced a simplistic `last_check_in_time` check with a proper `isEngineHealthy()` function that validates engines are active within the last 5 minutes. The engine health logic was extracted to a reusable utility for consistent health checking across the application.
userImpact: Users now see accurate counts of running and total engines on the workspace dashboard, with engines properly identified as healthy only if they have checked in within the last 5 minutes.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-2411] Disable users from the list that are already in the group"
summary: Removed user truncation in the `searchGroupMemberships` function that was limiting results by pagination. This ensures that the user list properly filters out users who are already members of the group, allowing the complete set of available users to be displayed for selection. Additionally, centralized the `API_FETCH_ALL_PAGE_SIZE` constant and added an `openapi:preview` script for local development.
userImpact: Users can now see the complete list of available users to add to a group, with those already in the group properly disabled and not appearing in the selection list.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-2211] Standardize how deep-link are being displayed throughout the app"
summary: Standardized deep-link behavior throughout the app by updating alert deep links to open in the current tab instead of a new tab. The HeaderItem component now displays the "new tab" icon only when links are actually configured to open in a new tab, ensuring consistent visual feedback.
userImpact: Users can now click alert links and navigate within the same tab, with the "new tab" icon appearing only when links are configured to open in a new tab.
author: michalarthurai

title: [UP-2825] Show job log for schema inspection in DatasetPreview
summary: Added the ability to display job logs for schema inspection directly in the DatasetPreview component. This enhancement improves visibility into schema inspection processes during dataset configuration, making it easier for users to debug and understand schema-related operations.
userImpact: Users can now view job logs for schema inspection within the DatasetPreview interface, providing better transparency and debugging capabilities during dataset setup.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: [UP-2996] - implementing new workspace dashboard without custom aggregations
summary: Implemented a new workspace dashboard that provides summary metrics and an improved empty state view without custom aggregations. This replaces the previous dashboard design with new WorkspaceDashboard and WorkspaceSummaryCards components that display key workspace information at a glance.
userImpact: Users can now view a cleaner, more focused workspace dashboard with summary cards showing key metrics and an improved empty state when no projects exist.
author: jakubdom

title: [UP-2847, UP-2848, UP-2849, UP-2850] workspace dashboard and custom aggregations
summary: Added custom aggregations feature to the workspace dashboard, enabling users to create, view, and manage custom aggregations. New components include CustomAggregationModal, CustomAggregationsTable, and dedicated views for creating and editing custom aggregations. The workspace dashboard and metrics configuration views have been updated to integrate this functionality.
userImpact: Users can now create, edit, and manage custom aggregations directly from the workspace dashboard, providing more flexibility in metrics configuration.
author: jakubdom

title: Traces updates 2
summary: This update enhances the traces and dashboard UI with multiple improvements: tool relevance scores are now converted for better display, a new agentic dashboard view is added, data refresh functionality allows users to fetch the newest traces, and the PII entity UX has been redesigned. Several bugs were fixed including metric chips display for zero values and availability checks for reranker/BERT-F scores.
userImpact: Users can now refresh traces to see the latest data, view tool relevance scores more clearly, access an agentic dashboard view, and see improved handling of metric display and PII entity presentation.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-2823] add better error handling in metrics calculation
summary: Improved error handling in the metrics calculation module by enhancing the error messaging passed to the snackbar notification system. The change ensures that axios errors are properly handled and displayed to users when metrics calculations fail.
userImpact: Users now receive clearer error messages when metrics calculations encounter failures, improving visibility into what went wrong.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-2863] Add optional endpoint URL input
summary: Added an optional endpoint URL input field to the Chat Playground model configuration, allowing users to specify custom API endpoints when configuring AI models. This enhancement includes updates to form validation, error messaging, and UI refinements to handle custom endpoint behavior.
userImpact: Users can now optionally provide a custom endpoint URL when configuring AI models in the Chat Playground, enabling integration with alternative or self-hosted API endpoints.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: [UP-2830] add docs link to dashboard
summary: Added a documentation link to the model dashboard component, providing users with quick access to relevant documentation. The link has been integrated into the dashboard UI with corresponding internationalization support for the link text.
userImpact: Users can now access documentation directly from the dashboard through a newly added docs link.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-2772] add 'add dataset' button with permissions check
summary: Added an 'Add Dataset' button to the datasets interface with built-in permissions checking. The button respects user access levels before allowing dataset creation. UI refinements were also applied, including removal of unnecessary margins and component restructuring for better maintainability.
userImpact: Users with appropriate permissions can now easily add datasets directly from the datasets interface using the new 'Add Dataset' button.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-2677 - PII default state
summary: Updated the UX of the PII (Personally Identifiable Information) entity configuration in the GenAI metrics UI. Changes include modifications to the PIIMetricConfig component and associated localization strings to improve the user experience.
userImpact: Users now see an improved interface for configuring PII entity settings in the GenAI metrics section.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-2795] Bug on redirection for org members
summary: Fixed a redirection issue for organization members without workspace creation permissions. New users without the ability to create workspaces are now correctly redirected to the /workspaces page, and a logout button has been added for users who have no active workspace.
userImpact: Organization members without workspace creation permissions are now properly redirected to the workspaces page instead of encountering a redirect loop.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: UP-2751 -  segmentation protections
summary: Implemented segmentation protections to prevent over-segmentation and configuration errors. Segmentation column selection is now limited to a maximum of 3 columns, and the "possible_segmentation" tag is no longer available as an option in the schema editor tag dropdown. UI improvements include refined multi-select chip styling to better support these constraints.
userImpact: Users can now select up to 3 segmentation columns with automatic enforcement, and the schema editor no longer exposes the potentially problematic possible_segmentation tag option.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-2914] Rendering of PermissionType in identity & access page
summary: Fixed incorrect i18n namespace in the organization permissions table columns configuration. This ensures that permission type labels are properly localized and rendered correctly in the identity & access page.
userImpact: Permission type labels in the organization permissions table now display with the correct translations in the identity & access page.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-2901] Add Quick Deploy Step to Onboarding & Engine Creation
summary: This PR adds a new "Quick Deploy" step to both the onboarding flow and engine creation flow, streamlining the setup process. A reusable `EngineSetupCard` component has been introduced to support this functionality and improve code maintainability across related UI sections.
userImpact: Users can now quickly deploy engines during onboarding and engine creation workflows with a dedicated deploy step.
author: lukasz94

title: Minor improvements to Agentic Trace viewer
summary: Improved the Agentic Trace viewer with several UI refinements: tool metric labels now display, relevance metric chips appear for LLM scores, and span timestamps are converted to local time for better readability. These changes enhance the trace viewing experience by making metrics more visible and timestamps more user-friendly.
userImpact: Users can now see tool metric labels, relevance metric indicators, and timestamps displayed in their local time zone when viewing agentic traces.
author: zach.fry

title: [UP-2799] Display only the supported models in chat playground
summary: The chat playground now displays only the supported models, filtering out unsupported options to reduce confusion. This includes support for the gpt5 family and automatically disables model selection when no API key is configured.
userImpact: Users see a cleaner model selection list in the chat playground containing only models they can actually use with their API configuration.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: Update dependency @vitejs/plugin-react-swc to v4
summary: Upgraded @vitejs/plugin-react-swc from v3.11.0 to v4.0.1. This update includes configuration changes for rollup options in optimize dependencies, aligning with the latest Vite plugin standards for better build compatibility.
userImpact: Build tooling is updated to use the latest Vite React SWC plugin with improved compatibility for upcoming Vite versions.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [UP-2800] Fix "last updated" timestamp
summary: Fixed timestamp formatting in the "last updated" display by creating a utility function that correctly handles timestamps with and without timezone indicators. The function uses dayjs to parse UTC timestamps (with "Z" indicator or +/- offset) directly, and applies UTC-to-local conversion for timestamps without timezone information.
userImpact: Users will now see the correct "last updated" timestamp displayed in the correct timezone based on the timestamp format provided.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: UP-2807 upsolve upgrade
summary: Bumps the Upsolve SDK dependency to version 6.13.18, updating the frontend to use the latest Upsolve library. This upgrade aligns with corresponding backend changes and includes improvements and fixes available in the newer Upsolve SDK version.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest Upsolve SDK improvements and bug fixes included in version 6.13.18.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [UP-2822 & UP-2824] UI/UX fixes
summary: UI/UX improvements across schema and dataset configuration screens. Changes include margin fixes, updated messaging around column tagging requirements, clarified terminology ("example" instead of "object"), and enhanced schema overview information to improve user understanding and visual consistency.
userImpact: Users see improved clarity in dataset schema configuration with better messaging about tagging requirements and refined terminology throughout the interface.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates for minor and patch versions across development and production packages. Updates include TypeScript ESLint tooling (8.38.0 → 8.39.0), lint-staged (16.1.2 → 16.1.4), lucide-react icons (0.535.0 → 0.536.0), and Node.js runtime (22.17.1 → 22.18.0-alpine). No breaking changes or major version upgrades included.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependencies are kept up-to-date with non-breaking changes to ensure security patches and bug fixes are applied.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [UP-2890] Add ODBC connector
summary: Added a new ODBC connector (Dataset Connector) to the platform, enabling users to connect to ODBC data sources. The connector includes UI updates for connector type selection, internationalization strings, and icon rendering support.
userImpact: Users can now configure and connect to ODBC data sources through the connector setup interface.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates all non-major dependencies across the monorepo, including cron (patch), lucide-react, Node.js, and TypeScript (minor versions). These are routine dependency updates managed by Renovate to keep the project's dependencies current with security fixes and improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [UP-2880] Update Engine installation options
summary: Updated Engine installation options to support both CPU and GPU variants for Kubernetes and AWS deployments. Added dynamic template version fetching with retry logic and fallback handling, as well as support for client secrets in the installation flow. GPU is now the default compute type for Kubernetes installations.
userImpact: Users can now choose between CPU and GPU configurations when installing the Engine on Kubernetes or AWS, with GPU as the default option, and template versions are automatically fetched and kept up-to-date.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies: @amplitude/analytics-browser (2.21.0 → 2.21.1) with cross-domain tracking support, openai (5.10.2 → 5.11.0) with API updates, and zustand (5.0.6 → 5.0.7). These patch and minor version updates bring bug fixes and new features from upstream libraries.
userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes in cross-domain tracking support and new OpenAI API features without breaking changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [UP-2810] Fix logic of deleting models
summary: Fixed an issue where deleting a model would incorrectly delete the associated dataset. The logic has been corrected to properly handle model deletion without affecting linked datasets.
userImpact: Users can now delete models without losing their datasets.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: chore(deps): update dependency dotenv-cli to v10
summary: Updated dotenv-cli from v9.0.0 to v10.0.0 across the monorepo packages. This dependency update includes a bump to dotenv-expand that resolves an upstream issue.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including @amplitude/analytics-browser, @hookform/resolvers, @types/node, @types/react, and @types/react-dom to their latest minor and patch versions. These routine dependency updates bring in bug fixes, performance improvements, and type definitions from upstream packages.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest stable updates to analytics, form handling, and TypeScript type definitions without breaking API changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [UP-2786] Bugfix for handling of optional metric config arguments
summary: Fixed handling of optional metric configuration arguments by filtering out empty or undefined values. This resolves issues where optional fields in metric aggregation configuration were not being processed correctly.
userImpact: Users can now properly configure optional metric arguments without encountering errors from empty or undefined values.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including React 19.1.1, lucide-react 0.533.0, CodeMirror SQL 6.9.1, and related packages. These updates include bug fixes for MSSQL completions and built-in variable support in CodeMirror, along with documentation improvements in lucide-react.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved SQL code completion for MSSQL dialects, better built-in variable suggestions, and latest React and icon library enhancements.

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates via Renovate Bot for non-major versions across the project. Updates include minor and patch releases for @eslint/js, @hookform/resolvers, @testing-library packages, cypress, and eslint. All changes are backward-compatible and pass visual regression testing.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2817 - remove home button from the Nav bar
summary: The home button has been removed from the navigation bar. This simplifies the navbar UI by eliminating redundant navigation, as users can access the home page through other means in the application.
userImpact: Users will no longer see the home button in the navigation bar; navigation to the home page is available through alternative methods.
author: jakubdom

title: [UP-2832] Engine onboarding fixes
summary: This MR fixes the engine onboarding flow by resolving issues with engine installation, project association, and step progression. Changes include refactoring the engine installation screen to add a dedicated project setup step, fixing prop defaults, and removing redundant logic to streamline the onboarding experience.
userImpact: Users can now successfully complete the engine onboarding process with improved step navigation and proper project-engine association without encountering installation flow errors.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: UP-2767 - nav bar fixes
summary: Fixed navigation bar layout issues including drawer collapsing behavior and navigation bar rendering. Changes were made to the layout components, workspace selector header, and project view to improve the navigation experience and fix visual alignment issues detected during testing.
userImpact: Users will now see a properly functioning navigation bar with correct drawer collapsing behavior and improved visual layout.
author: jakubdom

title: chore(deps): update dependency dotenv-cli to v9
summary: Updated dotenv-cli dependency from v8.0.0 to v9.0.0 across the project. This dependency update includes a bump to the underlying dotenv library and changes the default behavior to be quiet by default.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates multiple non-major dependencies to their latest minor and patch versions, including axios (1.10.0 → 1.11.0), cronstrue (3.1.0 → 3.2.0), dotenv, and several other packages. All changes are automated dependency updates managed by Renovate.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependencies are updated to receive bug fixes and improvements while maintaining compatibility.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Agentic models
summary: Introduces comprehensive support for agentic models throughout the application, including creation workflows, dedicated dashboards, and trace viewing capabilities. Adds new UI components for trace visualization with span details, metrics display, pagination, and raw data retrieval for agentic model monitoring.
userImpact: Users can now create agentic models, monitor their performance through dedicated dashboards with trace viewing, span inspection, and metrics analysis.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-2801] Default value for toxicity slider
summary: The toxicity slider now has a default value of 0.5 and includes default configuration for the toxicity metric. Previously, the slider lacked a sensible default, which could lead to unexpected behavior when users accessed the metrics configuration without explicitly setting a value.
userImpact: Users can now use the toxicity metric without manually setting a slider value first; it defaults to 0.5.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: UP-2768 - missing projects on the new nav bar fix
summary: Fixed an issue where projects were missing from the new navigation bar project list. The fix adjusts the workspaces content component and updates the related constants to ensure all projects display correctly in the navigation.
userImpact: Users will now see all projects displayed in the new navigation bar project list instead of some projects being missing.
author: jakubdom

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three development dependencies to their latest non-major versions: @stylistic/eslint-plugin (5.2.0 → 5.2.1), @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin (8.37.0 → 8.38.0), and @typescript-eslint/parser (8.37.0 → 8.38.0). These updates include bug fixes for eslint-stylistic's indent rule and minor improvements to TypeScript linting.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates improve development tooling and code quality checks.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2767 - nav bar UX improvements
summary: Improved user experience of the navigation bar with refinements to layout, modals, and project views. Changes include adjustments to app settings, the "add new" modal, and project page navigation to enhance usability and visual consistency.
userImpact: Users experience a more refined and intuitive navigation bar with improved layout and interaction patterns across the application.
author: jakubdom

title: [UP-2798] Add chat message validation rule skipped
summary: Added a skipped icon to the chat message validation rule display in the ValidationDetails component. The change includes UI improvements with adjusted margin and icon sizing to properly indicate when validation rules have been skipped.
userImpact: Users can now visually distinguish chat message validation rules that have been skipped through the addition of a dedicated skipped icon in the validation details view.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: UP-2788 - long workspace name fix
summary: Fixed a display error that occurred when workspace names were too long. The workspace selector header component was updated to properly handle and render long workspace names without causing layout issues.
userImpact: Users with long workspace names can now see them displayed correctly in the workspace selector without truncation or overflow errors.
author: jakubdom

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies across the project, including @alwaysmeticulous/recorder-loader, @amplitude/analytics-browser, @types/node, @vitejs/plugin-react-swc, eslint-config-prettier, and knip. These are routine dependency updates to stay current with upstream package releases and maintain compatibility.
userImpact: No direct user impact

summary: Fixed error message handling in engine and dataset deletion flows to properly display backend error messages to users. Added isAxiosError checks to safely extract and present error details from server responses, improving error visibility and debugging experience.

title: UP-2649 - Remove Data Refresh & Set Default
summary: Removed the data refresh schedule configuration step from the ML Model onboarding flow and set a sensible default refresh schedule of 1 hour frequency with 1 hour look-back window. This simplifies the onboarding process by eliminating an optional configuration step while ensuring data is kept current by default.
userImpact: Users creating new ML models no longer need to manually configure data refresh schedules; models now automatically refresh hourly with a 1-hour look-back period.

title: [UP-2648] + [UP-2651] - Add New Alert button, fix RefreshModal buttons

userImpact: Users can now quickly create new alerts from the toolbar, and the RefreshModal buttons display correctly with proper selection state handling.

userImpact: Users installing ML engines will now only see ML-specific configuration steps during onboarding when selecting the ML use case, reducing configuration overhead for other engine types.

summary: This merge request addresses multiple UI/UX issues across GenAI flow, engine management, and workspaces views. Changes include fixes for workspace double scrolling, engine association layout on extra-wide screens, and engine project management display issues, along with improvements to validation command tooltips and toxicity label messaging.
userImpact: Users will experience improved UI stability with eliminated double scrolling in workspaces, corrected engine association layout on larger screens, and clearer tooltips and labels throughout the GenAI and engine management interfaces.

summary: This merge request updates the onboarding flow with improved visual design, updated copy across all steps, and better user guidance. Changes include re-coloring the skeleton loader, adjusting component heights, adding tooltips and time estimates, and navigating to the GenAI engine creation flow for GenAI variants.
userImpact: Users now see a more polished onboarding experience with clearer instructions, visual improvements, and proper guidance for GenAI engine setup.

title: [UP-2445] Delete Model
summary: Added a dedicated deletion modal for Gen-AI models with improved UX handling. The implementation separates deletion logic for Gen-AI models from regular models, includes dataset cleanup on deletion, and ensures the modal can be exited even when background tasks are queued.
userImpact: Users can now delete Gen-AI models with a proper confirmation modal and improved user experience, with automatic cleanup of associated datasets.

title: fix(deps): update dependency openai to v5

userImpact: Applications using the OpenAI SDK now have access to new features including audio helpers, the responses API, and built-in tools support, but may require code updates due to breaking changes introduced in the major version upgrade.

title: UP-2402 fix threshold so alert rule is testable when threshold is zero
summary: Fixed a validation bug that disabled the TEST ALERT RULE button when the threshold value was set to zero. Zero is a valid threshold value but was incorrectly treated as an invalid/falsy condition, preventing users from testing alert rules with zero thresholds.
userImpact: Users can now test alert rules that have a threshold value of zero without the TEST ALERT RULE button being disabled.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency lint-staged to v16
summary: Updated lint-staged from v15 to v16, a major version bump. This includes a breaking change where processes are now spawned using nano-spawn instead of execa, and the --shell flag has been removed. Node.js scripts must now be explicitly invoked with `node`, and commands can no longer be evaluated directly via shell.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Developers may need to update their lint-staged configuration to explicitly use `node` for JavaScript tasks and create shell scripts for complex commands.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Add typesafe translation hook
summary: Introduces a new typesafe translation hook (useT) to provide compile-time type safety for translation keys across the application. The hook enables developers to access translations with full TypeScript support, preventing runtime errors from typos or missing translation keys. This change replaces unsafe string-based translation patterns with a strongly-typed alternative while maintaining backward compatibility.
userImpact: Developers can now use the typesafe useT hook to access translations with IDE autocomplete and compile-time validation, eliminating the risk of broken or missing translations in the UI.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-2084: Refresh button on list datasets page

title: fix(deps): update dependency cron to v4
summary: Updated the cron dependency from v3.5.0 to v4.0.0. This is a major version bump that introduces breaking changes: Node v16 support is dropped and the job.running property has been renamed to job.isActive. Applications using cron must be compatible with Node v17 or later and update any code referencing job.running to use job.isActive.
userImpact: Applications must update code that uses job.running to job.isActive and ensure they are running on Node.js v17 or later to remain compatible with cron v4.

title: UP-1974: Update the MUI license key
summary: Updated the MUI (Material-UI) license key in environment configuration files. The license key was updated in both the production (.env) and staging (.env.staging) environment files to ensure the application uses the current valid license.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a license key maintenance update that ensures proper MUI library licensing in deployed environments.
author: sergai

title: UP-1934: fix BE breaking changes for joined available datasets
summary: Fixed breaking changes from backend update for joined available datasets. The changes address two API modifications: `dataset_locator` is now optional on available datasets (requiring an additional null check), and the `dataset_schema` field type changed from `PutDatasetSchema` to `DatasetSchema`. These updates ensure frontend compatibility with the backend schema changes.
userImpact: Users can now successfully create new datasets without encountering errors related to optional dataset_locator fields or schema type mismatches.
author: alex380125

title: Up 1950 step 2
summary: Implements step 2 of the new model creation flow, integrating dataset configuration and connector selection. This work merges previous feature branches for new dataset views, dataset linking, and new connector flows into a cohesive wizard experience with updated UI components and routing.
userImpact: Users can now configure datasets and select connectors as part of the new model creation wizard, with improved dataset selection UI and streamlined navigation through the model setup process.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1947 - adding project empty state & add select
summary: Added a new project empty state view and artifact selection component to improve the project onboarding experience. When a project has no artifacts, users now see a dedicated empty state with options to add datasets, models, or connectors, along with SVG assets for visual representation.
userImpact: Users now see a dedicated empty state interface when viewing an empty project, with clear options to add their first artifact (dataset, model, or connector).
author: jakubdom

title: Add 1'st step
summary: Adds the first step of a new model configuration stepper component for the model creation flow. The implementation includes a new modelConfigStep view component, integration into the newModel workflow, and localized strings for the step.
userImpact: Users can now access and navigate the first step of the model configuration process in the new model creation interface.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: New Model Creation Flow
summary: Introduced a new multi-step model creation flow with dedicated UI components for dataset selection, connector configuration, metrics setup, and schedule management. The flow includes model-agnostic dataset views, problem type override capability, joined dataset support, and data refresh scheduling to streamline the model creation experience.
userImpact: Users can now create models through an organized stepper interface that guides them through dataset linking, connector setup, metrics configuration, and refresh scheduling in a single flow.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: Update dependency eslint-config-prettier to v10
summary: Updated eslint-config-prettier from v9.1.0 to v10.0.1, a major version bump that adds support for the @stylistic/eslint-plugin package. This brings the project's linting configuration in line with modern styling rule support.
userImpact: Developers can now use @stylistic/eslint-plugin rules with eslint-config-prettier without conflicts.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1408: Add job attempt to the url for a single job page
summary: This MR adds job attempt information to the URL for single job pages, improving state persistence and bookmarkability. The implementation includes refactoring the query parameters hook for better stability and removing redundant state management in the job view component.
userImpact: Users can now bookmark and share links to specific job attempts, with the attempt information preserved in the URL for better navigation and state recovery.

title: UP-1913 - Add dataset linking view
summary: Added a new dataset linking view component that allows users to link datasets to models. The view includes search functionality with debounce optimization and internationalized labels for dataset management operations.
userImpact: Users can now link datasets to models through a dedicated view with search and filtering capabilities.

title: Update all non-major dependencies

title: UP-1873 display the right dashboard and inference deep dive based on the model_problem_type

The system now supports four different dashboard variants: Shield, Binary Classification, Regression, and Custom, each with tailored visualizations and analytics for their respective problem types.

title: UP-1921: Cypress CI pipeline optimization
summary: Optimized the Cypress CI pipeline to serve the application from built assets using `vite preview` instead of a dev server. This ensures that E2E tests now run against the production-ready bundled version of the app, providing better parity with the actual production environment and improving test reliability.
userImpact: Cypress tests now validate the final production build, catching issues that dev-server execution might miss.

title: UP-1916 fixing error when skipping refresh data on adding data connector
summary: Fixed an error that occurred when skipping the refresh data step while adding a data connector. The fix wraps the `<CustomAwait>` component with `<Suspense>` to properly handle asynchronous operations during the connector setup flow.
userImpact: Users can now skip the refresh data step when adding a data connector without encountering an error.

userImpact: Users must ensure their application and translation files are compatible with i18next v24's breaking changes, including the removal of legacy JSON format support and mandatory Intl API usage.

title: UP-1805: Implement useMatchMutation hook for invalidating the cache and refactor tables to infinite loading
summary: Introduced a new `useMatchMutation` custom hook to centralize and simplify SWR cache key mutation logic, replacing the previous `findMutateKey` and `findInfiniteMutateKey` implementations. Simultaneously, refactored management tables from paginated to infinite loading to improve user experience and scalability.
userImpact: Users can now browse large management tables with infinite scrolling instead of pagination, while developers benefit from a cleaner, more maintainable mutation API for cache invalidation.

title: UP-1805: Refactor StyledDataGrid factory to support overlay adjustment logic
summary: Refactored StyledDataGrid component factory to support overlay adjustment logic for MUI DataGridPro. Introduced a custom `useDataGridVisibleRowCount` hook that manages visible row tracking and applies conditional minimum height to overlay wrappers when no rows are visible, providing a workaround for MUI v6 limitations that will be removable in MUI v7.
userImpact: DataGrid overlays now properly maintain layout dimensions in flex parent containers, preventing layout collapse when data is empty or filtered.
author: sergai

title: Update all non-major dependencies

title: NO-REF: Flat data memoization fix
summary: Fixed a memoization issue in the infinite data loading hook where the component failed to re-render when the `isValidating` flag changed during data refresh. The problem was caused by the `flatData` useMemo dependency only tracking structural changes to `swrInfinite.data`, not the validation state. Removed unnecessary memoization from SWR results to ensure proper re-renders when loading state changes.
userImpact: Loading indicators in tables now display correctly during data refresh, improving visibility of async data loading operations.

userImpact: Applications using zustand must review the v5 migration guide and update code to remove default exports and deprecated patterns; existing code may require updates to use `useShallow` to avoid infinite loop errors.
