These improvements enhance usability and correct small but impactful UX issues.
userImpact: Users can now exit the onboarding flow at will, see organization-level role bindings, experience corrected copy-to-clipboard button text, and witness real-time updates to entity names in the interface.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1572: Raw data retrieval e2e tests
summary: Added end-to-end tests for raw data retrieval functionality in the inference module. Test fixtures were added for data retrieval job states (running and completed), and the existing inferences test file was updated with new test coverage and assertions.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: sergai

title: UP-1572: Filter state and pagination fix
summary: Fixed filter state management and pagination behavior in the InferencesTable and InferenceDeepDiveTable components. These changes ensure that filter selections and pagination state persist correctly when users interact with the table controls, preventing unexpected resets or state inconsistencies.
userImpact: Users can now reliably filter and paginate through inference data without filter selections being unexpectedly cleared or pagination resetting.
author: sergai

title: UP-1572: raw data retrieval cleaning
summary: Removed the unused `useRawDataRetrieval` hook and cleaned up related code in the raw data retrieval flow. The hook was no longer being used and its removal simplifies the codebase by eliminating dead code.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is a code cleanup that removes unused internal utilities.
author: sergai

title: Fix lint
summary: Fixed a linting issue in app/src/main.tsx. This is a minor code quality improvement with no functional changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: zach.fry

title: [UP-1137] - replace graphana dashboard with upsolve
summary: Replaced the Grafana dashboard integration with Upsolve dashboard in the Model Dashboard component. The implementation now uses Upsolve's backend API endpoint to fetch authentication tokens instead of direct client-side token fetching, improving security and reducing exposure of API credentials in the frontend.
userImpact: Users can now view model dashboards using the Upsolve visualization platform instead of Grafana, with improved security through server-side token management.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1327: Mutate for infinite keys
summary: Added `findInfiniteMutateKey` utility to enable mutations of infinite query caches by matching partial argument patterns. This allows selective cache invalidation for infinite queries without needing to pass all original parameters, reducing boilerplate when triggering mutations on paginated data.
userImpact: Developers can now more easily mutate infinite query caches by matching on a subset of parameters, simplifying cache invalidation workflows for paginated endpoints.
author: sergai

title: Filter duplicate data entries and sort by time in AlertPerformanceGraph
summary: AlertPerformanceGraph now filters out duplicate data entries and ensures they are sorted by time. This prevents duplicate points from appearing on the performance graph and guarantees consistent chronological ordering of the data visualization.
userImpact: Users will see cleaner, more accurate performance graphs without duplicate data points, and metrics will be displayed in correct chronological order.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-993] - logs whitespaces
summary: Updated JobLogs component to display whitespaces in logs, improving log readability and debugging visibility. This change helps developers identify spacing-related issues in log output that may have been previously hidden.
userImpact: Users can now see whitespaces rendered in job logs, making it easier to diagnose formatting and spacing issues in logged output.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1206: Model dashboard accepts time filters from URL query string
summary: Model dashboard now accepts time filters from URL query parameters, allowing users to share or bookmark filtered views. The implementation reads initial filter values from the query string and populates the dashboard's date/time range selectors accordingly.
userImpact: Users can now pass time filter parameters via URL query string to pre-populate the model dashboard's date range, enabling shareable filtered dashboard links.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1353, UP-760] - onboarding listing datasets changes
summary: Updated the onboarding dataset listing workflow to automatically start a list datasets job on first fetch and implement SWR polling for more reliable job state tracking. Added error handling and fixed issues where the process would break at subsequent steps by ensuring jobs only run on explicit button clicks.
userImpact: Users can now reliably complete the dataset listing onboarding flow with automatic job initialization and improved state polling.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1214] [FE] As an Arthur UI user, I want to see raw data in the inference deep dive tab
summary: Implemented raw data viewing capability in the inference deep dive tab for Arthur UI users. The changes include a new data retrieval hook (`useRawDataRetrieval`), enhanced table filtering and column visibility controls, and refactored shared components (`NoRowsOverlay`, `NoRows` icon) to support the new functionality across the application.
userImpact: Users can now view raw inference data in the inference deep dive tab with support for filtering, date range selection, and column visibility controls.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1433: Deleting a project, UP-1434: Deleting a model
summary: Added the ability to delete projects and models through the UI. Users can now remove projects and individual models with a new delete confirmation dialog, with full test coverage for the deletion workflows in both project and model views.
userImpact: Users can now delete projects and models directly from the UI using the new delete functionality with confirmation dialogs.
author: sergai

title: Add meticulous and record interactions w/ scope.dev.arthur.ai
summary: Added Meticulous integration to record user interactions on the dev environment (scope.dev.arthur.ai). The recorder is conditionally initialized only when running on the specified hostname, with the recording token safely included in the build assets per Meticulous security guidelines.
userImpact: User interactions are now recorded on the dev environment, enabling better debugging and issue reproduction through Meticulous session playback.
author: zach.fry

title: NO-REF: QA fixes (UP-1389, UP-1366, UP-1371, UP-1457)
summary: Fixed multiple QA issues across management table components and job status displays. Changes include correcting failed job text color styling in JobStatus component, adjusting table column configurations across organization, project, workspace, and user role management wrappers, and removing unused code from RolesByEntity component.
userImpact: Users will see corrected visual styling for failed job indicators and improved table display consistency across organization, project, workspace, and user management interfaces.
author: sergai

title: UP-1458: Hide nav bar for org level
summary: Navigation bar is now hidden at the organization level to improve UI clarity and navigation hierarchy. The change modifies the layout components to conditionally render the navigation bar and updates tests to cover this new behavior.
userImpact: Users viewing organization-level pages no longer see the navigation bar, providing a cleaner interface experience at that level.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1496] - Chart changes
summary: Chart now starts at the earliest available timestamp from query results or selected time range, ensuring better data visualization alignment. Fixed a boundary display issue when no items cross the threshold, and updated the default time range to 2 weeks back to 4 hours ahead (rounded to hour start) for improved usability.
userImpact: Charts now display from the earliest relevant timestamp with corrected boundary rendering, and have a more intuitive default time range for alert performance visualization.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1300] [FE] Date/ time selectors behaviour not behaving as expected - QA FIX
summary: Fixed date/time selector behaviour in the model modal where the default `from` value was not correctly set to the last updated timestamp. Also corrected the publish-ui CI job trigger to properly detect changes in the packages/ui directory.
userImpact: Users will now see the correct default start date in the date/time selector modal, matching the last updated value as expected.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1445: Workpace loader for webhooks routes
summary: Implemented a workspace loader for webhook routes to ensure proper workspace context is loaded before rendering webhook-related pages. The change simplifies the routing logic in AppRoutes.tsx and adds comprehensive test coverage across multiple e2e test suites to validate workspace initialization in various scenarios.
userImpact: Users can now reliably access webhook routes with proper workspace context loaded, preventing errors from missing workspace data.
author: sergai

title: Release readiness QA tweaks
summary: Updated error messages and UI labels across group/role management, webhooks, and job logs for improved clarity during release QA. Changes include refined error handling when assigning groups or roles after creation, better webhook error messaging, improved onboarding labels, and enhanced job log display with variable breadcrumbs.
userImpact: Users see clearer, more actionable error messages when managing groups and roles, improved webhook feedback, and better navigation context in job logs.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1315] - Model specific activity logs
summary: Added activity logs as a new tab in the model view, allowing users to track model-specific job and activity history. The implementation includes updates to the model view component, jobs table integration, and localization strings.
userImpact: Users can now view activity logs directly within a model's detail page via a dedicated tab.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1407: Create workspace button
summary: Added a "Create workspace" button to the UI by updating the workspace modal and project modal components. This provides users with a more accessible entry point for creating new workspaces directly from the interface.
userImpact: Users can now create a new workspace using a dedicated button in the interface.
author: sergai

title: UP-1414: Disable workspace creating button
summary: The workspace creation button has been disabled in the product UI. This change prevents users from creating new workspaces and is reflected in the workspace selector and sub-header components.
userImpact: Users can no longer create new workspaces through the UI; the create workspace button is now disabled.
author: sergai

title: UP-1420: Data source: hide sensitve keys on confirmation screen
summary: Sensitive data source keys are now masked on the data source onboarding confirmation screen to prevent exposure of credentials during the setup flow. Changes were made to the confirmation and connect components to ensure sensitive fields remain hidden throughout the onboarding process.
userImpact: Users can now safely complete data source onboarding without sensitive credentials being visible on confirmation screens.
author: sergai

title: UP-1426: Workspace overview with dropdown menu
summary: Added a workspace overview page with dropdown menu functionality for workspace selection. The new workspacesMenu component was introduced to handle workspace switching, integrated with the drawer layout, and the shared Menu component was enhanced to support this new feature.
userImpact: Users can now view workspace overview and switch between workspaces using a dropdown menu in the navigation drawer.
author: sergai

title: fix: do not copy Dockerfiles into image so dockerfile changes do not trigger a...
summary: This MR improves Docker build efficiency and container configuration reliability by preventing Dockerfile changes from triggering expensive node rebuilds, adding nginx config validation at build time to catch configuration errors early, and enabling the same image to serve dual nginx listener ports (80 and 3023) across different deployment environments.
userImpact: Container builds are now faster when Dockerfile metadata changes, nginx configuration errors are caught immediately during build rather than at runtime, and the image can be deployed to both AWS and JD environments without modification.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: add a listener on 3023 to match aws sg configuration and allow...
summary: Added an nginx listener on port 3023 to align with AWS CloudFront security group configuration and enable health checks from the load balancer. This ensures proper traffic routing and health monitoring in the AWS environment.
userImpact: Load balancer health checks can now successfully reach the application via port 3023, enabling proper load balancing and instance health monitoring.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Release readiness 2
summary: This release readiness MR fixes three issues: infinite loading that occurred when returning to the alerts table by revalidating the first page, error handling in new user creation, and improves webhook testing error handling with better user-facing messages. The changes enhance stability and user experience during common workflows.
userImpact: Users no longer experience infinite loading when returning to the alerts table, new user creation errors are now handled correctly, and webhook testing provides clearer error messages.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: NO-REF: Add node options var bumping heap space
summary: Builds were failing with JavaScript heap out of memory errors during the unify-app build step. Added a Node.js options environment variable to the CI pipeline that increases the heap space allocation, resolving the memory allocation failures.
userImpact: Build pipeline stability is improved; the unify-app build no longer fails due to heap memory exhaustion.
author: peter496

title: Release readiness part 1
summary: This release readiness PR contains a collection of UI refinements and bug fixes across multiple components. Key changes include adding a connectorType field to dataSource info, sorting job logs, limiting character input in user and group creation forms to 100 characters, fixing button loading states, metrics modal scaling on smaller devices, and preview button color in the onboarding flow. Several refactoring efforts simplify component props and improve maintainability.
userImpact: Users experience improved form validation with 100-character limits on user/group creation fields, cleaner job logs display with sorting, fixed UI elements in onboarding and metrics modals, and better visibility of the connectorType field in data source configuration.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1379 - Activity log UX improvements
summary: Improved activity log UX by converting job type filters to multiselect with human-readable labels, reorganizing jobs table columns to hide contextually unnecessary ones, and filtering out internal Scheduled Jobs from user-facing tables. The changes streamline the activity log interface while hiding implementation details from end users.
userImpact: Users can now multi-select job types with clearer labels, see a more focused jobs table with relevant columns, and no longer see internal Scheduled Jobs in the activity log.
author: peter496

title: Up 1344 alerts updates
summary: Refactored alerts system to use infinite scroll pagination with improved performance and user experience. Changes include migrating alert tables and management views to the new useApiInfinite hook, adding data flattening and pagination support, updating the AlertDetails page to display trigger information, and enabling webhooks in the navigation with URL validation.
userImpact: Users can now browse large alert and alert rule lists more efficiently with infinite scroll pagination, see trigger points marked in alert details, and access webhook management from the navbar with improved validation.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1425] [FE] Create Shared Package for Components
summary: Created a new shared UI package (`packages/ui`) to consolidate reusable React components, utilities, and types across the monorepo. The package includes a manual publishing process with pre/post-pack hooks for local development and registry distribution, manual translation management (with English translations), and unit test capabilities. Supporting packages for TypeScript configuration and shared types were also extracted as reusable modules.
userImpact: Teams can now import and reuse common UI components, types, and TypeScript configurations across multiple applications in the monorepo, reducing duplication and improving consistency.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1300] [FE] Date/ time selectors behaviour not behaving as expected
summary: Fixed date/time selector behavior in the data refresh modal. The modal now re-renders on open to retrieve fresh date values, and form inputs are provided with default values to prevent null state issues after form submission.
userImpact: Users can now reliably use the data refresh modal with properly updated timestamps and stable input values that persist correctly after submission.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1413] [FE] Handle password policy errors on frontend
summary: Added a utility function to extract `error_description` from password policy error strings returned by the backend. This enables the frontend to properly parse and display password policy validation errors during user creation, improving error messaging clarity.
userImpact: Users now see clear, specific password policy error messages when creating accounts instead of raw error strings.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: Up 1344 alerts updates
summary: Major refactoring and enhancement of the alerts system (UP-1344) includes reorganizing alert rule components into a model-scoped structure, improving the AlertDetails page with trigger point visualization, implementing infinite scroll for the alerts table, and enhancing the alert performance graph to reflect selected time ranges. Additionally, webhook validation and code editor error handling have been improved.
userImpact: Users can now view alert details with trigger points marked, browse alerts with infinite scroll pagination, and see performance graphs that accurately reflect selected time ranges. Alert rule management is now organized within the model hierarchy.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1384: Nav bar tests
summary: Added comprehensive end-to-end tests for the navigation bar drawer component, including tests for menu selectors, submenu text, and drawer open/close behavior. This improves test coverage and reliability of the navigation UI.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1319 update time options
summary: Updated the time range shortcuts in the Model Refresh Modal to match the options specified in UP-1319. The changes include updates to the helper functions that generate time options and corresponding localization strings in the UI.
userImpact: Users now see updated time range options when selecting refresh intervals in the Model Refresh Modal.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1164 Pin actions column
summary: The actions column in the Group Table is now pinned to remain visible while scrolling horizontally. The column is non-resizable to maintain consistent layout and prevent accidental adjustments to the action buttons area.
userImpact: Users can now keep the actions column in view while scrolling through Group Table data horizontally, improving accessibility and usability when working with many columns.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1378 Errors when creating users
summary: Fixed user creation error handling to display specific error messages for 409 conflicts (when a user already exists) and 400 validation errors (such as password requirements). Previously, these backend error responses were not properly surfaced to users, resulting in unclear error messages during user creation failures.
userImpact: Users now see clear, specific error messages when user creation fails due to duplicate accounts or password validation issues, instead of generic errors.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1252 single select for tasks table
summary: Modified the tasks table to enforce single-row selection instead of multi-select. Users can now select only one row at a time in the tasks/datasets table, improving clarity and preventing accidental multiple selections.
userImpact: Users can now select only one row in the tasks/datasets table for clearer interactions.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1242 design changes
summary: Improved the alert performance chart visualization by adding a missing axis label and formatting axis ticks for better readability. These design changes enhance the clarity of the alert metrics display in the alert rules configuration interface.
userImpact: Users can now see clearer, properly labeled axes and formatted tick marks on the alert performance chart for improved understanding of alert metrics.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1127, 1129, 1130, 1336] - Webhooks table and edit states, webhook creation bugfixes
summary: Added a webhooks management table view with full edit and delete capabilities. Users can now view all webhooks in a paginated table, edit webhook names and configuration, and manage webhook lifecycle. Also fixed several bugs in the webhook creation flow including navigation issues and design inconsistencies.
userImpact: Users can now view, edit, and manage webhooks from a dedicated table view with improved creation flow and bug fixes for a more reliable webhook management experience.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: Update jitesoft/node-yarn Docker tag to v21
summary: Updated the jitesoft/node-yarn Docker base image from version 20 to version 21. This updates the Node.js runtime environment used in the Docker container to the latest major version.
userImpact: Builds now use the latest Node.js v21 runtime instead of v20, providing access to newer language features and performance improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency react-i18next to v15
summary: Updates react-i18next from v14.1.2 to v15.1.0, bringing modern JavaScript features, improved Trans component compatibility with React 19, and bug fixes for translation handling. This major version bump includes optional chaining, nullish coalescing operators, and requires i18next v19.4.5 or later.
userImpact: Users benefit from React 19 compatibility fixes and improved translation component rendering without warnings, along with enhanced translation handling when alwaysFormat is enabled.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1257 Tasks table
summary: Enhanced the tasks/available datasets table with server-side pagination, filtering by name, and sorting by name. These improvements allow users to efficiently browse and manage datasets when dealing with large collections, reducing load times and improving discoverability.
userImpact: Users can now paginate through datasets, search datasets by name, and sort results by name in the tasks table.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: NO-REF: Remove overwrite of docker credentials
summary: Removed redundant docker-start step execution that was being run twice in the CI pipeline—once in docker-build-and-publish-main and again in docker-build-and-publish-template—causing ECR credentials to be overwritten between runs.
userImpact: Docker credential handling in CI/CD pipeline now works correctly without unintended credential overwrites during the build-and-publish process.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Add docker auth to docker steps
summary: Added Docker authentication configuration to Docker steps in the CI/CD pipeline configuration. This improves security by enabling proper credential handling during Docker operations in GitLab CI workflows.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1269: Slide in drawer content
summary: Refactored drawer content to slide in from the side, extracting drawer logic from the layout component into a dedicated drawerContent component. The changes include improved overflow handling, proper mounting/unmounting behavior, and configuration support for the drawer behavior.
userImpact: Users now see a slide-in drawer for navigation and content, providing a cleaner and more modern UI interaction pattern.
author: sergai

title: Update dependency jsdom to v25
summary: Updated the jsdom devDependency from v24 to v25, a major version bump. The primary change in jsdom v25 modifies the prototype chain of EventTarget to point to Object.prototype inside jsdom instead of Node.js's Object.prototype when runScripts is set to non-default values, ensuring the prototype chain stays within the jsdom sandbox.
userImpact: Projects using jsdom with runScripts set to "dangerously" or "outside-only" may experience changes in instanceof behavior due to the isolated prototype chain.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update cypress/base Docker tag to v22
summary: Updated the cypress/base Docker image tag from v20.18.0 to v22.7.0 in the GitLab CI configuration. This brings the testing environment to a newer major version with improved compatibility and bug fixes. Meticulous testing confirmed zero visual differences across 1404 screens, validating the upgrade.
userImpact: Testing infrastructure now uses a newer version of the cypress/base Docker image, ensuring access to latest features and stability improvements in the CI/CD pipeline.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates for non-major versions across the project, including updates to Emotion styling libraries, CodeMirror SQL support, MUI components, Fontsource fonts, ESLint tooling, and other development and runtime dependencies. These updates bring security patches, bug fixes, and minor feature improvements from upstream packages.
userImpact: Users benefit from latest bug fixes and security patches in UI libraries, styling engines, and development tooling without breaking API changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1081 renovate bot changes
summary: Updated renovate bot configuration to group all minor and patch updates into single merge requests, enable auto-merging for the renovate bot user, assign FE team members for visibility, and remove rate limits to ensure all dependencies are updated promptly.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: [UP-1338] [FE] Create a seconds to proper unit parser for scheduled jobs
summary: Added a seconds-to-proper-unit parser for scheduled jobs in form editing state. The parser automatically converts raw seconds values into human-readable units when displaying scheduled job configuration. This improves the UX of the data refresh and schedule forms by showing values in appropriate time units.
userImpact: Users can now see scheduled job intervals displayed in proper time units (e.g., minutes, hours) instead of raw seconds, making form values easier to understand and validate.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1268 Multi Drop down menu
summary: Implemented multi-level dropdown menu component for the new navigation bar, supporting hierarchical menu structures with expand/collapse functionality. The new Menu component provides flexible styling and state management for nested menu items with tooltips and keyboard interaction support.
userImpact: Users can now navigate through nested dropdown menus in the updated navigation bar, allowing quick access to projects and workspace selections through an improved hierarchical menu interface.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1332] [FE] Update onboarding flow to create model before confirmation step so that we can reuse it's details for parts of the onboarding
summary: Updated the onboarding flow to create the model during the tasks step instead of the confirmation step, enabling the data refresh step to reuse model details throughout the onboarding process. This restructuring improves the flow by making model information available earlier for downstream configuration steps.
userImpact: Users can now configure data refresh schedules and access model details during the onboarding confirmation step, with the model created and available earlier in the workflow.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1140] [FE] Add job log attempt # to API call
summary: The job log page now supports multiple job attempts, with the first attempt set as the default. Users can switch between attempts via the API call to fetch logs. The implementation refactored the job log subscription hook and updated fixtures to reflect the new attempts-based data structure.
userImpact: Users can now view and switch between different execution attempts of a job in the activity log, allowing better debugging and tracking of multi-attempt jobs.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1242 Alert Rule Metric Name
summary: Added support for custom metric names when creating alert rules. Users can now specify a custom metric name during alert rule creation, and this metric name is displayed across alert rule details and alert details pages for better clarity and organization.
userImpact: Users can now create alert rules with custom metric names and view those names in alert rule and alert details pages.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1254 New Navigation bar
summary: A complete redesign of the navigation bar with a new layout, including a slide-in drawer, multi-level dropdown menus, and updated theming. This MR consolidates approved changes from three feature branches to deliver a modernized navigation experience with improved usability and visual consistency.
userImpact: Users now have access to a redesigned navigation bar with a slide-in drawer menu, multi-level dropdown menus for projects, and an updated visual theme throughout the application.
author: sergai

title: UP-1267 New theme
summary: This MR introduces a new theme and layout to support the upcoming navigation bar redesign. It replaces the previous sidebar-based navigation with a new app bar and sub-header component structure, updates the theme styling, and refactors associated UI components to work with the new layout paradigm.
userImpact: Users will see a redesigned navigation bar and updated application layout as part of the new navigation experience.
author: sergai

title: UP-1266 date format issues
summary: Fixed date formatting to use 12-hour format instead of 24-hour format across the application. Added missing translation string for "updated at" to ensure proper localization support for the date display feature.
userImpact: Dates now display in 12-hour format with proper AM/PM indicators, and the "updated at" label is correctly translated in all supported languages.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: NO-REF: granulate workspaces tests
summary: Refactored and expanded the workspaces end-to-end tests to improve test coverage and clarity. This change enhances the test suite for workspace functionality without modifying production code.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: [UP-1106, UP-1255, UP-1259, UP-1260] - Alert rules testing functionality, QA fixes
summary: This MR addresses multiple QA issues in the alert rules testing functionality. Key fixes include correcting table height and revalidation behavior, removing client-side sorting in favor of API sorting, fixing threshold value persistence as integers, adding line numbers to the code editor, and enhancing the metrics modal with an alert performance graph visualization.
userImpact: Users can now create and test alert rules with improved UI reliability, correct data ordering, accurate threshold values, and better code editing experience with line numbers and performance metrics visualization.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1119: useSWRInfinite
summary: Introduces useSWRInfinite hook implementation (useApiInfinite) for handling paginated data fetching with infinite scroll support. The hook flattens paginated responses, includes unit tests and integration into table components (PermissionsTable, AlertsTableWrapper, OrgPermissionsTableWrapper), and configures SWR defaults to reduce redundant requests.
userImpact: Users can now implement infinite scroll and paginated data loading efficiently in tables and lists using the new useApiInfinite hook.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1128] - Add a new webhook flow
summary: Introduces a new webhook creation flow with a multi-step setup process, including name and configuration steps. Users can now add webhooks with support for configurable headers (with secret field masking), test functionality, and a review step before final submission. The implementation includes reusable components for webhook headers management and secret field handling.
userImpact: Users can now create and configure webhooks through a guided multi-step interface, with secure handling of sensitive headers and the ability to test webhook configurations before saving.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: NO-REF: Route not authorized tests
summary: Added test coverage for route authorization scenarios across project, workspace, and model end-to-end tests. These tests verify that unauthorized access attempts are properly handled, improving test reliability and preventing regressions in authorization logic.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1190: Name field editable
summary: A new TextFieldEditable component has been added to enable inline editing of name fields across the application. Users can now double-click on name fields in alert rules, models, groups, and projects to edit them directly without navigating to a separate form.
userImpact: Users can now double-click on name fields to edit them inline across alert rules, models, groups, and projects.
author: sergai

title: [UP-832] [FE] Adjust all CRON Inputs once we will have ready backend implementation and connect form to BE
summary: Implemented CRON-based job scheduling UI across model and activity log pages. Added reusable wrappers and form components to manage scheduled data refresh jobs, including creation, editing, and removal of schedules. Enhanced the `findMutateKey` function to support `useAPIInfinite` cases and added comprehensive unit and e2e tests for scheduling workflows.
userImpact: Users can now create, manage, and remove scheduled data refresh jobs on both the model page and activity logs page, with full CRON expression support.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: NO-REF: Stabilizing route not authorized tests
summary: Fixed flaky route authorization tests across project, workspace, and model end-to-end test suites by stabilizing test conditions. This improves CI/CD reliability and reduces intermittent test failures.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1203 Connector step
summary: Fixed the Connect to Arthur Shield step flow to automatically advance to the next step upon successful connection, removing the need for users to manually trigger progression. Previously, the connection would succeed but remain on the same step, requiring users to click the button a second time to continue.
userImpact: Users can now seamlessly progress through the onboarding flow after successfully connecting to Arthur Shield without manual intervention.
author: sergai

title: UP-1202: Modal container align
summary: Updated modal container alignment across multiple modal components in the application. The ModalContainer component was modified to improve alignment handling, with corresponding updates to modal implementations including NewWorkspaceModal, AddRoleBindingModal, BasicDeleteModal, DeleteModal, NewProjectModal, AddGroupModal, AddUserModal, and exitModal.
userImpact: Modals throughout the application now display with improved and consistent alignment.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1109] [FE] Setup of Individual Alert Rule Page - default
summary: Updated the translation label from "Updated" to "Last Modified" on the Individual Alert Rule page for clearer user communication. This change improves the terminology used to describe when an alert rule was last modified.
userImpact: Users will now see "Last Modified" instead of "Updated" when viewing alert rule details, providing clearer language about rule modification timestamps.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1246: Fixes for JobsV1 endpoint types incompatibility
summary: Fixed JobsV1 endpoint type incompatibility following the removal of `total_records` from the get job API endpoint. Updated JobsTable and related wrapper components to handle pagination and data display without relying on the removed field, and removed the deprecated useJobs hook.
userImpact: Jobs table now displays and paginates correctly after the API endpoint changes, resolving incompatibility issues with the updated JobsV1 API.
author: sergai

title: UP-1098: Alert performance graph
summary: Refactored and optimized the alert performance graph component with improved chart configuration, filtering, and data handling. The changes include a new dedicated AlertPerformanceGraph component, enhanced CustomTooltip with better formatting options, and a new HeaderItem component for improved UI consistency. The legacy perfGraph view has been removed in favor of this more modular approach.
userImpact: Users now benefit from a more performant and refined alert performance graph with improved visual presentation and interactive features.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF - Move jobs to EKS runners
summary: Updated GitLab CI configuration to run frontend build jobs on internal EKS runners instead of shared runners. This change reduces consumption of GitLab compute minutes by migrating workloads to dedicated infrastructure while maintaining the existing pipeline structure.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: [UP-1142] Use swr instead of a local hook for fetching jobs
summary: Replaced the local hook for fetching jobs with SWR to improve reliability and eliminate bugs. This change also adds a new filter for the activity logs table, and updates the jobs display to show only non-scheduled jobs by default.
userImpact: Users now benefit from more stable job fetching behavior and can filter the activity logs table with the new filter option.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1112] [FE] Setup of Individual Alert Rule Page - delete alert rule
summary: Added the ability to delete alert rules from the Individual Alert Rule Page. Users can now remove unwanted alert rules through a delete option, with confirmation via a modal dialog and feedback through snackbar notifications.
userImpact: Users can now delete alert rules directly from the alert rule detail view with proper confirmation and feedback.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1099: Alert dimensions
summary: Added display of alert dimensions object in the alert details view. A new AlertDimensionsTableWrapper component renders dimensions in a structured table format with appropriate columns and localization support.
userImpact: Users can now view alert dimensions directly within the alert details interface, providing better visibility into alert metadata and attributes.
author: sergai

title: UP-991 Add Dataset
summary: Added the ability for users to create and add new datasets to existing connectors through a dedicated modal interface. The implementation includes automatic model creation alongside dataset addition, comprehensive E2E test coverage, and proper error handling with user feedback via snackbars.
userImpact: Users can now add new datasets to a connector and automatically create the associated model through a single modal workflow.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: Shadcn shared package
summary: Extracted shadcn UI components into a dedicated shared package with its own build configuration, Tailwind setup, and TypeScript configuration. The main app now imports from the shared package instead of maintaining local component copies, enabling reuse across the monorepo.
userImpact: UI components are now centralized in a shared package, reducing duplication and improving consistency across projects.
author: sergai

title: fix: [UP-947] - design inconsistencies
summary: Fixed design inconsistencies in the organization user management flow by correcting button label capitalization, applying the correct font family (IBM Plex Mono) to user information and group/role assignment sections, and adding a back navigation option consistent with other Identity & Access pages.
userImpact: Users can now navigate back from the new user flow and will see improved design consistency with proper typography and button labels.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1101, UP-1102] - Manage alert rules table, design tweaks, alert testing mocked
summary: Added a new alert rules management table and view with associated design tweaks to the theme and components. Implemented a metrics modal with mocked data to display alert configuration details, and added toolbar functionality for alert rule management with improved infinite scroll handling using unique row IDs.
userImpact: Users can now view and manage alert rules in a dedicated table interface with improved UI design, metrics visualization, and toolbar controls for alert rule operations.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-495 maintain state in onboarding flow
summary: Fixed onboarding flow state persistence so that when users navigate back to a previous step, their previously entered data is retained instead of being cleared. This allows users to review and edit their inputs without re-entering all information.
userImpact: Users can now navigate backward through the onboarding flow and see their previously entered data intact, enabling them to review and modify answers without losing their progress.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1195 - Fix job filter change
summary: Fixed job filter query parameter to use the correct field name `kinds` instead of `kind` in the JobsTable columns component. The backend API endpoint GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/jobs was updated to accept `kinds` as a query parameter, and this change now reflects in the frontend filter implementation.
userImpact: Users can now correctly filter jobs using the `kinds` parameter, restoring the broken job filtering functionality in the jobs table.
author: peter496

title: [UP-1102] - New alert rule flow
summary: Added new alert rule creation flow at `/models/{modelId}/alert-rules/new` with a multi-step form for configuring alert rules. The implementation includes new CodeEditor and CodeDisplay components, an information step for rule metadata, and a configuration step with SQL query validation using AST parsing.
userImpact: Users can now navigate to a dedicated flow to create new alert rules with guided steps for entering rule information and writing SQL queries.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: Downgrade swagger-typescript-api
summary: Downgraded swagger-typescript-api from version 13.0.19 to 13.0.16 to resolve a TypeError occurring during type building. Version 13.0.19 introduced a breaking issue where the build process fails with "e.commands is not iterable".
userImpact: Type building now works correctly and build failures are resolved.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1109] [FE] Setup of Individual Alert Rule Page - default
summary: Implemented the Individual Alert Rule Page with complete UI components including alert rule details display, alert rule form, and alerts history table. The page includes proper routing, mocked data, and date formatting aligned with design specifications.
userImpact: Users can now view individual alert rule details, including rule configuration, form inputs, and a historical table of alert occurrences with a default date range.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-963] [FE] Setup of Identity & Access Page : Org Access Default Page Delete - QA Fixes
summary: Fixed translation issues in the Identity & Access management page following QA testing. Updated English localization strings and corrected a component reference to ensure proper language display in the org access default page.
userImpact: Users now see correct and consistent translations in the Identity & Access management interface.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1137 - Migrate job creation endpoint to batch equivalent
summary: Migrated job creation endpoint to use the batch API equivalent. Updated all API hooks and components that create jobs to call the new batch endpoint instead of the single-job endpoint. This aligns the frontend with backend API changes.
userImpact: Job creation now uses the batch API endpoint, ensuring compatibility with the updated backend job creation system.
author: peter496

title: UP-1122 Inference Deep Dive Table
summary: Added a new "Inference Deep Dive" tab to the model page with a comprehensive inference deep dive table, including components for displaying inference results, rule details, message validation, and side panel functionality. The code was ported from the shield repository with styling adjustments to match the current design system. The tab is currently disabled and uses mocked data as the backend API is not yet ready.
userImpact: Users will have access to a new Inference Deep Dive tab on the model page that displays detailed inference analysis once the backend implementation is completed and the feature is enabled.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1012] [FE] Org Mgmt Identity & Access:  Individual Role View
summary: Fixed QA-identified issues in the Organization Management Identity & Access Individual Role View. Updates to CustomTabPanel and RolesTable components ensure proper functionality and user experience in the role management interface.
userImpact: Users can now manage individual roles without encountering the previously identified QA issues.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-955] [FE] Workspace Management :  redirect to specific workspace role
summary: Users can now click on a workspace role in user and organization management tables to navigate directly to a single role view. This change is applied consistently across all management interfaces (users, groups, and workspace management), improving navigation efficiency when reviewing role-specific details.
userImpact: Users can now click on workspace roles in management tables to view detailed role information without additional navigation steps.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1082, UP-1097: Alert details overview + beautiful charts example usage
summary: This MR integrates the Beautiful Charts library and implements an alert details overview page, allowing users to view comprehensive alert information with visual charts. A new local `shadcn` package was created to house chart components while maintaining separation from the core application codebase and eslint configuration.
userImpact: Users can now navigate to individual alert detail pages to view comprehensive alert information including charts and visual representations.
author: sergai

title: [UP-963] [FE] Setup of Identity & Access Page : Org Access Default Page Delete
summary: Added proper error handling for missing permissions when removing role bindings across organization, project, and workspace access levels. Refactored role binding utilities into reusable components and consolidated error handling into a shared utility function for consistent user feedback.
userImpact: Users now receive clear error messages when they lack permissions to modify role bindings, instead of encountering silent failures or generic errors.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1062: Using StyledGrid for groups selection
summary: Refactored the GroupsSelectionTable component to use StyledGrid for improved consistency and maintainability. This change simplifies the component's internal structure by reducing code complexity while maintaining the same user-facing functionality.
userImpact: No direct user impact; groups selection table maintains the same appearance and behavior.
author: sergai

title: UP-1009 keep users selected
summary: Added a prop to the UsersSelectionTable component to persist user selections across pagination. Previously, selections were lost when navigating between pages, making it difficult to manage multiple user selections in workflows requiring page navigation.
userImpact: Users can now maintain their selections while navigating through multiple pages of the UsersSelectionTable without losing previously selected users.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1050] - New workspace flow
summary: Introduced a new workspace creation modal that allows users to create workspaces directly from the workspace selector. The feature includes a dedicated modal component, comprehensive e2e tests, and proper i18n support with English translations. The implementation follows established patterns for data-testid handling and includes styling for disabled states pending resolution of dataplane issues.
userImpact: Users can now create new workspaces through an intuitive modal interface accessible from the workspace selector.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1086: User roles per organization
summary: This MR adds support for managing user role assignments at the organization level. Users can now assign specific roles to team members within individual organizations, with a new modal interface for adding role bindings and updated components to handle organization-scoped role management.
userImpact: Users can now assign and manage role bindings for team members within specific organizations.
author: sergai

title: UP-1131: Keep previous data for paginated table
summary: Implemented the `keepPreviousData` flag across all paginated table components to preserve previously fetched data while new pages load. This prevents data from being cleared during pagination transitions and improves the user experience by maintaining visible content while fetches are in progress.
userImpact: Users navigating between pages in tables will no longer see data disappear while the next page loads, resulting in smoother pagination interactions.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: Stabilize home tests
summary: Improved stability of home and workspace end-to-end tests by addressing flakiness issues. Changes were made to the test implementation to reduce false failures and improve test reliability in the CI/CD pipeline.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: sergai

title: UP-1071 navigation items
summary: Refactored the ListItem component and consolidated navigation item implementations by removing duplicate components. Fixed an issue where selected items in navigation were not displaying correctly. The component was moved to a shared location and standardized across the application.
userImpact: Navigation items now display selected state correctly and consistently across the application.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1012] [FE] Org Mgmt Identity & Access:  Individual Role View
summary: Implemented individual role detail view in the Organization Management module, allowing users to view role information, associated permissions, and member assignments. Added new role page with dedicated tables for displaying basic role details and role-level permissions, along with supporting API and routing infrastructure.
userImpact: Users can now navigate to and view detailed information about individual roles, including their permissions and assigned members, through a dedicated role detail page in organization management.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1092 Hide privacy toggle
summary: The privacy toggle in the "Create Project" modal has been hidden until its functionality is fully implemented. The toggle code has been commented out rather than removed to facilitate easier restoration when development resumes.
userImpact: Users will no longer see the disabled privacy toggle in the Create Project modal, reducing UI clutter until the feature is ready for use.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1094 Alert Check Job Name
summary: Added "alert_check" job name to the English translation file. This ensures the alert check job type is properly displayed and localized in the user interface.
userImpact: Alert check jobs now display with the correct localized name in the UI.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1059: Alerts history page
summary: Implemented a dedicated alerts history page with filtering and time range selection capabilities. Users can now view alerts with customizable date range filters, parse filter parameters, and leverage an infinite loading mechanism for improved browsing experience. The changes enhance the AlertsTable and AlertsTableWrapper components with new toolbar functionality and utility helpers for managing time range and filter parsing.
userImpact: Users can now access a dedicated alerts history page with time range filtering, improved filtering capabilities, and infinite scrolling to browse historical alerts more effectively.
author: sergai

title: UP-1093: Sidebar navigation
summary: Fixed sidebar navigation behavior by refactoring the sideBar component and introducing a dedicated useSideBar hook. The changes improve the layout structure and include updated e2e tests to ensure navigation works correctly across project, model, and workspace views.
userImpact: Users can now navigate properly through the sidebar without encountering previous navigation issues.
author: sergai

title: [Up-950][FE] - Create user summary
summary: Added comprehensive user creation workflow with IAM assignment capabilities, including a new summary step that displays user information and assigned roles/groups. The implementation introduces a RowSelectTable component for selection management, a RolePreviewDrawer for previewing role permissions, and complete user creation logic with snackbar feedback. This covers the core user creation flow with group and role assignment at workspace and project levels.
userImpact: Users can now create new users and assign them to groups and roles with clear visual confirmation of assignments through a summary view before submission.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1013] [FE] Org Mgmt Identity & Access:  Default Permissions tab
summary: Implemented a new Permissions tab in Organization Management IAM with infinite scroll table logic and a dedicated permissions view. The feature includes a custom PermissionsTable component with support for filtering, searching, and paginated data loading to handle large permission datasets efficiently.
userImpact: Users can now view and manage organization permissions in the Identity & Access Management section with improved performance through infinite scrolling and search capabilities.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: OpenAPI update
summary: Updated the OpenAPI specification file to reflect current API contract. Also applied corresponding updates to end-to-end test suites across authentication, IAM, projects, data sources, and jobs modules to align with the OpenAPI changes.
userImpact: API documentation is now current and accurately reflects the actual API surface available to integrators.
author: sergai

title: Rename JobStatusSnackbar to StyledSnackbar
summary: Renamed the `JobStatusSnackbar` component to `StyledSnackbar` to better reflect its general-purpose usage beyond job-specific contexts. The component and all import statements have been updated across the codebase to use the new name.
userImpact: No direct user impact—this is a code organization change that improves naming clarity for developers.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP 975 model page link
summary: Added a link from the job page back to the associated model page for jobs that are linked to a specific model. This improves navigation and user workflow by allowing users to quickly access model details from job results.
userImpact: Users can now navigate directly from a job page to its associated model page via a new link.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-760: Data sources views refactor
summary: Refactored data sources views to replace legacy hooks with the new `useAPI` hook, eliminating redundant re-renders and duplicate API requests. Components were split into smaller, more maintainable pieces with improved data fetching patterns.
userImpact: Data sources pages now load more efficiently with faster response times and smoother UI interactions, eliminating duplicate requests and unnecessary re-renders.
author: sergai

title: Replace data grid with prop version
summary: Replaced the standard `DataGrid` component with `DataGridPro` across all data table implementations in the application. This consolidation simplifies the component architecture by removing the separate `StyledDataGridPro` wrapper and standardizing on the pro version for all table displays.
userImpact: Users now benefit from DataGrid Pro features and functionality across all tables throughout the application, including alerts, groups, jobs, users, and management tables.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-990 Update Job View
summary: Adjusted layout and styling of the Job View page to prevent error messages from being cut off. Modified component widths, error log styles, and container overflow properties to improve visibility of job data and error information.
userImpact: Error messages on the Job View page are now fully visible and no longer obscured by layout issues.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-942: Keycloak initialization
summary: This MR refactors Keycloak initialization to dynamically source configuration from the backend in production builds via the BE OIDC metadata API, while preserving the existing `.env` + `.env.development` setup for local development. The implementation removes the hardcoded `.env.production` file and adds error handling for startup failures, allowing the frontend to automatically discover the correct Keycloak URL and backend endpoints based on the deployment host.
userImpact: Deployment configurations no longer require hardcoded environment variables for Keycloak and backend URLs in production; they are dynamically discovered from the running backend, simplifying multi-environment deployments.
author: sergai

title: Fix broken links in README
summary: Fixed broken links in the README file. This ensures users can navigate to referenced resources without encountering dead or invalid URLs.
userImpact: Users can now successfully follow all links in the README documentation.
author: snpranav

title: Increment arthur-engine version
summary: This PR increments the arthur-engine version to 2.1.543 and merges accumulated development changes into the main production branch. The release includes improvements to evaluation mapping, RAG navigation, row ID columns, audit logging for the GenAI engine, synthetic dataset generation monitoring, and UI enhancements for continuous evaluation tracking.
userImpact: Production deployment is triggered with the latest arthur-engine version containing bug fixes, new features for evaluation and monitoring, and improved UI capabilities.
author: arthur-github-automator-app[bot]

title: chore: doc updates
summary: Updated release documentation to specify that a new Action should be used to open the release PR. This clarification helps maintainers follow the correct procedure when preparing releases.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdbarthur

title: feat(UP-4349): Skip empty eval config step
summary: Updated the prompt experiment creation workflow to skip the evaluation configuration step when it is empty. This improves the user experience by eliminating unnecessary steps and streamlining the modal flow.
userImpact: Users can now create prompt experiments more efficiently by automatically skipping empty evaluation configuration steps.
author: TheLukaszNs-Blazity

title: UP-3149 Follow-up
summary: Closes observability gaps in the trace retention service by adding always-on logging for retention runs (including zero-deletion cases), scheduled next-run timestamps, and startup configuration confirmation. Introduces the configurable TRACE_RETENTION_INTERVAL_HOURS environment variable (default 24 hours, minimum 1 hour) to support high-volume customers with custom retention schedules, while preserving the existing default behavior for standard deployments.
userImpact: Operators can now grep logs for trace retention lifecycle events (start, completion, next-run scheduling) and customize the retention check interval via TRACE_RETENTION_INTERVAL_HOURS without code changes, while all customers benefit from improved diagnostic visibility into retention runs.
author: martin-arthur-ai

title: pass the AuditLogEnabled param to the substack
summary: Added AuditLogEnabled parameter propagation to the arthur-genai-engine stack and its substacks, enabling audit logging configuration to be passed through the CloudFormation stack hierarchy. This allows operators to enable or disable audit logging for GenAI engine deployments through the root stack parameters.
userImpact: Users can now configure audit logging for GenAI engine deployments by setting the AuditLogEnabled parameter at the root stack level.
author: notthattal

title: create a dummy commit to trigger sts dev pipeline
summary: A dummy commit was added to trigger the sts-arthur-dev deployment pipeline in GitLab. This is a procedural change with no functional impact on the codebase.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: notthattal

title: add engine audit logging to cloudwatch
summary: Added engine audit logging to CloudWatch, creating an audit trail log group in CloudWatch for the genai-engine. This capability mirrors the existing audit logging infrastructure and enables better operational visibility and compliance tracking for engine activities.
userImpact: Engine audit events are now logged to CloudWatch, providing a persistent audit trail for tracking engine operations and enabling compliance monitoring.
author: notthattal

title: Add --frozen flag on uv sync calls to install exactly what's locked i…
summary: Updated all `uv sync` calls to use the `--frozen` flag, ensuring reproducible dependency installation by strictly adhering to the lockfile without resolving or updating dependencies. This improves build consistency and prevents unexpected dependency changes across different environments and CI/CD runs.
userImpact: Builds and deployments now install the exact pinned versions from the lockfile, eliminating variability in dependency resolution across different runs and environments.
author: ntatsumi

title: add audit logging to genai engine
summary: Extends audit logging capabilities to the genai-engine by migrating the audit log middleware from the scope service. Introduces a configurable mapping of Response models to their ID extraction fields, enabling audit trail visibility across genai-engine API operations including prompts, evals, and chat completions.
userImpact: Users can now view audit logs for all genai-engine API operations, tracking who accessed or modified resources like prompts, evaluations, and chat completions for compliance and operational visibility.
author: notthattal

title: feat(UP-4232): add eval mapping reset on change
summary: Added evaluation mapping reset functionality when evaluation settings change in the prompt experiments modal. This ensures that evaluation mappings are automatically refreshed whenever users modify evaluation configurations, preventing stale mappings from being used in experiment creation.
userImpact: Users can now have confidence that evaluation mappings are automatically reset and updated when they change evaluation settings during prompt experiment creation.
author: TheLukaszNs-Blazity

title: feat(UP-4231): fix rag navigation
summary: Fixed RAG navigation routes to direct users to the unified RAG hub instead of legacy standalone routes. This update affects multiple RAG-related components including the experiment detail view, configurations page, experiments header, and main experiments page.
userImpact: Users navigating within RAG features now correctly reach the unified RAG hub instead of encountering broken or outdated route paths.
author: TheLukaszNs-Blazity

title: feat(ui): add continuous eval name to AnnotationCell schema and table
summary: Added continuous_eval_name field to the ContinuousEvalAnnotation schema to properly parse the field from API responses, and introduced a new "Continuous Eval Name" column in the annotations table dialog for continuous eval rows. The backend was already returning this field—this is a frontend-only change.
userImpact: Users can now see the continuous evaluation name displayed in the annotations table dialog for continuous eval annotations.
author: iabouhashish

title: feat(UP-3772): add row ID column
summary: Added a row ID column to the dataset table view. This provides users with a visible identifier for each row, improving data identification and reference within the UI.
userImpact: Users can now see unique row IDs displayed in the dataset table, making it easier to reference and identify specific data rows.
author: TheLukaszNs-Blazity

title: Bundle system task for monitoring Synthetic Dataset Generation
summary: Introduces a new "Synthetic Dataset Generation" system task that bundles pre-loaded prompts and monitors the SDG workflow via tracing. A new "Empty" model provider is added to allow SDG workflows to store prompts without a configured model, with clear user guidance to select an actual model from the provider interface before execution.
userImpact: Users can now monitor Synthetic Dataset Generation workflows through a dedicated system task, and SDG workflows come pre-configured with prompts that guide users to select a real model provider before running generation.
author: fryz
